units.sanskrit: अन्तःकरणमेतेषु चक्षुरािदषु वष्मर्िण । अहिमत्यिभमानेन ितष्ठत्याभासतेजसा ॥ १०३ ॥
units.transliteration: antaḥkaraṇameteṣu cakṣurādiṣu varṣmaṇi | ahamityabhimānena tiṣṭhatyābhāsatejasā || 103 ||
units.translation: “The antaḥkaraṇa has its seat in the eye and other organs of the body, identifying itself with them with the sense of ‘I’ by reﬂection of the Ātman in it.

units.sanskrit: मा भैष्ट िवद्वंस्तव नास्त्यपायः संसारिसन्धोस्तरणेऽस्त्युपायः । येनैव याता यतयोऽस्य पारं तमेव मागर्ं तव िनिदर् शािम ॥ ४३ ॥
units.transliteration: mā bhaiṣṭa vidvaṃstava nāstyapāyaḥ saṃsārasindhostaraṇe'styupāyaḥ | yenaiva yātā yatayo'sya pāraṃ tameva mārgaṃ tava nirdiśāmi || 43 ||
units.translation: “Fear not, O learned one; there is no danger for thee; there is a means of crossing this sea of relative existence; that very way by which sages have gone beyond it, I shall inculcate to thee.”

units.sanskrit: आत्मानात्मिववेकः कतर्व्यो बन्धमुक्तये िवदुषा । तेनैवानन्दी भवित स्वं िवज्ञाय सिच्चदानन्दम् ॥ १५२ ॥
units.transliteration: ātmānātmavivekaḥ kartavyo bandhamuktaye viduṣā | tenaivānandī bhavati svaṃ vijñāya saccidānandam || 152 ||
units.translation: ātmānātmavivekaḥ—discrimination of the Self from the non-Self; kartavyaḥ —should be done; bandhamuktaye—for liberation from bondage to

units.sanskrit: सम्यिग्वचारतः िसद्धा रज्जुतत्त्वावधारणा । भ्रान्तोिदतमहासपर्भयदुःखिवनािशनी ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: samyagvicārataḥ siddhā rajjutattvāvadhāraṇā | bhrāntoditamahāsarpabhayaduḥkhavināśinī || 12 ||
units.translation: The conviction of the reality of the rope, gained by adequate reasoning, ends the great fear and misery caused by the snake, worked up in the deluded mind.

units.sanskrit: शरीरपोषणाथीर् सन् य आत्मानं िददृक्षित । ग्राहं दारुिधया धृत्वा निद ततु र्ं स गच्छित ॥ ८४ ॥
units.transliteration: śarīrapoṣaṇārthī san ya ātmānaṃ didṛkṣati | grāhaṃ dārudhiyā dhṛtvā nadi tartuṃ sa gacchati || 84 ||
units.translation: “He who wishes to realize Ātman through indulgence of the body is like one who wishes to cross a river on the back of a crocodile, thinking it is a wooden log.”
units.note: Body is an instrument for realizing Paramātman — not the Self

units.sanskrit: सवर्दा स्थापनं बुद्धेः शुद्धे ब्रह्मिण सवर्दा । तत्समाधानिमत्युक्तं न तु िचत्तस्य लालनम् ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: sarvadā sthāpanaṃ buddheḥ śuddhe brahmaṇi sarvadā | tatsamādhānamityuktaṃ na tu cittasya lālanam || 26 ||
units.translation: Samādhāna or one-pointedness is constant concentration of the intellect on the ever-pure Brahman; it is not indulgence of the curious mind.

units.sanskrit: नैवात्मािप प्राणमयो वायुिवकारो गन्तागन्ता वायुवदन्तबर्िहरेषः । यस्माित्कं िचत्क्वािप न वेत्तीष्टमिनष्टं स्वं वान्यं वा िकं चन िनत्यं परतन्त्रः ॥ १६६ ॥
units.transliteration: naivātmāpi prāṇamayo vāyuvikāro gantāgantā vāyuvadantarbahireṣaḥ | yasmātkiṃcitkvāpi na vettīṣṭamaniṣṭaṃ svaṃ vānyaṃ vā kiṃcana nityaṃ paratantraḥ || 166 ||
units.translation: “This prāṇamaya-kośa can never be the Ātman. It is the effect of the wind. Like wind it goes in and comes out. As it is not aware of good or evil anywhere or at any time, either of itself or of others; and as it is dependent also, it cannot be the Ātman.“

units.sanskrit: दोषेण तीव्रो िवषयः कृ ष्णसपर्िवषादिप । िवषं िनहिन्त भोक्तारं द्रष्टारं चक्षुषाप्ययम् ॥ ७७ ॥
units.transliteration: doṣeṇa tīvro viṣayaḥ kṛṣṇasarpaviṣādapi | viṣaṃ nihanti bhoktāraṃ draṣṭāraṃ cakṣuṣāpyayam || 77 ||
units.translation: “Sense objects are even more virulent than a king cobra. Poison is fatal to one who swallows it, but the sense objects kill him who merely looks at them with his eyes.”
units.note: Sense objects more virulent than poison — even a glance is fatal

units.sanskrit: ंसः अत्रानात्मन्यहिमित मितबर्न्ध एषोऽस्य पु प्राप्तोऽज्ञानाज्जननमरणक्लेशसंपातहेतुः । येनैवायं वपुिरदमसत्सत्यिमत्यात्मबुद्ध्या पुष्यत्युक्षत्यवित िवषयैस्तन्तुिभः कोशकृ द्वत् ॥ १३७ ॥
units.transliteration: atrānātmanyahamiti matirbandha eṣo'sya puṃsaḥ prāpto'jñānājjananamaraṇakleśasaṃpātahetuḥ | yenaivāyaṃ vapuridamasatsatyamityātmabuddhyā puṣyatyukṣatyavati viṣayaistantubhiḥ kośakṛdvat || 137 ||
units.translation: “The belief that the anātman is the Ātman is man’s bondage. This belief arises from avidyā. It is the cause of griefs like birth, death etc. Thinking this unreal body to be real like the Ātman nourishes it, bathes it, protects it by sense objects, and gets bound by them, like the silkworm by its threads.“

units.sanskrit: यः पश्यित स्वयं सवर्ं यं न पश्यित कश्चन । यश्चेतयित बुद्ध्यािद न तद्यं चेतयत्ययम् ॥ १२७ ॥
units.transliteration: yaḥ paśyati svayaṃ sarvaṃ yaṃ na paśyati kaścana | yaścetayati buddhyādi na tadyaṃ cetayatyayam || 127 ||
units.translation: “That which perceives everything, but which nothing can perceive; that which illumines the buddhi etc., but cannot Itself be illumined— that is the Ātman.

units.sanskrit: यस्त्वयाद्य कृ तः प्रश्नो वरीयाञ्ज्छास्त्रिवन्मतः । सूत्रप्रायो िनगूढाथोर् ज्ञातव्यश्च मुमुक्षुिभः ॥ ६७ ॥
units.transliteration: yastvayādya kṛtaḥ praśno varīyāñjchāstravinmataḥ | sūtraprāyo nigūḍhārtho jñātavyaśca mumukṣubhiḥ || 67 ||
units.translation: “The questions you have raised today are excellent, accepted by those well-versed in the scriptures, aphoristic, full of hidden meaning and are ﬁt to be known by all seekers of liberation.”

units.sanskrit: मनो नाम महाव्याघ्रो िवषयारण्यभूिमषु । चरत्यत्र न गच्छन्तु साधवो ये मुमुक्षवः ॥ १७६ ॥
units.transliteration: mano nāma mahāvyāghro viṣayāraṇyabhūmiṣu | caratyatra na gacchantu sādhavo ye mumukṣavaḥ || 176 ||
units.translation: “A terrible tiger called the mind wanders in the vast forest of sense objects. Let the good persons who seek salvation not go there.”
units.note: Tiger called mind wanders in forest of sense-objects — seekers should not go there

units.sanskrit: शान्ता महान्तो िनवसिन्त सन्तो वसन्तवल्लोकिहतं चरन्तः । तीणार्ः स्वयं भीमभवाणर्वं जनान् अहेतुनान् यानिप तारयन्तः ॥ ३७ ॥
units.transliteration: durvārasaṃsāradavāgnitaptaṃ dodhūyamānaṃ duradṛṣṭavātaiḥ | bhītaṃ prapannaṃ paripāhi mṛtyoḥ śaraṇyamanyadyadahaṃ na jāne || 36 ||
units.translation: “Save me from death: afﬂicted as I am by the unquenchable forest-ﬁre of this world, and thrown violently by the cruel storms of misfortune; terriﬁed and seeking refuge in Thee, for I do not know of any other man with whom to seek shelter.”

units.sanskrit: अनुव्रजिच्चत्प्रितिबम्बशिक्तः िवज्ञानसंज्ञः प्रकृ तेिवर् कारः । ज्ञानिक्रयावानहिमत्यजस्रं देहेिन्द्रयािदष्विभमन्यते भृशम् ॥ १८५ ॥
units.transliteration: anuvrajaccitpratibimbaśaktiḥ vijñānasaṃjñaḥ prakṛtervikāraḥ | jñānakriyāvānahamityajasraṃ dehendriyādiṣvabhimanyate bhṛśam || 185 ||
units.translation: “Accompanied by a reﬂection of the light of consciousness, the intellectual sheath is a modiﬁcation of Primordial Matter (prakṛti). It is endowed with the functions of knowledge and action, and is always completely identiﬁed with the body, sense organs, etc.“

units.sanskrit: अत्रािभमानादहिमत्यहंकृ ितः । स्वाथार्नुसन्धानगुणेन िचत्तम् ॥ ९४ ॥
units.transliteration: atrābhimānādahamityahaṃkṛtiḥ | svārthānusandhānaguṇena cittam || 94 ||
units.translation: “The ego is so called by reason of its identiﬁcation with the body as one‘s own self, and citta from its function of constantly emembering and contemplating things of its interest.

units.sanskrit: ंस्त्वं ततो िवप्रता जन्तूनां नरजन्म दुलर्भमतः पु तस्माद्वैिदकधमर्मागर्परता िवद्वत्त्वमस्मात्परम् । आत्मानात्मिववेचनं स्वनुभवो ब्रह्मात्मना संिस्थितः मुिक्तनोर् शतजन्मकोिटसुकृ तैः पुण्यैिवर् ना लभ्यते ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: jantūnāṃ narajanma durlabhamataḥ puṃstvaṃ tato vipratā tasmādvaidikadharmamārgaparatā vidvattvamasmātparam | ātmānātmavivecanaṃ svanubhavo brahmātmanā saṃsthitiḥ muktirno śatajanmakoṭisukṛtaiḥ puṇyairvinā labhyate || 2 ||
units.translation: For those who take birth, birth as a human being is difﬁcult to get; more difﬁcult than that is birth as a male; and more than

units.sanskrit: कविलतिदननाथेर् दुिदर् ने सान्द्रमेघैः व्यथयित िहमझंझावायुरुग्रो यथैतान् । अिवरततमसात्मन्यावृते मूढबुिद्धं क्षपयित बहुदुःखैस्तीव्रिवक्षेपशिक्तः ॥ १४३ ॥
units.transliteration: bhānuprabhāsaṃjanitābhrapaṅktiḥ bhānuṃ tirodhāya vijṛmbhate yathā | ātmoditāhaṃkṛtirātmatattvaṃ tathā tirodhāya vijṛmbhate svayam || 142 ||
units.translation: “As the rows of clouds arising from the sun’s rays conceal the sun and appear themselves, so

units.sanskrit: पथ्यमौषधसेवा च िक्रयते येन रोिगणा । आरोग्यिसिद्धदृर्ष्टास्य नान्यानुिष्ठतकमर्णा ॥ ५३ ॥
units.transliteration: pathyamauṣadhasevā ca kriyate yena rogiṇā | ārogyasiddhirdṛṣṭāsya nānyānuṣṭhitakarmaṇā || 53 ||
units.translation: “The patient who faithfully follows the right diet and takes the proper medicine alone is perceived to recover from illness; no one recovers because another undergoes the treatment.”

units.sanskrit: प्रज्ञावानिप पिण्डतोऽिप चतुरोऽप्यत्यन्तसूक्ष्मात्मदृग् व्यालीढस्तमसा न वेित्त बहुधा संबोिधतोऽिप स्फु टम् । भ्रान्त्यारोिपतमेव साधु कलयत्यालम्बते तद्गुणान् हन्तासौ प्रबला दुरन्ततमसः शिक्तमर्हत्यावृितः ॥ ११४ ॥
units.transliteration: eṣāvṛtirnāma tamoguṇasya śaktirmayā vastvavabhāsate'nyathā | saiṣā nidānaṃ puruṣasya saṃsṛteḥ vikṣepaśakteḥ pravaṇasya hetuḥ || 113 ||
units.translation: “The veiling power belongs to tamas. It makes for the wrong projection of objects differently from what they are. It is the root cause of the functioning of the projecting power, and is the original cause

units.sanskrit: उपािधसंबन्धवशात्परात्मा ह्युपािधधमार्ननुभाित तद्गुणः । अयोिवकारानिवकािरविन्हवत् सदैकरूपोऽिप परः स्वभावात् ॥ १९१ ॥
units.transliteration: upādhisaṃbandhavaśātparātmā hyupādhidharmānanubhāti tadguṇaḥ | ayovikārānavikārivanhivat sadaikarūpo'pi paraḥ svabhāvāt || 191 ||
units.translation: “Even though the Supreme Self is by nature perfect and ever-unchanging, due to Its association with the

units.sanskrit: िवषयाख्यग्रहो येन सुिवरक्त्यिसना हतः । स गच्छित भवाम्भोधेः पारं प्रत्यूहविजर् तः ॥ ८० ॥
units.transliteration: viṣayākhyagraho yena suviraktyasinā hataḥ | sa gacchati bhavāmbhodheḥ pāraṃ pratyūhavarjitaḥ || 80 ||
units.translation: “He who slays the crocodile called ‘sense-objects’ with the sword of mature dispassion
units.note: Sword of mature dispassion slays the crocodile of sense-objects

units.sanskrit: परस्परांशैिमर् िलतािन भूत्वा स्थूलािन च स्थूलशरीरहेतवः । मात्रास्तदीया िवषया भविन्त शब्दादयः पञ्च सुखाय भोक्तुः ॥ ७४ ॥
units.transliteration: ahaṃmametiprathitaṃ śarīraṃ mohāspadaṃ sthūlamitīryate budhaiḥ | nabhonabhasvaddahanāmbubhūmayaḥ sūkṣmāṇi bhūtāni bhavanti tāni || 73 ||
units.translation: “This body, the seat of delusion, expressing in terms of ‘I’ and ‘mine,’ is termed by the wise as the gross body. Space, air, ﬁre, water, and earth are the subtle elements.“

units.sanskrit: न जायते नो िम्रयते न वधर्ते न क्षीयते नो िवकरोित िनत्यः । िवलीयमानेऽिप वपुष्यमुिष्मन् न लीयते कु म्भ इवाम्बरं स्वयम् ॥ १३४ ॥
units.transliteration: jñātā mano'haṃkṛtivikriyāṇāṃ dehendriyaprāṇakṛtakriyāṇām | ayo'gnivattānanuvartamāno na ceṣṭate no vikaroti kiṃcana || 133 ||
units.translation: “The Ātman is the knower of the modiﬁcations

units.sanskrit: िवरज्य िवषयव्राताद्दोषदृष्ट्या मुहुमु र्हुः । स्वलक्ष्ये िनयतावस्था मनसः शम उच्यते ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: virajya viṣayavrātāddoṣadṛṣṭyā muhurmuhuḥ | svalakṣye niyatāvasthā manasaḥ śama ucyate || 22 ||
units.translation: Resting the mind steadfastly on its Goal (Brahman) after detaching itself from manifold sense objects by continually observing their defects,

units.sanskrit: देहेिन्द्रयादावसित भ्रमोिदतां िवद्वानहं तां न जहाित यावत् । तावन्न तस्यािस्त िवमुिक्तवातार्प्य् अस्त्वेष वेदान्तनयान्तदशीर् ॥ १६२ ॥
units.transliteration: dehendriyādāvasati bhramoditāṃ vidvānahaṃ tāṃ na jahāti yāvat | tāvanna tasyāsti vimuktivārtāpy astveṣa vedāntanayāntadarśī || 162 ||
units.translation: “So long as the mere scholar who has only book learning does not give up his idea arising from the wrong impression that his body, sense organs etc., which are asat—unreal—are the Ātman, there can be no talk of his liberation, even if he is
units.note: Scholar with book-learning who misidentifies body-sheaths with Self cannot attain liberation

units.sanskrit: पािणपादािदमान्देहो नात्मा व्यङ्गेऽिप जीवनात् । तत्तच्छक्तेरनाशाच्च न िनयम्यो िनयामकः ॥ १५६ ॥
units.transliteration: pāṇipādādimāndeho nātmā vyaṅge'pi jīvanāt | tattacchakteranāśācca na niyamyo niyāmakaḥ || 156 ||
units.translation: “The body has hands, feet etc. It is not the Ātman because one is able to live even when limbs are amputated, as their powers are not lost. The ruled cannot be the ruler.”

units.sanskrit: मेधावी पुरुषो िवद्वानुहापोहिवचक्षणः । अिधकायार्त्मिवद्यायामुक्तलक्षणलिक्षतः ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: medhāvī puruṣo vidvānuhāpohavicakṣaṇaḥ | adhikāryātmavidyāyāmuktalakṣaṇalakṣitaḥ || 16 ||
units.translation: An intelligent and learned man skilled in arguing in favor of the Scriptures and refuting counter-arguments, and who has the above characteristics is a ﬁt recipient of knowledge of Ātman.

units.sanskrit: यत्सुषुप्तौ िनिवर् षय आत्मानन्दोऽनुभूयते । श्रुितः प्रत्यक्षमैितह्यमनुमानं च जाग्रित ॥ १0७ ॥
units.transliteration: yatsuṣuptau nirviṣaya ātmānando'nubhūyate | śrutiḥ pratyakṣamaitihyamanumānaṃ ca jāgrati || 107 ||
units.translation: In dreamless sleep there are no sense objects; the bliss of Ātman is experienced then. This is attested by śruti, sense perception, tradition and inference, in the waking state.

units.sanskrit: अज्ञानयोगात्परमात्मनस्तव ह्यनात्मबन्धस्तत एव संसृितः । तयोिवर् वेकोिदतबोधविन्हः अज्ञानकायर्ं प्रदहेत्समूलम् ॥ ४७ ॥
units.transliteration: ajñānayogātparamātmanastava hyanātmabandhastata eva saṃsṛtiḥ | tayorvivekoditabodhavanhiḥ ajñānakāryaṃ pradahetsamūlam || 47 ||
units.translation: “You are indeed the Supreme Self; but due to association with ignorance, you ﬁnd yourself in bondage to the not-Self, which is the sole cause of the cycle of births and deaths. All the effects of ignorance—root and branch—are burnt by the ﬁre of knowledge, which arises from discrimination between Self and the not-Self.”
units.note: You are the Supreme Self — bondage is due to association with ignorance alone

units.sanskrit: वीणाया रूपसौन्दयर्ं तन्त्रीवादनसौष्ठवम् । प्रजारञ्ज्जनमात्रं तन्न साम्राज्याय कल्पते ॥ ५७ ॥
units.transliteration: vīṇāyā rūpasaundaryaṃ tantrīvādanasauṣṭhavam | prajārañjjanamātraṃ tanna sāmrājyāya kalpate || 57 ||
units.translation: “The beautiful form of the vīnā and the proﬁciency of one playing its melodies serve but to please an audience; they do not, by themselves, ever prove sufﬁcient to confer full sovereignty.”

units.sanskrit: िमश्रस्य सत्त्वस्य भविन्त धमार्ः त्वमािनताद्या िनयमा यमाद्याः । श्रद्धा च भिक्तश्च मुमुक्षता च दैवी च सम्पित्तरसिन्नवृित्तः ॥ ११८ ॥
units.transliteration: sattvaṃ viśuddhaṃ jalavattathāpi tābhyāṃ militvā saraṇāya kalpate | yatrātmabimbaḥ pratibimbitaḥ san prakāśayatyarka ivākhilaṃ jaḍam || 117 ||
units.translation: “Though sattva is very pure like clear water, yet, in

units.sanskrit: वदन्तु शास्त्रािण यजन्तु देवान् कु वर्न्तु कमार्िण भजन्तु देवताः । आत्मैक्यबोधेन िवनािप मुिक्तः न िसध्यित ब्रह्मशतान्तरेऽिप ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: vadantu śāstrāṇi yajantu devān kurvantu karmāṇi bhajantu devatāḥ | ātmaikyabodhena vināpi muktiḥ na sidhyati brahmaśatāntare'pi || 6 ||
units.translation: Let people quote scriptures and sacriﬁce to the gods; let them perform rituals and worship the deities; but there is no Liberation without realization of one’s identity with Ātman—

units.sanskrit: अज्ञानमूलोऽयमनात्मबन्धो नैसिगर् कोऽनािदरनन्त ईिरतः । जन्माप्ययव्यािधजरािददुःख प्रवाहपातं जनयत्यमुष्य ॥ १४६ ॥
units.transliteration: ajñānamūlo'yamanātmabandho naisargiko'nādirananta īritaḥ | janmāpyayavyādhijarādiduḥkha pravāhapātaṃ janayatyamuṣya || 146 ||
units.translation: “This bondage caused by the non-Self is rooted in ignorance and is self-caused. It is described as beginningless and endless. It subjects one to an endless ﬂood of miseries: birth, death, disease and old age.“ Ātma and Anatma

units.sanskrit: वायुनानीयते मेघ: पुनस्तेनैव नीयते । मनसा कल्प्यते बन्धो मोक्षस्तेनैव कल्प्यते ॥ १७२ ॥
units.transliteration: vāyunānīyate meghaḥ punastenaiva nīyate | manasā kalpyate bandho mokṣastenaiva kalpyate || 172 ||
units.translation: “The clouds are brought together by the wind, and they are again dispersed by it. Bondage is created by the mind, and liberation is also brought about by it.”
units.note: Mind creates bondage, mind brings liberation — wind gathers and disperses clouds

units.sanskrit: स्वात्मतत्त्वानुसन्धानं भिक्तिरत्यपरे जगुः । उक्तसाधनसंपन्नस्तत्त्विजज्ञासुरात्मनः । उपसीदेद्गुरुं प्राज्ञ्यं यस्माद्बन्धिवमोक्षणम् ॥ ३२ ॥
units.transliteration: svātmatattvānusandhānaṃ bhaktirityapare jaguḥ | uktasādhanasaṃpannastattvajijñāsurātmanaḥ upasīdedguruṃ prājñyaṃ yasmādbandhavimokṣaṇam || 32 ||
units.translation: Others say that bhakti means a constant enquiry into the Truth of one‘s own Self. One who has the above- mentioned qualiﬁcations and is desirous to know the Truth of the Self should, therefore, approach an illumined Guru for redeeming himself from bondage.

units.sanskrit: माया मायाकायर्ं सवर्ं महदािददेहपयर्न्तम् । असिददमनात्मतत्त्वं िविद्ध त्वं मरुमरीिचकाकल्पम् ॥ १२३ ॥
units.transliteration: māyā māyākāryaṃ sarvaṃ mahadādidehaparyantam | asadidamanātmatattvaṃ viddhi tvaṃ marumarīcikākalpam || 123 ||
units.translation: “Know that all these—Māyā, and its effects from the mahat up to the body—are asat
units.note: Maya and its effects from mahat to body are asat — unreal

units.sanskrit: अकृ त्वा दृश्यिवलयमज्ञात्वा तत्त्वमात्मनः । ब्रह्मशब्दैः कु तो मुिक्तरुिक्तमात्रफलैनृ र्णाम् ॥ ६३ ॥
units.transliteration: akṛtvā dṛśyavilayamajñātvā tattvamātmanaḥ | brahmaśabdaiḥ kuto muktiruktimātraphalairnṛṇām || 63 ||
units.translation: “Without achieving the dissolution of the world of perceptions and without realizing the Truth of the Self, how can one achieve full liberation by a mere repetition of the word ‘Brahman’? Surely it will result only in a wasteful effort of speech.“

units.sanskrit: सवार्िप बाह्यसंसारः पुरुषस्य यदाश्रयः । िविद्ध देहिमदं स्थूलं गृहवद्गृहमेिधनः ॥ ९0 ॥
units.transliteration: sarvāpi bāhyasaṃsāraḥ puruṣasya yadāśrayaḥ | viddhi dehamidaṃ sthūlaṃ gṛhavadgṛhamedhinaḥ || 90 ||
units.translation: “Know that this gross body is that on which rests man’s entire contact with the external world, and is like the house of a materialistic householder.”

units.sanskrit: तन्मनःशोधनं कायर्ं प्रयत्नेन मुमुक्षुणा । िवशुद्धे सित चैतिस्मन्मुिक्तः करफलायते ॥ १८१ ॥
units.transliteration: tanmanaḥśodhanaṃ kāryaṃ prayatnena mumukṣuṇā | viśuddhe sati caitasminmuktiḥ karaphalāyate || 181 ||
units.translation: “Therefore, the man who earnestly desires liberation must diligently purify his mind. For one whose mind is completely puriﬁed, liberation is as easily realised as a fruit in the palm of one’s hand.”
units.note: Purified mind makes liberation as easy as picking a fruit in the palm

units.sanskrit: स्वयं पिरच्छेदमुपेत्य बुद्धेः तादात्म्यदोषेण परं मृषात्मनः । सवार्त्मकः सन्निप वीक्षते स्वयं स्वतः पृथक्त्वेन मृदो घटािनव ॥ १९० ॥
units.transliteration: svayaṃ paricchedamupetya buddheḥ tādātmyadoṣeṇa paraṃ mṛṣātmanaḥ | sarvātmakaḥ sannapi vīkṣate svayaṃ svataḥ pṛthaktvena mṛdo ghaṭāniva || 190 ||
units.translation: “This Ātman, although the Self of all, by mistaken identiﬁcation with the intellect, assumes the limitations of the intellect, considering Itself as different—like pots thinking they are different from the clay of which they are made.“

units.sanskrit: िवषमिवषयमागैर्गर्च्छतोऽनच्छबुद्धेः प्रितपदमिभयातो मृत्युरप्येष िविद्ध । िहतसुजनगुरुक्त्या गच्छतः स्वस्य युक्त्या प्रभवित फलिसिद्धः सत्यिमत्येव िविद्ध ॥ ८१ ॥
units.transliteration: viṣamaviṣayamārgairgacchato'nacchabuddheḥ pratipadamabhiyāto mṛtyurapyeṣa viddhi | hitasujanaguruktyā gacchataḥ svasya yuktyā prabhavati phalasiddhiḥ satyamityeva viddhi || 81 ||
units.translation: “Know that obstacle and death haunt at every step a man of impure mind who travels on the road of sense pleasure, beset with dangers and difﬁculties. But he who leads his life by the advice of well-meaning friends, of the guru and by his own reasoning, attains the fruition of his purpose. Know this to be the truth.“

units.sanskrit: मज्जािस्थमेदःपलरक्तचमर् त्वगाह्वयैधार्तुिभरेिभरिन्वतम् । पादोरुवक्षोभुजपृष्ठमस्तकै ः अङ्गैरुपाङ्गैरुपयुक्तमेतत् ॥ ७२ ॥
units.transliteration: majjāsthimedaḥpalaraktacarma tvagāhvayairdhātubhirebhiranvitam | pādoruvakṣobhujapṛṣṭhamastakaiḥ aṅgairupāṅgairupayuktametat || 72 ||
units.translation: “Composed of the seven ingredients: marrow, bones, fat, ﬂesh, blood, dermis and epidermis, and consisting of the following parts: legs, thighs, chest, arms, back and the head…“

units.sanskrit: ज्ञाता मनोऽहंकृ ितिविक्रयाणां देहेिन्द्रयप्राणकृ तिक्रयाणाम् । अयोऽिग्नवत्ताननुवतर्मानो न चेष्टते नो िवकरोित िकं चन ॥ १३३ ॥
units.transliteration: atraiva sattvātmani dhīguhāyāṃ avyākṛtākāśa uśatprakāśaḥ | ākāśa uccai ravivatprakāśate svatejasā viśvamidaṃ prakāśayan || 132 ||
units.translation: “In this body itself, in the secret cave of buddhi

units.sanskrit: िवषयेभ्यः परावत्यर् स्थापनं स्वस्वगोलके । उभयेषािमिन्द्रयाणां स दमः पिरकीितर् तः । बाह्यानालम्बनं वृत्तेरेषोपरितरुत्तमा ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: viṣayebhyaḥ parāvartya sthāpanaṃ svasvagolake | ubhayeṣāmindriyāṇāṃ sa damaḥ parikīrtitaḥ bāhyānālambanaṃ vṛttereṣoparatiruttamā || 23 ||
units.translation: Turning both kinds of sense-organs away from sense objects, and placing them in their respective centers of activity is called dama or self-control. The best uparati or self-withdrawal consists in the thought-waves ceasing to act from the inﬂuence of external objects.

units.sanskrit: देहात्मधीरेव नृणामसिद्धयां जन्मािददुःखप्रभवस्य बीजम् । यतस्ततस्त्वं जिह तां प्रयत्नात् त्यक्ते तु िचत्ते न पुनभर्वाशा ॥ १६४ ॥
units.transliteration: chāyāśarīre pratibimbagātre yatsvapnadehe hṛdi kalpitāṅge | yathātmabuddhistava nāsti kācij jīvaccharīre ca tathaiva māstu || 163 ||
units.translation: “Just as you do not identify ourself with your shadow of your body, in its reﬂection in a mirror, when it is seen in a dream, or when you imagine it in your mind, so too you should not identify yourself with your living body.“

units.sanskrit: देहतद्धमर्तत्कमर्तदवस्थािदसािक्षणः । सत एव स्वतःिसद्धं तद्वैलक्षण्यमात्मनः ॥ १५७ ॥
units.transliteration: dehataddharmatatkarmatadavasthādisākṣiṇaḥ | sata eva svataḥsiddhaṃ tadvailakṣaṇyamātmanaḥ || 157 ||
units.translation: “It is self-evident that the Self is the enduring Reality, that It is different from the body, its characteristics, states and activities, and that It is the Witness of them all.”

units.sanskrit: िववेिकनो िवरक्तस्य शमािदगुणशािलनः । मुमुक्षोरेव िह ब्रह्मिजज्ञासायोग्यता मता ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: vivekino viraktasya śamādiguṇaśālinaḥ | mumukṣoreva hi brahmajijñāsāyogyatā matā || 17 ||
units.translation: A man who can discriminate between the Real and the unreal, whose mind is turned away from the unreal, who possesses the six virtues of calmness etc., and who is longing for Liberation, is alone considered qualiﬁed to inquire into Brahman.

units.sanskrit: आत्माथर्त्वेन िह प्रेयािन्वषयो न स्वतः िप्रयः । स्वत एव िह सवेर्षामात्मा िप्रयतमो यतः तत आत्मा सदानन्दो नास्य दुःखं कदाचन ॥ १०६ ॥
units.transliteration: ātmārthatvena hi preyānviṣayo na svataḥ priyaḥ | svata eva hi sarveṣāmātmā priyatamo yataḥ tata ātmā sadānando nāsya duḥkhaṃ kadācana || 106 ||
units.translation: ātmārthatvena hi preyānviṣayo na svataḥ priyaḥ |

units.sanskrit: श्रद्धाभिक्तध्यानयोगाम्मुमुक्षोः मुक्तेहेर्तून्विक्त साक्षाच्छ्रु तेगीर्ः । यो वा एतेष्वेव ितष्ठत्यमुष्य मोक्षोऽिवद्याकिल्पताद्देहबन्धात् ॥ ४६ ॥
units.transliteration: śraddhābhaktidhyānayogāmmumukṣoḥ mukterhetūnvakti sākṣācchrutergīḥ | yo vā eteṣveva tiṣṭhatyamuṣya mokṣo'vidyākalpitāddehabandhāt || 46 ||
units.translation: śraddhābhaktidhyānayogāmmumukṣoḥ

units.sanskrit: न योगेन न सांख्येन कमर्णा नो न िवद्यया । ब्रह्मात्मैकत्वबोधेन मोक्षः िसध्यित नान्यथा ॥ ५६ ॥
units.transliteration: na yogena na sāṃkhyena karmaṇā no na vidyayā | brahmātmaikatvabodhena mokṣaḥ sidhyati nānyathā || 56 ||
units.translation: “Neither by yoga, nor by Sāṅkhya, nor by action, nor by learning, is liberation possible. Only by the realization of the oneness of Brahman, the Absolute, and Ātman, the Self, is liberation possible, and in no other way.”
units.note: Liberation only through realization of the oneness of Brahman and Ātman — not yoga, sankhya, action, or learning

units.sanskrit: सत्त्वं िवशुद्धं जलवत्तथािप ताभ्यां िमिलत्वा सरणाय कल्पते । यत्रात्मिबम्बः प्रितिबिम्बतः सन् प्रकाशयत्यकर् इवािखलं जडम् ॥ ११७ ॥
units.transliteration: ajñānamālasyajaḍatvanidrā pramādamūḍhatvamukhāstamoguṇāḥ | etaiḥ prayukto nahi vetti kiṃcin nidrāluvatstambhavadeva tiṣṭhati || 116 ||
units.translation: “Ignorance, apathy, sloth, sleep, negligence,

units.sanskrit: अमृतत्वस्य नाशािस्त िवत्तेनेत्येव िह श्रुितः । ब्रवीित कमर्णो मुक्तेरहेतुत्वं स्फु टं यतः ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: amṛtatvasya nāśāsti vittenetyeva hi śrutiḥ | bravīti karmaṇo mukterahetutvaṃ sphuṭaṃ yataḥ || 7 ||
units.translation: There is no hope of immortality by means of riches— such indeed is the declaration of the Vedas. Hence it is clear: works cannot be the cause of Liberation.
units.note: Immortality cannot be gained by wealth — śruti reference

units.sanskrit: छेत्तु नास्त्रैनर् शस्त्रैरिनलेन विन्हना ं न शक्यो न च कमर्कोिटिभः । िववेकिवज्ञानमहािसना िवना धातुः प्रसादेन िशतेन मञ्जुना ॥ १४७ ॥
units.transliteration: nāstrairna śastrairanilena vanhinā chettuṃ na śakyo na ca karmakoṭibhiḥ | vivekavijñānamahāsinā vinā dhātuḥ prasādena śitena mañjunā || 147 ||
units.translation: “Neither by ordinary nor divine weapons, nor by wind, nor by ﬁre, nor by tens of millions of actions, can this bondage be destroyed. Nothing except the mind, sharpened by the great sword of Knowledge —discrimination given by the Grace of the Good Lord —can one end this bondage.“

units.sanskrit: देहािदसवर्िवषये पिरकल्प्य रागं बध्नाित तेन पुरुषं पशुवद्गुणेन । वैरस्यमत्र िवषवत्सुवुधाय पश्चाद् एनं िवमोचयित तन्मन एव बन्धात् ॥ १७३ ॥
units.transliteration: dehādisarvaviṣaye parikalpya rāgaṃ badhnāti tena puruṣaṃ paśuvadguṇena | vairasyamatra viṣavatsuvudhāya paścād enaṃ vimocayati tanmana eva bandhāt || 173 ||
units.translation: “The mind produces attachment to objects like the body etc., binding a man like an animal bound with a rope. Later, the same mind creates aversion for them like poison, and liberates him from that self-same bondage.“

units.sanskrit: श्रोित्रयोऽवृिजनोऽकामहतो यो ब्रह्मिवत्तमः । ब्रह्मण्युपरतः शान्तो िनिरन्धन इवानलः । अहेतुकदयािसन्धुबर्न्धुरानमतां सताम् ॥ ३३ ॥
units.transliteration: śrotriyo'vṛjino'kāmahato yo brahmavittamaḥ | brahmaṇyuparataḥ śānto nirindhana ivānalaḥ ahetukadayāsindhurbandhurānamatāṃ satām || 33 ||
units.translation: śrotriyo'vṛjino'kāmahato yo brahmavittamaḥ |

units.sanskrit: देहेिन्द्रयप्राणमनोऽहमादयः सवेर् िवकारा िवषयाः सुखादयः । व्योमािदभूतान्यिखलं न िवश्वं अव्यक्तपयर्न्तिमदं ह्यनात्मा ॥ १२२ ॥
units.transliteration: dehendriyaprāṇamano'hamādayaḥ sarve vikārā viṣayāḥ sukhādayaḥ | vyomādibhūtānyakhilaṃ na viśvaṃ avyaktaparyantamidaṃ hyanātmā || 122 ||
units.translation: “The body, the sense organs, the breath, the mind, the sense of ‘I', all forms of functions, the sense

units.sanskrit: न गच्छित िवना पानं व्यािधरौषधशब्दतः । िवनापरोक्षानुभवं ब्रह्मशब्दैनर् मुच्यते ॥ ६२ ॥
units.transliteration: na gacchati vinā pānaṃ vyādhirauṣadhaśabdataḥ | vināparokṣānubhavaṃ brahmaśabdairna mucyate || 62 ||
units.translation: “A disease is not cured by merely repeating the name of the medicine, without taking it. Similarly, without direct realization, none can be liberated by a mere utterance of the word ‘Brahman’.”

units.sanskrit: स्थूलस्य संभवजरामरणािन धमार्ः स्थौल्यादयो बहुिवधाः िशशुताद्यवस्थाः । वणार्श्रमािदिनयमा बहुधामयाः स्युः पूजावमानबहुमानमुखा िवशेषाः ॥ ९१ ॥
units.transliteration: sthūlasya saṃbhavajarāmaraṇāni dharmāḥ sthaulyādayo bahuvidhāḥ śiśutādyavasthāḥ | varṇāśramādiniyamā bahudhāmayāḥ syuḥ pūjāvamānabahumānamukhā viśeṣāḥ || 91 ||
units.translation: “The features of the gross body are birth, old age and death. It has many kinds of states like stoutness,

units.sanskrit: अतः प्राहुमर्नोऽिवद्यां पिण्डतास्तत्त्वदिशर् नः । येनैव भ्राम्यते िवश्वं वायुनेवाभ्रमण्डलम् ॥ १८० ॥
units.transliteration: ataḥ prāhurmano'vidyāṃ paṇḍitāstattvadarśinaḥ | yenaiva bhrāmyate viśvaṃ vāyunevābhramaṇḍalam || 180 ||
units.translation: “So the wise who know the truth have declared that

units.sanskrit: उच्छ्वासिनःश्वासिवजृम्भणक्षुत् प्रस्यन्दनाद्युत्क्रमणािदकाः िक्रयाः । प्राणािदकमार्िण वदिन्त तज्ञाः प्राणस्य धमार्वशनािपपासे ॥ १०२ ॥
units.transliteration: ucchvāsaniḥśvāsavijṛmbhaṇakṣut prasyandanādyutkramaṇādikāḥ kriyāḥ | prāṇādikarmāṇi vadanti tajñāḥ prāṇasya dharmāvaśanāpipāse || 102 ||
units.translation: “Those who are knowledgeable say that actions of exhaling, inhaling, yawning, sneezing, secretion and leaving the body etc., hunger and thirst are qualities of

units.sanskrit: िवद्वान् स तस्मा उपसित्तमीयुषे मुमुक्षवे साधु यथोक्तकािरणे । प्रशान्तिचत्ताय शमािन्वताय तत्त्वोपदेशं कृ पयैव कु यार्त् ॥ ४२ ॥
units.transliteration: vidvān sa tasmā upasattimīyuṣe mumukṣave sādhu yathoktakāriṇe | praśāntacittāya śamānvitāya tattvopadeśaṃ kṛpayaiva kuryāt || 42 ||
units.translation: To him who has sought his protection, thirsting for Liberation, who duly obeys the injunctions of the Scriptures, who is of serene mind endowed with calmness, the sage proceeds to inculcate the truth out of sheer grace.

units.sanskrit: देहोऽयमन्नभवनोऽन्नमायास्तु कोशः चान्नेन जीवित िवनश्यित तिद्वहीनः । त्वक्चमर्मांसरुिधरािस्थपुरीषरािशः नायं स्वयं भिवतुमहर्ित िनत्यशुद्धः ॥ १५४ ॥
units.transliteration: muñjādiṣīkāmiva dṛśyavargāt pratyañcamātmānamasaṅgamakriyam | vivicya tatra pravilāpya sarvaṃ tadātmanā tiṣṭhati yaḥ sa muktaḥ || 153 ||
units.translation: “He is liberated who separates the Ātman, which is unattached and inactive, from every object of sense

units.sanskrit: अथर्स्य िनश्चयो दृष्टो िवचारेण िहतोिक्ततः । न स्नानेन न दानेन प्राणायमशतेन वा ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: arthasya niścayo dṛṣṭo vicāreṇa hitoktitaḥ | na snānena na dānena prāṇāyamaśatena vā || 13 ||
units.translation: The conviction of the Truth proceeds from investigation of the salutary counsel of the wise, not by bathing in sacred

units.sanskrit: मोह एव महामृत्युमु र्मुक्षोवर्पुरािदषु । मोहो िविनिजर् तो येन स मुिक्तपदमहर्ित ॥ ८५ ॥
units.transliteration: moha eva mahāmṛtyurmumukṣorvapurādiṣu | moho vinirjito yena sa muktipadamarhati || 85 ||
units.translation: “For the seeker of liberation, attachment to the body is a tragic death. He alone who is free from this attachment deserves liberation.”

units.sanskrit: अहंकारािददेहान्तान् बन्धानज्ञानकिल्पतान् । स्वस्वरूपावबोधेन मोक्तुिमच्छा मुमुक्षुता ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: ahaṃkārādidehāntān bandhānajñānakalpitān | svasvarūpāvabodhena moktumicchā mumukṣutā || 27 ||
units.translation: Mumukṣutvām or yearning for liberation is the desire to free oneself from all bondage superimposed by ignorance—from egoism to the body— by realizing one’s true nature.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञानेिन्द्रयािण च मनश्च मनोमायाः स्यात् कोशो ममाहिमित वस्तुिवकल्पहेतुः । संज्ञािदभेदकलनाकिलतो बलीयांस् तत्पूवर्कोशमिभपूयर् िवजृम्भते यः ॥ १६७ ॥
units.transliteration: jñānendriyāṇi ca manaśca manomāyāḥ syāt kośo mamāhamiti vastuvikalpahetuḥ | saṃjñādibhedakalanākalito balīyāṃs tatpūrvakośamabhipūrya vijṛmbhate yaḥ || 167 ||
units.translation: “The organs of knowledge and the mind form the
units.note: Manomaya-kośa / vijñānamaya-kośa — cognitive sheaths are not the Self

units.sanskrit: शब्दािदिभः पञ्चिभरेव पञ्च पञ्चत्वमापुः स्वगुणेन बद्धाः । कु रङ्गमातङ्गपतङ्गमीन भृङ्गा नरः पञ्चिभरिञ्चतः िकम् ॥ ७६ ॥
units.transliteration: śabdādibhiḥ pañcabhireva pañca pañcatvamāpuḥ svaguṇena baddhāḥ | kuraṅgamātaṅgapataṅgamīna bhṛṅgā naraḥ pañcabhirañcitaḥ kim || 76 ||
units.translation: śabdādibhiḥ pañcabhireva pañca

units.sanskrit: ं त्वं स्वमात्मानमात्मन्य् िनयिमतमनसामु अयमहिमित साक्षािद्विद्ध बुिद्धप्रसादात् । ं जिनमरणतरङ्गापारसंसारिसन्धु प्रतर भव कृ ताथोर् ब्रह्मरूपेण संस्थः ॥ १३६ ॥
units.transliteration: niyamitamanasāmuṃ tvaṃ svamātmānamātmany ayamahamiti sākṣādviddhi buddhiprasādāt | janimaraṇataraṅgāpārasaṃsārasindhuṃ pratara bhava kṛtārtho brahmarūpeṇa saṃsthaḥ || 136 ||
units.translation: “Directly realise Ātman as your ‘I’ by means of a controlled mind and puriﬁed intellect. Cross this shoeless ocean of saṃsāra, with its waves of birth and death, by identifying yourself with the Self. Thus established as Brahman, be a person of fulﬁlled purpose.“

units.sanskrit: यो िवजानाित सकलं जाग्रत्स्वप्नसुषुिप्तषु । बुिद्धतद्वृित्तसद्भावमभावमहिमत्ययम् ॥ १२६ ॥
units.transliteration: yo vijānāti sakalaṃ jāgratsvapnasuṣuptiṣu | buddhitadvṛttisadbhāvamabhāvamahamityayam || 126 ||
units.translation: “That which as ‘I’ knows everything in the waking, dream and dreamless sleep states, the presence of the buddhi and its actions, and their absence—that is the Ātman.

units.sanskrit: तस्मात्सवर्प्रयत्नेन भवबन्धिवमुक्तये । स्वैरेव यत्नः कतर्व्यो रोगादािवव पिण्डतैः ॥ ६६ ॥
units.transliteration: tasmātsarvaprayatnena bhavabandhavimuktaye | svaireva yatnaḥ kartavyo rogādāviva paṇḍitaiḥ || 66 ||
units.translation: “Therefore the wise seeker should, as in the case of illness etc., strive by every means at his disposal to be free from the bondage of repeated births and deaths.”

units.sanskrit: असङ्गिचद्रूपममु ं िवमोह्य देहेिन्द्रयप्राणगुणैिनर् बद्ध्य । अहंममेित भ्रमायात्यजस्रं मनः स्वकृ त्येषु फलोपभुिक्तषु ॥ १७८ ॥
units.transliteration: manaḥ prasūte viṣayānaśeṣān sthūlātmanā sūkṣmatayā ca bhoktuḥ | śarīravarṇāśramajātibhedān guṇakriyāhetuphalāni nityam || 177 ||
units.translation: “The mind always produces the experiences of all sense-objects in the gross and subtle form for the

units.sanskrit: शान्ता महान्तो िनवसिन्त सन्तो वसन्तवल्लोकिहतं चरन्तः । तीणार्ः स्वयं भीमभवाणर्वं जनान् अहेतुनान् यानिप तारयन्तः ॥ ३७ ॥
units.transliteration: śāntā mahānto nivasanti santo vasantavallokahitaṃ carantaḥ | tīrṇāḥ svayaṃ bhīmabhavārṇavaṃ janān ahetunān yānapi tārayantaḥ || 37 ||
units.translation: śāntā mahānto nivasanti santo

units.sanskrit: बुिद्धबु र्द्धीिन्द्रयैः साधर्ं सवृित्तः कतृ र्लक्षणः । िवज्ञानमायाकोशः स्यात्पु ंसः संसारकारणम् ॥ १८४ ॥
units.transliteration: buddhirbuddhīndriyaiḥ sārdhaṃ savṛttiḥ kartṛlakṣaṇaḥ | vijñānaMāyākośaḥ syātpuṃsaḥ saṃsārakāraṇam || 184 ||
units.translation: “Buddhi with its organs of knowledge and actions having the characteristics of an agent, is called the

units.sanskrit: प्राणापानव्यानोदानसमाना भवत्यसौ प्राणः । स्वयमेव वृित्तभेदािद्वकृ ितभेदात्सुवणर्सिललािदवत् ॥ ९५ ॥
units.transliteration: prāṇāpānavyānodānasamānā bhavatyasau prāṇaḥ | svayameva vṛttibhedādvikṛtibhedātsuvarṇasalilādivat || 95 ||
units.translation: “The breath by itself becomes

units.sanskrit: दुलर्भं त्रयमेवैतद्देवानुग्रहहेतुकम् । मनुष्यत्वं मुमुक्षुत्वं महापुरुषसंश्रयः ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: durlabhaṃ trayamevaitaddevānugrahahetukam | manuṣyatvaṃ mumukṣutvaṃ mahāpuruṣasaṃśrayaḥ || 3 ||
units.translation: There are three things which are rare indeed and are due to the grace of God: namely, a human birth, the longing for Liberation, and the protecting care of a perfected sage.
units.note: Three rare gifts — human birth, longing for liberation, perfected teacher

units.sanskrit: कविलतिदननाथेर् दुिदर् ने सान्द्रमेघैः व्यथयित िहमझंझावायुरुग्रो यथैतान् । अिवरततमसात्मन्यावृते मूढबुिद्धं क्षपयित बहुदुःखैस्तीव्रिवक्षेपशिक्तः ॥ १४३ ॥
units.transliteration: kavalitadinanārthe durdine sāndrameghaiḥ vyathayati himajhaṃjhāvāyurugro yathaitān | aviratatamasātmanyāvṛte mūḍhabuddhiṃ kṣapayati bahuduḥkhaistīvravikṣepaśaktiḥ || 143 ||
units.translation: “Just as on a rainy day when the sun is completely hidden by dense clouds, cold blasts afﬂict a shivering traveler, so too the man whose Ātman is concealed from him is subject to intense

units.sanskrit: मस्तकन्यस्तभारादेदु र्ःखमन्यैिनर् वायर्ते । क्षुधािदकृ तदुःखं तु िवना स्वेन न के निचत् ॥ ५२ ॥
units.transliteration: mastakanyastabhārāderduḥkhamanyairnivāryate | kṣudhādikṛtaduḥkhaṃ tu vinā svena na kenacit || 52 ||
units.translation: “Exhaustion and fatigue caused by carrying a load on the head, etc. can be relieved by others. But none save one‘s own self can end the pangs caused by hunger, etc.”

units.sanskrit: कामः क्रोधो लोभदम्भाद्यसूया अहंकारेष्यार्मत्सराद्यास्तु घोराः । धमार् एते राजसाः पुम्प्रवृित्तः यस्मादेषा तद्रजो बन्धहेतुः ॥ ११२ ॥
units.transliteration: kāmaḥ krodho lobhadambhādyasūyā ahaṃkārerṣyāmatsarādyāstu ghorāḥ | dharmā ete rājasāḥ pumpravṛttiḥ yasmādeṣā tadrajo bandhahetuḥ || 112 ||
units.translation: “Desire; anger, avarice, pride, jealousy, egoism, envy, miserliness etc., the terrible characteristics of rajas, are inducements to actions of men. This rajas is the cause of bondage.“

units.sanskrit: सन्नाप्यसन्नाप्युभयाित्मका नो िभन्नाप्यिभन्नाप्युभयाित्मका नो । साङ्गाप्यनङ्गा ह्युभयाित्मका नो महाद्भुतािनवर्चनीयरूपा ॥ १०९ ॥
units.transliteration: sannāpyasannāpyubhayātmikā no bhinnāpyabhinnāpyubhayātmikā no | sāṅgāpyanaṅgā hyubhayātmikā no mahādbhutānirvacanīyarūpā || 109 ||
units.translation: sat-na—neither existent; api-asat-na—nor nonexistent;

units.sanskrit: को नाम बन्धः कथमेष आगतः कथं प्रितष्ठास्य कथं िवमोक्षः । कोऽसावनात्मा परमः क आत्मा तयोिवर् वेकः कथमेतदुच्यताम् ॥ ४९ ॥
units.transliteration: ko nāma bandhaḥ kathameṣa āgataḥ kathaṃ pratiṣṭhāsya kathaṃ vimokṣaḥ | ko'sāvanātmā paramaḥ ka ātmā tayorvivekaḥ kathametaducyatām || 49 ||
units.translation: “What, indeed, is bondage? How has it come? How does it continue to exist? How can one get out of it completely? What is this non-Self? Who is the Supreme Self? What is the process of discrimination between them? Please explain all these to me.“

units.sanskrit: शल्यरािशमार्ंसिलप्तो मलपूणोर्ऽितकश्मलः । कथं भवेदयं वेत्ता स्वयमेतिद्वलक्षणः ॥ १५८ ॥
units.transliteration: śalyarāśirmāṃsalipto malapūrṇo'tikaśmalaḥ | kathaṃ bhavedayaṃ vettā svayametadvilakṣaṇaḥ || 158 ||
units.translation: śalyarāśiḥ—package of bones; māṃsaliptaḥ—covered with ﬂesh;

units.sanskrit: साधनान्यत्र चत्वािर किथतािन मनीिषिभः । येषु सत्स्वेव सिन्नष्ठा यदभावे न िसध्यित ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: sādhanānyatra catvāri kathitāni manīṣibhiḥ | yeṣu satsveva sanniṣṭhā yadabhāve na sidhyati || 18 ||
units.translation: Great sages have spoken of four qualiﬁcations— which, when present, one succeeds in realization of Brahman, and in the absence of which the goal is not attained.

units.sanskrit: अतो िवमुक्त्यै प्रयतेित्वद्वान् संन्यस्तबाह्याथर्सुखस्पृहः सन् । सन्तं महान्तं समुपेत्य देिशकं तेनोपिदष्टाथर्समािहतात्मा ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: ato vimuktyai prayatetvidvān saṃnyastabāhyārthasukhaspṛhaḥ san | santaṃ mahāntaṃ samupetya deśikaṃ tenopadiṣṭārthasamāhitātmā || 8 ||
units.translation: Therefore the learned man should strive for Liberation, renouncing desire for pleasures from external objects, duly approaching a good and generous preceptor, and living attuned to the signiﬁcance of his words.
units.note: The learned man should approach a great guru and abide by his words

units.sanskrit: श्रुितप्रमाणैकमतेः स्वधमर् िनष्ठा तयैवात्मिवशुिद्धरस्य । िवशुद्धबुद्धेः परमात्मवेदनं तेनैव संसारसमूलनाशः ॥ १४८ ॥
units.transliteration: śrutipramāṇaikamateḥ svadharma niṣṭhā tayaivātmaviśuddhirasya | viśuddhabuddheḥ paramātmavedanaṃ tenaiva saṃsārasamūlanāśaḥ || 148 ||
units.translation: śrutipramāṇaikamateḥ svadharma

units.sanskrit: अिवज्ञाते परे तत्त्वे शास्त्राधीितस्तु िनष्फला । िवज्ञातेऽिप परे तत्त्वे शास्त्राधीितस्तु िनष्फला ॥ ५९ ॥
units.transliteration: avijñāte pare tattve śāstrādhītistu niṣphalā | vijñāte'pi pare tattve śāstrādhītistu niṣphalā || 59 ||
units.translation: “Without knowing the Supreme Reality, the study of

units.sanskrit: िवशुद्धसत्त्वस्य गुणाः प्रसादः स्वात्मानुभूितः परमा प्रशािन्तः । तृिप्तः प्रहषर्ः परमात्मिनष्ठा यया सदानन्दरसं समृच्छित ॥ ११९ ॥
units.transliteration: viśuddhasattvasya guṇāḥ prasādaḥ svātmānubhūtiḥ paramā praśāntiḥ | tṛptiḥ praharṣaḥ paramātmaniṣṭhā yayā sadānandarasaṃ samṛcchati || 119 ||
units.translation: “The characteristics of pure sattva are limpidity of mind, realisation of one’s Self, supreme peace, contentment,

units.sanskrit: आपातवैराग्यवतो मुमुक्षून् भवािब्धपारं प्रितयातुमुद्यतान् । आशाग्रहो मज्जयतेऽन्तराले िनगृह्य कण्ठे िविनवत्यर् वेगात् ॥ ७९ ॥
units.transliteration: āpātavairāgyavato mumukṣūn bhavābdhipāraṃ pratiyātumudyatān | āśāgraho majjayate'ntarāle nigṛhya kaṇṭhe vinivartya vegāt || 79 ||
units.translation: āpātavairāgyavato mumukṣūn

units.sanskrit: ितरोभूते स्वात्मन्यमलतरतेजोवित पुमान् अनात्मानं मोहादहिमित शरीरं कलयित । ततः कामक्रोधप्रभृितिभरमु ं बन्धनगुणैः परं िवक्षेपाख्या रजस उरुशिक्तव्यर्थयित ॥ १४० ॥
units.transliteration: akhaṇḍanityādvayabodhaśaktyā sphurantamātmānamanantavaibhavam | samāvṛṇotyāvṛtiśaktireṣā tamomayī rāhurivārkabimbam || 139 ||
units.translation: “Although eternal Ātman of inﬁnite glory, nondual, one without a second, manifests Itself by the power

units.sanskrit: मन्दमध्यमरूपािप वैराग्येण शमािदना । प्रसादेन गुरोः सेयं प्रवृद्धा सूयते फलम् ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: mandamadhyamarūpāpi vairāgyeṇa śamādinā | prasādena guroḥ seyaṃ pravṛddhā sūyate phalam || 28 ||
units.translation: Even though torpid or mediocre,

units.sanskrit: न ह्यस्त्यिवद्या मनसोऽितिरक्ता मनो ह्यिवद्या भवबन्धहेतुः । तिस्मिन्वनष्टे सकलं िवनष्टं िवजृिम्भतेऽिस्मन्सकलं िवजृम्भते ॥ १६९ ॥
units.transliteration: pañcendriyaiḥ pañcabhireva hotṛbhiḥ pracīyamāno viṣayājyadhārayā | jājvalyamāno bahuvāsanendhanaiḥ manomayāgnirdahati prapañcam || 168 ||
units.translation: “The manomaya-kośa is the sacriﬁcial ﬁre. The ﬁve sense organs are the priests who continuously pour oblations of sense objects into the ﬁre. The many

units.sanskrit: असङ्गिचद्रूपममु ं िवमोह्य देहेिन्द्रयप्राणगुणैिनर् बद्ध्य । अहंममेित भ्रमायात्यजस्रं मनः स्वकृ त्येषु फलोपभुिक्तषु ॥ १७८ ॥
units.transliteration: asaṅgacidrūpamamuṃ vimohya dehendriyaprāṇaguṇairnibaddhya | ahaṃmameti bhramāyātyajasraṃ manaḥ svakṛtyeṣu phalopabhuktiṣu || 178 ||
units.translation: “The Ātman is pure intelligence unattached to anything. Binding him by the cords of the body, sense organs and vital airs, disabling him from understanding his real

units.sanskrit: ब्रह्मानन्दरसानुभूितकिलतैः पूतैर्ः सुशीतैयु र्तैः युष्मद्वाक्कलशोिज्झतैः श्रुितसुखैवार्क्यामृतैः सेचय । संतप्तं भवतापदावदहनज्वालािभरेनं प्रभो धन्यास्ते भवदीक्षणक्षणगतेः पात्रीकृ ताः स्वीकृ ताः ॥ ३९ ॥
units.transliteration: ayaṃ svabhāvaḥ svata eva yatpara śramāpanodapravaṇaṃ mahātmanām | sudhāṃśureṣa svayamarkakarkaśa prabhābhitaptāmavati kṣitiṃ kila || 38 ||
units.translation: “Indeed, it is the very nature of the magnanimous ones to help remove the troubles of others, even as the moon cools the earth, scorched by the ﬂaming rays of the sun, of its own accord.”

units.sanskrit: यस्य सिन्निधमात्रेण देहेिन्द्रयमनोिधयः । िवषयेषु स्वकीयेषु वतर्न्ते प्रेिरता इव ॥ १२९ ॥
units.transliteration: yasya sannidhimātreṇa dehendriyamanodhiyaḥ | viṣayeṣu svakīyeṣu vartante preritā iva || 129 ||
units.translation: “Simply by whose presence, the body, senses, mind and intellect act in their spheres as if impelled thereto—that is the Ātman.

units.sanskrit: मोक्षस्य हेतुः प्रथमो िनगद्यते वैराग्यमत्यन्तमिनत्यवस्तुषु । ततः शमश्चािप दमिस्तितक्षा न्यासः प्रसक्तािखलकमर्णां भृशम् ॥ ६९ ॥
units.transliteration: mokṣasya hetuḥ prathamo nigadyate vairāgyamatyantamanityavastuṣu | tataḥ śamaścāpi damastitikṣā nyāsaḥ prasaktākhilakarmaṇāṃ bhṛśam || 69 ||
units.translation: “The ﬁrst step to liberation is complete detachment from impermanent things. Then follow calmness, self-control, forbearance, and complete renunciation of all selﬁsh actions.“

units.sanskrit: िवषयाशामहापाशाद्यो िवमुक्तः सुदुस्त्यजात् । स एव कल्पते मुक्त्यै नान्यः षट्शास्त्रवेद्यिप ॥ ७८ ॥
units.transliteration: viṣayāśāmahāpāśādyo vimuktaḥ sudustyajāt | sa eva kalpate muktyai nānyaḥ ṣaṭśāstravedyapi || 78 ||
units.translation: “One who is liberated from the terrible bonds of desires for sense objects, so very difﬁcult to renounce, is alone ﬁt for liberation—and no one else, even if well-versed in all six schools of Vedic philosophy.”

units.sanskrit: अतिस्मं स्तद्बुिद्धः प्रभवित िवमूढस्य तमसा िववेकाभावाद्वै स्फु रित भुजगे रज्जुिधषणा । ततोऽनथर्व्रातो िनपतित समादातुरिधकः ततो योऽसद्ग्राहः स िह भवित बन्धः शृणु सखे ॥ १३८ ॥
units.transliteration: atasmiṃstadbuddhiḥ prabhavati vimūḍhasya tamasā vivekābhāvādvai sphurati bhujage rajjudhiṣaṇā | tato'narthavrāto nipatati samādāturadhikaḥ tato yo'sadgrāhaḥ sa hi bhavati bandhaḥ śṛṇu sakhe || 138 ||
units.translation: “By ajñāna, which is of the nature of tamas, an undiscriminating man mistakes what is not a thing as that thing. This is due to lack of discrimination, like thinking a serpent to be a rope. Then great calamities befall one subject to it. Hence, listen, my friend! Mistaking not-Self for the Self is what constitutes bondage.“
units.note: Rope-snake: ajnana (tamas) causes misidentification of Self with non-Self

units.sanskrit: वैराग्यं च मुमुक्षुत्वं तीव्रं यस्य तु िवद्यते । तिस्मन्नेवाथर्वन्तः स्युः फलवन्तः शमादयः ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: vairāgyaṃ ca mumukṣutvaṃ tīvraṃ yasya tu vidyate | tasminnevārthavantaḥ syuḥ phalavantaḥ śamādayaḥ || 29 ||
units.translation: Calmness and other practices have their meaning and bear fruit indeed only in one who has an intense spirit of renunciation and yearning for liberation.
units.note: Calmness and other practices bear fruit only with intense vairagya and mumukshutva

units.sanskrit: स्वप्नेऽथर्शून्ये सृजित स्वशक्त्या भोक्त्रािदिवश्वं मन एव सवर्म् । तथैव जाग्रत्यिप नो िवशेषः तत्सवर्मेतन्मनसो िवजृम्भणम् ॥ १७० ॥
units.transliteration: na hyastyavidyā manaso'tiriktā mano hyavidyā bhavabandhahetuḥ | tasminvinaṣṭe sakalaṃ vinaṣṭaṃ vijṛmbhite'sminsakalaṃ vijṛmbhate || 169 ||
units.translation: “There is no avidyā apart from the mind. The mind

units.sanskrit: अध्यासदोषात्पुरुषस्य संसृितः अध्यासबन्धस्त्वमुनैव किल्पतः । रजस्तमोदोषवतोऽिववेिकनो जन्मािददुःखस्य िनदानमेतत् ॥ १७९ ॥
units.transliteration: adhyāsadoṣātpuruṣasya saṃsṛtiḥ adhyāsabandhastvamunaiva kalpitaḥ | rajastamodoṣavato'vivekino janmādiduḥkhasya nidānametat || 179 ||
units.translation: “The defect of superimposition causes transmigration. The bondage of superimposition is created by the mind alone. It is the primal cause of the griefs of birth, suffering etc. for the man without discrimination, caught up in rajas and tamas.“

units.sanskrit: ब्रह्मानन्दरसानुभूितकिलतैः पूतैर्ः सुशीतैयु र्तैः युष्मद्वाक्कलशोिज्झतैः श्रुितसुखैवार्क्यामृतैः सेचय । संतप्तं भवतापदावदहनज्वालािभरेनं प्रभो धन्यास्ते भवदीक्षणक्षणगतेः पात्रीकृ ताः स्वीकृ ताः ॥ ३९ ॥
units.transliteration: brahmānandarasānubhūtikalitaiḥ pūrtaiḥ suśītairyutaiḥ yuṣmadvākkalaśojjhitaiḥ śrutisukhairvākyāmṛtaiḥ secaya | saṃtaptaṃ bhavatāpadāvadahanajvālābhirenaṃ prabho dhanyāste bhavadīkṣaṇakṣaṇagateḥ pātrīkṛtāḥ svīkṛtāḥ || 39 ||
units.translation: “O Lord, thy nectarean speech, sweetened by the elixir- like Bliss of Brahman—pure, cooling, issuing in streams from thy lips as from a pitcher, and pleasing to the ear— do thou shower upon me who am tormented by worldly afﬂictions as by the ﬂames of a forest ﬁre. Blessed are those who have received even a passing glance from Thy eyes, accepting them under Thy protection.”

units.sanskrit: येन िवश्विमदं व्याप्तं यं न व्याप्नोित िकं चन । अभारूपिमदं सवर्ं यं भान्त्यमनुभात्ययम् ॥ १२८ ॥
units.transliteration: yena viśvamidaṃ vyāptaṃ yaṃ na vyāpnoti kiṃcana | abhārūpamidaṃ sarvaṃ yaṃ bhāntyamanubhātyayam || 128 ||
units.translation: “That by which this universe is enveloped, which no object whatsoever envelops; that effulgent one on which all this universe of the form of non-effulgence depends for its shining—that is the Ātman.

units.sanskrit: शृणुष्वाविहतो िवद्वन्यन्मया समुदीयर्ते । तदेतच्छ्रवणात्सद्यो भवबन्धािद्वमोक्ष्यसे ॥ ६८ ॥
units.transliteration: śṛṇuṣvāvahito vidvanyanmayā samudīryate | tadetacchravaṇātsadyo bhavabandhādvimokṣyase || 68 ||
units.translation: Listen attentively, O learned one, to what I shall now tell you. Hearing it, you shall immediately be liberated from the bondage of worldly existence.”

units.sanskrit: सन्नाप्यसन्नाप्युभयाित्मका नो िभन्नाप्यिभन्नाप्युभयाित्मका नो । साङ्गाप्यनङ्गा ह्युभयाित्मका नो महाद्भुतािनवर्चनीयरूपा ॥ १०९ ॥
units.transliteration: avyaktanāmnī parameśaśaktiḥ anādyavidyā triguṇātmikā parā | kāryānumeyā sudhiyaiva māyā yayā jagatsarvamidaṃ prasūyate || 108 ||
units.translation: “Māyā is called avyakta. It is the power of Parameśvara. It is beginningless avidyā. It is composed of three guṇas. It is superior to its effects, and is to be inferred from them by the wise

units.sanskrit: िशष्य उवाच । कृ पया श्रूयतां स्वािमन् प्रश्नोऽयं िक्रयते मया । यदुत्तरमहं श्रुत्वा कृ ताथर्ः स्यां भवन्मुखात् ॥ ४८ ॥
units.transliteration: śiṣya uvāca | kṛpayā śrūyatāṃ svāmin praśno'yaṃ kriyate mayā | yaduttaramahaṃ śrutvā kṛtārthaḥ syāṃ bhavanmukhāt || 48 ||
units.translation: śiṣya uvāca |

units.sanskrit: देहोऽहिमत्येव जडस्य बुिद्धः देहे च जीवे िवदुषस्त्वहंधीः । िववेकिवज्ञानवतो महात्मनो ब्रह्माहिमत्येव मितः सदात्मिन ॥ १६० ॥
units.transliteration: tvaṅmāṃsamedo'sthipurīṣarāśā vahaṃmatiṃ mūḍhajanaḥ karoti | vilakṣaṇaṃ vetti vicāraśīlo nijasvarūpaṃ paramārtha bhūtam || 159 ||
units.translation: “The fool identiﬁes Ātman with his body made of skin, ﬂesh, fat, bones and excreta. The man of discrimination, however, knows his true nature to be the Supreme Reality.“

units.sanskrit: आदौ िनत्यािनत्यवस्तुिववेकः पिरगम्यते । इहामुत्रफलभोगिवरागस्तदनन्तरम् । शमािदषट्कसम्पित्तमु र्मुक्षुत्विमित स्फु टम् ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: ādau nityānityavastuvivekaḥ parigamyate | ihāmutraphalabhogavirāgastadanantaram śamādiṣaṭkasampattirmumukṣutvamiti sphuṭam || 19 ||
units.translation: ādau nityānityavastuvivekaḥ parigamyate |

units.sanskrit: उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं मग्नं संसारवािरधौ । योगारूढत्वमासाद्य सम्यग्दशर्निनष्ठया ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: uddharedātmanātmānaṃ magnaṃ saṃsāravāridhau | yogārūḍhatvamāsādya samyagdarśananiṣṭhayā || 9 ||
units.translation: Becoming absorbed in profound abstract meditation through devotion to right discrimination, one should lift oneself from the ocean of birth and death wherein one has come to be drowned.
units.note: Raise yourself by yourself from the ocean of samsara through discrimination

units.sanskrit: कोशैरन्नमयाद्यैः पञ्चिभरात्मा न संवृतो भाित । िनजशिक्तसमुत्पन्नैः शैवालपटलैिरवाम्बु वापीस्थम् ॥ १४९ ॥
units.transliteration: kośairannamayādyaiḥ pañcabhirātmā na saṃvṛto bhāti | nijaśaktisamutpannaiḥ śaivālapaṭalairivāmbu vāpīstham || 149 ||
units.translation: “The Ātman does not shine clearly, being covered by the ﬁve food and other sheaths which have arisen by its own power, like the water in a well covered by a sheet of moss, which is born out of itself.”

units.sanskrit: वाग्वैखरी शब्दझरी शास्त्रव्याख्यानकौशलम् । वैदुष्यं िवदुषां तद्वद्भुक्तये न तु मुक्तये ॥ ५८ ॥
units.transliteration: vāgvaikharī śabdajharī śāstravyākhyānakauśalam | vaiduṣyaṃ viduṣāṃ tadvadbhuktaye na tu muktaye || 58 ||
units.translation: “Loud speech in a stream of words, efﬁciency in commenting upon the scriptures, erudition—these bring only a little material satisfaction to the scholar; they are insufﬁcient to bring about liberation.”

units.sanskrit: िमश्रस्य सत्त्वस्य भविन्त धमार्ः त्वमािनताद्या िनयमा यमाद्याः । श्रद्धा च भिक्तश्च मुमुक्षता च दैवी च सम्पित्तरसिन्नवृित्तः ॥ ११८ ॥
units.transliteration: miśrasya sattvasya bhavanti dharmāḥ tvamānitādyā niyamā yamādyāḥ | śraddhā ca bhaktiśca mumukṣatā ca daivī ca sampattirasannivṛttiḥ || 118 ||
units.translation: “The features of mixed sattva are the complete absence of pride, etc., the presence of yama-niyama etc., and faith, devotion, longing for liberation, the divine tendencies, and natural withdrawal from everything unreal.“

units.sanskrit: बाह्येिन्द्रयैः स्थूलपदाथर्सेवां स्रक्चन्दनस्त्र्यािदिविचत्ररूपाम् । करोित जीवः स्वयमेतदात्मना तस्मात्प्रशिस्तवर्पुषोऽस्य जागरे ॥ ८९ ॥
units.transliteration: pañcīkṛtebhyo bhūtebhyaḥ sthūlebhyaḥ pūrvakarmaṇā | samutpannamidaṃ sthūlaṃ bhogāyatanamātmanaḥ avasthā jāgarastasya sthūlārthānubhavo yataḥ || 88 ||
units.translation: “This gross body is produced by one’s past-life karma

units.sanskrit: योऽयं िवज्ञानमायाः प्राणेषु हृिद स्फु रत्ययं ज्योितः । कू टस्थः सन्नात्मा कतार् भोक्ता भवत्युपािधस्थः ॥ १८९ ॥
units.transliteration: yo'yaṃ vijñānamāyāḥ prāṇeṣu hṛdi sphuratyayaṃ jyotiḥ | kūṭasthaḥ sannātmā kartā bhoktā bhavatyupādhisthaḥ || 189 ||
units.translation: “The Self, which is Absolute Knowledge, shines within
units.note: Self is Absolute Knowledge, shines within

units.sanskrit: कत्रार्िदभावं प्रितपद्य राजते यत्र स्वयं भाित ह्ययं परात्मा । धीमात्रकोपािधरशेषसाक्षी न िलप्यते तत्कृ तकमर्लेशैः यस्मादसङ्गस्तत एव कमर्िभः न िलप्यते िकं िचदुपािधना कृ तैः ॥ ९९ ॥
units.transliteration: svapno bhavatyasya vibhaktyavasthā svamātraśeṣeṇa vibhāti yatra | svapne tu buddhiḥ svayameva jāgrat- kālīnanānāvidhavāsanābhiḥ || 98 ||
units.translation: “Dream is the state when the subtle body is distinctly in expression, where it expresses all by itself. In dream, the intellect by itself revels as the agent of experiences etc., due to the various impressions it gathered during the waking state.“

units.sanskrit: बाह्येिन्द्रयैः स्थूलपदाथर्सेवां स्रक्चन्दनस्त्र्यािदिविचत्ररूपाम् । करोित जीवः स्वयमेतदात्मना तस्मात्प्रशिस्तवर्पुषोऽस्य जागरे ॥ ८९ ॥
units.transliteration: bāhyendriyaiḥ sthūlapadārthasevāṃ srakcandanastryādivicitrarūpām | karoti jīvaḥ svayametadātmanā tasmātpraśastirvapuṣo'sya jāgare || 89 ||
units.translation: “Identifying itself with the body, the jīva enjoys gross objects like garlands, sandalwood paste, women etc., through the external organs. Hence the importance of this body in waking consciousness.“

units.sanskrit: देहािदिनष्ठाश्रमधमर्कमर् गुणािभमानः सततं ममेित । िवज्ञानकोशोऽयमितप्रकाशः प्रकृ ष्टसािन्नध्यवशात्परात्मनः । अतो भवत्येष उपािधरस्य यदात्मधीः संसरित भ्रमेण ॥ १८८ ॥
units.transliteration: dehādiniṣṭhāśramadharmakarma guṇābhimānaḥ satataṃ mameti | vijñānakośo’yamatiprakāśaḥ prakṛṣṭasānnidhyavaśātparātmanaḥ | ato bhavatyeṣa upādhirasyayadātmadhīḥ saṃsarati bhrameṇa || 188 ||
units.translation: “Identifying with the attributes of the stages of life, their duties and functions, which actually belong to the body,

units.sanskrit: कत्रार्िदभावं प्रितपद्य राजते यत्र स्वयं भाित ह्ययं परात्मा । धीमात्रकोपािधरशेषसाक्षी न िलप्यते तत्कृ तकमर्लेशैः यस्मादसङ्गस्तत एव कमर्िभः न िलप्यते िकं िचदुपािधना कृ तैः ॥ ९९ ॥
units.transliteration: kartrādibhāvaṃ pratipadya rājate yatra svayaṃ bhāti hyayaṃ parātmā | dhīmātrakopādhiraśeṣasākṣīna na lipyate tatkṛtakarmaleśaiḥ yasmādasaṅgastata eva karmabhiḥ na lipyate kiṃcidupādhinā kṛtaiḥ || 99 ||
units.translation: “In this dreaming condition, the supreme Self shines in its own glory, with the intellect as its only limitation—the witness of everything, uncontaminated in the least by the activities of the intellect. Since it is entirely unattached, it is not tainted by any action that its apparent conditioning may perform.“
units.note: In dream, the Self shines as witness — uncontaminated by activities of the mind

units.sanskrit: ब्रह्म सत्यं जगिन्मथ्येत्येवंरूपो िविनश्चयः । सोऽयं िनत्यािनत्यवस्तुिववेकः समुदाहृतः ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: brahma satyaṃ jaganmithyetyevaṃrūpo viniścayaḥ | so'yaṃ nityānityavastuvivekaḥ samudāhṛtaḥ || 20 ||
units.translation: A ﬁrm conviction that Brahman alone is Real and the universe unreal is known as viveka: discrimination between the Real and the unreal.
units.note: Brahman alone is Real, the universe is unreal — definition of viveka

units.sanskrit: अत्रात्मबुिद्धं त्यज मूढबुद्धे त्वङ् मांसमेदोऽिस्थपुरीषराशौ । सवार्त्मिन ब्रह्मिण िनिवर् कल्पे कु रुष्व शािन्त परमां भजस्व ॥ १६१ ॥
units.transliteration: deho'hamityeva jaḍasya buddhiḥ dehe ca jīve viduṣastvahaṃdhīḥ | vivekavijñānavato mahātmano brahmāhamityeva matiḥ sadātmani || 160 ||
units.translation: “The fool thinks ‘I am the body’. The śāstrik scholar has

units.sanskrit: यद्बोद्धव्यं तवेदानीमात्मानात्मिववेचनम् । तदुच्यते मया सम्यक्श्रुत्वात्मन्यवधारय ॥ ७१ ॥
units.transliteration: yadboddhavyaṃ tavedānīmātmānātmavivecanam | taducyate mayā samyakśrutvātmanyavadhāraya || 71 ||
units.translation: “Now I am going to describe the discrimination between the Self and not-Self most elaborately. It is what you ought to know. Listen to it properly and then decide about it well in your mind.”

units.sanskrit: अत्रैव सत्त्वात्मिन धीगुहायां अव्याकृ ताकाश उशत्प्रकाशः । आकाश उच्चै रिववत्प्रकाशते स्वतेजसा िवश्विमदं प्रकाशयन् ॥ १३२ ॥
units.transliteration: eṣo'ntarātmā puruṣaḥ purāṇo nirantarākhaṇḍasukhānubhūtiḥ | sadaikarūpaḥ pratibodhamātro yeneṣitā vāgasavaścaranti || 131 ||
units.translation: “This is the innermost Ātman, the primordial Puruṣa ever abiding in the body, the ancient eternal of the nature of integral experience of bliss, ever the same, accompanying every mental

units.sanskrit: मोक्षस्य कांक्षा यिद वै तवािस्त त्यजाितदूरािद्वषयािन्वषं यथा । पीयूषवत्तोषदयाक्षमाजर्व प्रशािन्तदान्तीभर्ज िनत्यमादरात् ॥ ८२ ॥
units.transliteration: mokṣasya kāṃkṣā yadi vai tavāsti tyajātidūrādviṣayānviṣaṃ yathā | pīyūṣavattoṣadayākṣamārjava praśāntidāntīrbhaja nityamādarāt || 82 ||
units.translation: “If you ever desire liberation, ﬂing away all desires as if they were poison. Drink daily with great eagerness the nectar of contentment, compassion, forbearance, truth, straightforwardness, calmness and self-control (control of the mind and the external senses).“

units.sanskrit: अतोऽस्य जीवभावोऽिप िनत्या भवित संसृितः । न िनवतेर्त तन्मोक्षः कथं मे श्रीगुरो वद ॥ १९३ ॥
units.transliteration: ato'sya jīvabhāvo'pi nityā bhavati saṃsṛtiḥ | na nivarteta tanmokṣaḥ kathaṃ me śrīguro vada || 193 ||
units.translation: “So the embodied state of the Self must also be without end, ever subject to transmigration. Please tell me, O revered Master, how then there can be liberation for the Self?“

units.sanskrit: िवषयाणामानुकू ल्ये सुखी दुःखी िवपयर्ये । सुखं दुःखं च तद्धमर्ः सदानन्दस्य नात्मनः ॥ १०५ ॥
units.transliteration: viṣayāṇāmānukūlye sukhī duḥkhī viparyaye | sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ ca taddharmaḥ sadānandasya nātmanaḥ || 105 ||
units.translation: “When sense objects are favorable, one considers oneself happy, and unhappy if they are otherwise. Pleasure and suffering

units.sanskrit: वेदान्ताथर्िवचारेण जायते ज्ञानमुत्तमम् । तेनात्यिन्तकसंसारदुःखनाशो भवत्यनु ॥ ४५ ॥
units.transliteration: vedāntārthavicāreṇa jāyate jñānamuttamam | tenātyantikasaṃsāraduḥkhanāśo bhavatyanu || 45 ||
units.translation: “The highest knowledge arises from inquiry into the

units.sanskrit: देहोऽयमन्नभवनोऽन्नमायास्तु कोशः चान्नेन जीवित िवनश्यित तिद्वहीनः । त्वक्चमर्मांसरुिधरािस्थपुरीषरािशः नायं स्वयं भिवतुमहर्ित िनत्यशुद्धः ॥ १५४ ॥
units.transliteration: deho'yamannabhavano'nnamāyāstu kośaḥ cānnena jīvati vinaśyati tadvihīnaḥ | tvakcarmamāṃsarudhirāsthipurīṣarāśiḥ nāyaṃ svayaṃ bhavitumarhati nityaśuddhaḥ || 154 ||
units.translation: “The sheath of the gross body is annamaya: food-born and food-sustained. It lives by food and dies without it. It is an aggregate of skin, ﬂesh, blood and the excreta. It does not deserve to be the ever-pure Ātman.“

units.sanskrit: अिधकािरणमाशास्ते फलिसिद्धिवर् शेषतः । उपाया देशकालाद्याः सन्त्यिस्मन्सहकािरणः ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: adhikāriṇamāśāste phalasiddhirviśeṣataḥ | upāyā deśakālādyāḥ santyasminsahakāriṇaḥ || 14 ||
units.translation: Success depends mainly on the qualiﬁcations of the aspirant; time, place and other such means are but auxiliaries in this regard.

units.sanskrit: लब्ध्वा कथंिचन्नरजन्म दुलर्भं ंस्त्वं श्रुितपारदशर्नम् । तत्रािप पु यस्त्वात्ममुक्तौ न यतेत मूढधीः स ह्यात्महा स्वं िविनहन्त्यसद्ग्रहात् ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: labdhvā kathaṃcinnarajanma durlabhaṃ tatrāpi puṃstvaṃ śrutipāradarśanam | yastvātmamuktau na yateta mūḍhadhīḥ sa hyātmahā svaṃ vinihantyasadgrahāt || 4 ||
units.translation: The man who, having somehow obtained a human birth with a male body and mastery of the Vedas to boot, is so foolish not to exert himself for Self- realization, verily commits suicide— for he kills himself by clinging to things unreal.

units.sanskrit: एताभ्यामेव शिक्तभ्यां बन्धः पु ंसः समागतः । याभ्यां िवमोिहतो देहं मत्वात्मानं भ्रमत्ययम् ॥ १४४ ॥
units.transliteration: etābhyāmeva śaktibhyāṃ bandhaḥ puṃsaḥ samāgataḥ | yābhyāṃ vimohito dehaṃ matvātmānaṃ bhramatyayam || 144 ||
units.translation: “Constrained by these two powers, deluded by them, subject to bondage, a man mistaking the body to be the Ātman wanders about in such delusion.”

units.sanskrit: अिवद्याकामकमार्िदपाशबन्धं िवमोिचतुम् । कः शक्नुयािद्वनात्मानं कल्पकोिटशतैरिप ॥ ५५ ॥
units.transliteration: avidyākāmakarmādipāśabandhaṃ vimocitum | kaḥ śaknuyādvinātmānaṃ kalpakoṭiśatairapi || 55 ||
units.translation: “Who else but oneself can help rid oneself of the bondage caused by the chains of ignorance, desire, action, etc.—even in hundreds of millions of years?”

units.sanskrit: अज्ञानमालस्यजडत्विनद्रा प्रमादमूढत्वमुखास्तमोगुणाः । एतैः प्रयुक्तो निह वेित्त िकं िचन् िनद्रालुवत्स्तम्भवदेव ितष्ठित ॥ ११६ ॥
units.transliteration: abhāvanā vā viparītabhāvanā asaṃbhāvanā vipratipattirasyāḥ | saṃsargayuktaṃ na vimuñcati dhruvaṃ vikṣepaśaktiḥ kṣapayatyajasram || 115 ||
units.translation: “Absence of right judgement, the impression that the body is the self, wrong impressions and doubt never release from their hold a person who is affected by this concealing power. Then, the projecting power always destroys the person so affected.“

units.sanskrit: मनोमयो नािप भवेत्परात्मा ह्याद्यन्तवत्त्वात्पिरणािमभावात् । दुःखात्मकत्वािद्वषयत्वहेतोः द्रष्टा िह दृश्यात्मतया न दृष्टः ॥ १८३ ॥
units.transliteration: manomayo nāpi bhavetparātmā hyādyantavattvātpariṇāmibhāvāt | duḥkhātmakatvādviṣayatvahetoḥ draṣṭā hi dṛśyātmatayā na dṛṣṭaḥ || 183 ||
units.translation: “The manomaya-kośa also cannot be the Paramātma, for it has a beginning and an end, is subject to modiﬁcations, is of the nature of suffering, and is an object. The seen object cannot be the seeing subject.“
units.note: Manomaya-kośa cannot be Paramātman — has beginning, end, modifications, is an object

units.sanskrit: बुद्धीिन्द्रयािण श्रवणं त्वगिक्ष घ्राणं च िजव्हा िवषयावबोधनात् । वाक्पािणपादा गुदमप्युपस्थः कमेर्िन्द्रयािण प्रवणेन कमर्सु ॥ ९२ ॥
units.transliteration: buddhīndriyāṇi śravaṇaṃ tvagakṣi ghrāṇaṃ ca jivhā viṣayāvabodhanāt | vākpāṇipādā gudamapyupasthaḥ karmendriyāṇi pravaṇena karmasu || 92 ||
units.translation: “The ear, the skin, the eyes, the nose and the

units.sanskrit: सवर्प्रकारप्रिमितप्रशािन्तः बीजात्मनाविस्थितरेव बुद्धेः । सुषुिप्तरेतस्य िकल प्रतीितः िकं िचन्न वेद्मीित जगत्प्रिसद्धेः ॥ १२१ ॥
units.transliteration: sarvaprakārapramitipraśāntiḥ bījātmanāvasthitireva buddheḥ | suṣuptiretasya kila pratītiḥ kiṃcinna vedmīti jagatprasiddheḥ || 121 ||
units.translation: “In suṣupti all pramaṇas (sources of knowledge) are still. The buddhi remains only in the form of a seed. The test of this is the universal verdict: ‘I did not know anything while asleep’.“

units.sanskrit: अज्ञानसपर्दष्टस्य ब्रह्मज्ञानौषधं िवना । िकमु वेदैश्च शास्त्रैश्च िकमु मन्त्रैः िकमौषधैः ॥ ६१ ॥
units.transliteration: ajñānasarpadaṣṭasya brahmajñānauṣadhaṃ vinā | kimu vedaiśca śāstraiśca kimu mantraiḥ kimauṣadhaiḥ || 61 ||
units.translation: “For one who has been bitten by the serpent of ignorance, the only remedy is knowledge of Brahman. Of what use are the Vedas and the scriptures, mantras and medicines to such a victim of poison?”

units.sanskrit: स्वप्नेऽथर्शून्ये सृजित स्वशक्त्या भोक्त्रािदिवश्वं मन एव सवर्म् । तथैव जाग्रत्यिप नो िवशेषः तत्सवर्मेतन्मनसो िवजृम्भणम् ॥ १७० ॥
units.transliteration: svapne'rthaśūnye sṛjati svaśaktyā bhoktrādiviśvaṃ mana eva sarvam | tathaiva jāgratyapi no viśeṣaḥ tatsarvametanmanaso vijṛmbhaṇam || 170 ||
units.translation: “In the absence of external objects in dream, the mind alone creates everything—the enjoyer etc.—by its power. So, too, there is no difference in the waking state. All that is also only a projection of the mind.“

units.sanskrit: एतयोमर्न्दता यत्र िवरक्तत्वमुमुक्षयोः । मरौ सलीलवत्तत्र शमादेभार्नमात्रता ॥ ३० ॥
units.transliteration: etayormandatā yatra viraktatvamumukṣayoḥ | marau salīlavattatra śamāderbhānamātratā || 30 ||
units.translation: However, where renunciation and yearning for

units.sanskrit: तच्छैवालापनये सम्यक्सिललं प्रतीयते शुद्धम् । ंसः ॥ १५० ॥ तृष्णासन्तापहरं सद्यः सौख्यप्रदं परं पु
units.transliteration: tacchaivālāpanaye samyaksalilaṃ pratīyate śuddham | tṛṣṇāsantāpaharaṃ sadyaḥ saukhyapradaṃ paraṃ puṃsaḥ || 150 ||
units.translation: “When the moss is removed, the water becomes visible in all clarity. It removes the pangs of thirst and immediately ﬁlls men with happiness.“
units.note: Moss removed — water seen clearly: jnana removes avidya covering the Self

units.sanskrit: संन्यस्य सवर्कमार्िण भवबन्धिवमुक्तये । यत्यतां पिण्डतैधीर्रैरात्माभ्यास उपिस्थतैः ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: saṃnyasya sarvakarmāṇi bhavabandhavimuktaye | yatyatāṃ paṇḍitairdhīrairātmābhyāsa upasthitaiḥ || 10 ||
units.translation: Let the wise and erudite man, commencing the practice of realization of Ātman, renounce all works and cut loose the bonds of birth and death.

units.sanskrit: उच्छ्वासिनःश्वासिवजृम्भणक्षुत् प्रस्यन्दनाद्युत्क्रमणािदकाः िक्रयाः । प्राणािदकमार्िण वदिन्त तज्ञाः प्राणस्य धमार्वशनािपपासे ॥ १०२ ॥
units.transliteration: andhatvamandatvapaṭutvadharmāḥ sauguṇyavaiguṇyavaśāddhi cakṣuṣaḥ | bādhiryamūkatvamukhāstathaiva śrotrādidharmā na tu vetturātmanaḥ || 101 ||
units.translation: “The qualities of blindness, defective and clear vision belong to the eye because of its healthy or diseased condition. Similarly, qualities like deafness, dumbness, etc. pertain to the ear etc., but they do not belong to the Ātman, which is always the witness.“
units.note: Blindness/deafness belong to the eye/ear, not to the witness Self

units.sanskrit: िवद्वान् स तस्मा उपसित्तमीयुषे मुमुक्षवे साधु यथोक्तकािरणे । प्रशान्तिचत्ताय शमािन्वताय तत्त्वोपदेशं कृ पयैव कु यार्त् ॥ ४२ ॥
units.transliteration: tathā vadantaṃ śaraṇāgataṃ svaṃ saṃsāradāvānalatāpataptam | nirīkṣya kāruṇyarasārdradṛṣṭyā dadyādabhītiṃ sahasā mahātmā || 41 ||
units.translation: As he speaks, afﬂicted by the blazing forest-ﬁre

units.sanskrit: य एषु मूढा िवषयेषु बद्धा रागोरुपाशेन सुदुदर्मेन । आयािन्त िनयार्न्त्यध ऊध्वर्मुच्चैः स्वकमर्दूतेन जवेन नीताः ॥ ७५ ॥
units.transliteration: ya eṣu mūḍhā viṣayeṣu baddhā rāgorupāśena sudurdamena | āyānti niryāntyadha ūrdhvamuccaiḥ svakarmadūtena javena nītāḥ || 75 ||
units.translation: “Those thoughtless ones who are bound to these sense-objects by the stout ropes of attachment so very difﬁcult to cut asunder, come and go, carried up and down by the compelling force of the envoy of the reactions of their own past actions.“

units.sanskrit: ं त्वं स्वमात्मानमात्मन्य् िनयिमतमनसामु अयमहिमित साक्षािद्विद्ध बुिद्धप्रसादात् । ं जिनमरणतरङ्गापारसंसारिसन्धु प्रतर भव कृ ताथोर् ब्रह्मरूपेण संस्थः ॥ १३६ ॥
units.transliteration: prakṛtivikṛtibhinnaḥ śuddhabodhasvabhāvaḥ sadasadidamaśeṣaṃ bhāsayannirviśeṣaḥ | vilasati paramātmā jāgradādiṣvavasthā svahamahamiti sākṣātsākṣirūpeṇa buddheḥ || 135 ||
units.translation: “Different from prakṛti (ignorance) and its tranformations, of the nature of pure intelligence, and without qualities, illumining all this material world with all that has form and is formless, the Ātman shines through the waking and other states as their witness, and is referred to as the ‘I’.“
units.note: The Ātman: pure intelligence, without qualities, illumining all material world — different from prakriti

units.sanskrit: सहनं सवर्दुःखानामप्रतीकारपूवर्कम् । िचन्तािवलापरिहतं सा ितितक्षा िनगद्यते ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: sahanaṃ sarvaduḥkhānāmapratīkārapūrvakam | cintāvilāparahitaṃ sā titikṣā nigadyate || 24 ||
units.translation: Bearing all afﬂictions without caring to redress them, while remaining free from anxiety or lamentation

units.sanskrit: देहात्मधीरेव नृणामसिद्धयां जन्मािददुःखप्रभवस्य बीजम् । यतस्ततस्त्वं जिह तां प्रयत्नात् त्यक्ते तु िचत्ते न पुनभर्वाशा ॥ १६४ ॥
units.transliteration: dehātmadhīreva nṛṇāmasaddhiyāṃ janmādiduḥkhaprabhavasya bījam | yatastatastvaṃ jahi tāṃ prayatnāt tyakte tu citte na punarbhavāśā || 164 ||
units.translation: “For those whose intellect rests on asat, the notion that the body is the Ātman causes the arising of sorrows, beginning with birth. Therefore, put an end to this notion with all effort. Once the mind is detached from asat, there is no possibility of being born again.“

units.sanskrit: मोहं जिह महामृत्यु ं देहदारसुतािदषु । यं िजत्वा मुनयो यािन्त तिद्वष्णोः परमं पदम् ॥ ८६ ॥
units.transliteration: mohaṃ jahi mahāmṛtyuṃ dehadārasutādiṣu | yaṃ jitvā munayo yānti tadviṣṇoḥ paramaṃ padam || 86 ||
units.translation: Give up your attachment to the body, wife and sons, which is the great death. Conquering it, the sages

units.sanskrit: वागािद पञ्च श्रवणािद पञ्च प्राणािद पञ्चाभ्रमुखािन पञ्च । बुद्ध्याद्यिवद्यािप च कामकमर्णी पुयर्ष्टकं सूक्ष्मशरीरमाहुः ॥ ९६ ॥
units.transliteration: vāgādi pañca śravaṇādi pañca prāṇādi pañcābhramukhāni pañca | buddhyādyavidyāpi ca kāmakarmaṇī puryaṣṭakaṃ sūkṣmaśarīramāhuḥ || 96 ||
units.translation: “The aggregate of these eight—namely, the ﬁve

units.sanskrit: देहािदिनष्ठाश्रमधमर्कमर् गुणािभमानः सततं ममेित । िवज्ञानकोशोऽयमितप्रकाशः प्रकृ ष्टसािन्नध्यवशात्परात्मनः । अतो भवत्येष उपािधरस्य यदात्मधीः संसरित भ्रमेण ॥ १८८ ॥
units.transliteration: bhuṅkte vicitrāsvapi yoniṣu vrajan nāyāti niryātyadha ūrdhvameṣaḥ | asyaiva vijñānamāyāsya jāgrat svapnādyavasthāḥ sukhaduḥkhabhogaḥ || 187 ||
units.translation: “It comes and goes up and down, taking birth in various bodies. Waking, dream and other states of consciousness, and the experiences of joy and sorrow belong to this intellectual sheath.“

units.sanskrit: िववेकवैराग्यगुणाितरेकाच् छु द्धत्वमासाद्य मनो िवमुक्त्यै । भवत्यतो बुिद्धमतो मुमुक्षोस् ताभ्यां दृढाभ्यां भिवतव्यमग्रे ॥ १७५ ॥
units.transliteration: tasmānmanaḥ kāraṇamasya jantoḥ bandhasya mokṣasya ca vā vidhāne | bandhasya heturmalinaṃ rajoguṇaiḥ mokṣasya śuddhaṃ virajastamaskam || 174 ||
units.translation: “Therefore, the mind is the cause of both liberation and bondage. When tainted by the effects of rajas, it causes bondage. When it is free from rajas and tamas, it becomes the cause of liberation.“

units.sanskrit: तमाराध्य गुरुं भक्त्या प्रह्वप्रश्रयसेवनैः । प्रसन्नं तमनुप्राप्य पृच्छेज्ज्ञातव्यमात्मनः ॥ ३४ ॥
units.transliteration: tamārādhya guruṃ bhaktyā prahvapraśrayasevanaiḥ | prasannaṃ tamanuprāpya pṛcchejjñātavyamātmanaḥ || 34 ||
units.translation: Worship that Guru with deep devotion. And when he is pleased with your surrender, humility and service, approach and ask for what you must know.

units.sanskrit: यो िवजानाित सकलं जाग्रत्स्वप्नसुषुिप्तषु । बुिद्धतद्वृित्तसद्भावमभावमहिमत्ययम् ॥ १२६ ॥
units.transliteration: asti kaścitsvayaṃ nityamahaṃpratyayalambanaḥ | avasthātrayasākṣī saṃpañcakośavilakṣaṇaḥ || 125 ||
units.translation: “There is something which exists by itself as the substratum of the consciousness of ‘I’. Being the witness of the three states, it is different from the ﬁve sheaths. That is the Ātman.”
units.note: Substratum of I-consciousness, witness of the three states, beyond the five sheaths — that is the Ātman

units.sanskrit: आप्तोिक्तं खननं तथोपिरिशलाद्युत्कषर्णं स्वीकृ ितं िनक्षेपः समपेक्षते निह बिहः शब्दैस्तु िनगर्च्छित । तद्वद्ब्रह्मिवदोपदेशमननध्यानािदिभलर्भ्यते मायाकायर्ितरोिहतं स्वममलं तत्त्वं न दुयु र्िक्तिभः ॥ ६५ ॥
units.transliteration: āptoktiṃ khananaṃ tathopariśilādyutkarṣaṇaṃ svīkṛtiṃ nikṣepaḥ samapekṣate nahi bahiḥ śabdaistu nirgacchati | tadvadbrahmavidopadeśamananadhyānādibhirlabhyate māyākāryatirohitaṃ svamamalaṃ tattvaṃ na duryuktibhiḥ || 65 ||
units.translation: āptoktiṃ khananaṃ tathopariśilādyutkarṣaṇaṃ svīkṛtiṃ
units.note: Treasure hidden by earth: pure Truth hidden by Maya, found only through guru + manana + dhyana

units.sanskrit: ऋणमोचनकतार्रः िपतुः सिन्त सुतादयः । बन्धमोचनकतार् तु स्वस्मादन्यो न कश्चन ॥ ५१ ॥
units.transliteration: ṛṇamocanakartāraḥ pituḥ santi sutādayaḥ | bandhamocanakartā tu svasmādanyo na kaścana || 51 ||
units.translation: ṛṇamocana-kartāraḥ—to relieve from debts and obligations; pituḥ—of

units.sanskrit: िवक्षेपशक्ती रजसः िक्रयाित्मका यतः प्रवृित्तः प्रसृता पुराणी । रागादयोऽस्याः प्रभविन्त िनत्यं दुःखादयो ये मनसो िवकाराः ॥ १११ ॥
units.transliteration: vikṣepaśaktī rajasaḥ kriyātmikā yataḥ pravṛttiḥ prasṛtā purāṇī | rāgādayo'syāḥ prabhavanti nityaṃ duḥkhādayo ye manaso vikārāḥ || 111 ||
units.translation: “The vikṣepa-śakti pertains to rajas; it is of the nature of activity. From it is the well-

units.sanskrit: ितरोभूते स्वात्मन्यमलतरतेजोवित पुमान् अनात्मानं मोहादहिमित शरीरं कलयित । ततः कामक्रोधप्रभृितिभरमु ं बन्धनगुणैः परं िवक्षेपाख्या रजस उरुशिक्तव्यर्थयित ॥ १४० ॥
units.transliteration: tirobhūte svātmanyamalataratejovati pumān anātmānaṃ mohādahamiti śarīraṃ kalayati | tataḥ kāmakrodhaprabhṛtibhiramuṃ bandhanaguṇaiḥ paraṃ vikṣepākhyā rajasa uruśaktirvyathayati || 140 ||
units.translation: “When one’s Ātman of supreme effulgence is concealed, a man considers his body to be the Ātman due to non-discrimination. Then, by the fetters of desire, anger, etc.,

units.sanskrit: पञ्चानामिप कोशानामपवादे िवभात्ययं शुद्धः । िनत्यानन्दैकरसः प्रत्यग्रूपः परः स्वयंज्योितः ॥ १५१ ॥
units.transliteration: pañcānāmapi kośānāmapavāde vibhātyayaṃ śuddhaḥ | nityānandaikarasaḥ pratyagrūpaḥ paraḥ svayaṃjyotiḥ || 151 ||
units.translation: “When the ﬁve sheaths are set aside, this pure, ever- blissful, indwelling, Supreme Self-effulgent Ātman appears clear—untouched by the anatman.“

units.sanskrit: िचत्तस्य शुद्धये कमर् न तु वस्तूपलब्धये । वस्तुिसिद्धिवर् चारेण न िकं िचत्कमर्कोिटिभः ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: cittasya śuddhaye karma na tu vastūpalabdhaye | vastusiddhirvicāreṇa na kiṃcitkarmakoṭibhiḥ || 11 ||
units.translation: Karma-yoga leads to puriﬁcation of the mind, not to perception of the Reality. Realization of Truth is attained by discrimination, and not in the least by ten million actions.

units.sanskrit: सवर्व्यापृितकरणं िलङ्गिमदं स्यािच्चदात्मनः पु ंसः । वास्यािदकिमव तक्ष्णस्तेनैवात्मा भवत्यसङ्गोऽयम् ॥ १०० ॥
units.transliteration: sarvavyāpṛtikaraṇaṃ liṅgamidaṃ syāccidātmanaḥ puṃsaḥ | vāsyādikamiva takṣṇastenaivātmā bhavatyasaṅgo'yam || 100 ||
units.translation: This liṅga-śarīra (subtle body) is the indispensable operative cause of all the activities of the individual self, which is to the nature of intelligence as tools are to a carpenter. For that reason the Ātman is unattached.

units.sanskrit: कथं तरेयं भविसन्धुमेतं का वा गितमेर् कतमोऽस्त्युपायः । जाने न िकिञ्ज्चत्कृ पयाव मां प्रभो संसारदुःखक्षितमातनुष्व ॥ ४० ॥
units.transliteration: kathaṃ tareyaṃ bhavasindhumetaṃ kā vā gatirme katamo'styupāyaḥ | jāne na kiñjcitkṛpayāva māṃ prabho saṃsāraduḥkhakṣatimātanuṣva || 40 ||
units.translation: “How to cross this ocean of worldly existence? What is to be my ultimate destination? Which of the many means should I adopt? I know nothing of these. O Lord! Save me and describe in all detail how to end the misery of this earthly existence.”

units.sanskrit: परस्परांशैिमर् िलतािन भूत्वा स्थूलािन च स्थूलशरीरहेतवः । मात्रास्तदीया िवषया भविन्त शब्दादयः पञ्च सुखाय भोक्तुः ॥ ७४ ॥
units.transliteration: parasparāṃśairmilitāni bhūtvā sthūlāni ca sthūlaśarīrahetavaḥ | mātrāstadīyā viṣayā bhavanti śabdādayaḥ pañca sukhāya bhoktuḥ || 74 ||
units.translation: “Having united with parts of one another, they become gross, and become the cause for the formation of the gross body. Their subtle essence constitutes the sense-objects, ﬁve in number, such as sound etc., which contribute to the enjoyment of the experiencer, the individual ego.“

units.sanskrit: प्रकृ ितिवकृ ितिभन्नः शुद्धबोधस्वभावः सदसिददमशेषं भासयिन्निवर् शेषः । िवलसित परमात्मा जाग्रदािदष्ववस्था स्वहमहिमित साक्षात्सािक्षरूपेण बुद्धेः ॥ १३५ ॥
units.transliteration: na jāyate no mriyate na vardhate na kṣīyate no vikaroti nityaḥ | vilīyamāne'pi vapuṣyamuṣmin na līyate kumbha ivāmbaraṃ svayam || 134 ||
units.translation: “The Ātman is not born; it does not die; it does not grow or decline; it does not change. It is eternal. Even if this body is destroyed, it does not become extinct—even as space does not become extinct on the destruction of the pot which enclosed it.“

units.sanskrit: शास्त्रस्य गुरुवाक्यस्य सत्यबुद्ध्यवधारणम् । सा श्रद्धा किथता सिद्भयर्या वस्तूपलभ्यते ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: śāstrasya guruvākyasya satyabuddhyavadhāraṇam | sā śraddhā kathitā sadbhiryayā vastūpalabhyate || 25 ||
units.translation: śāstrasya—of the scriptures; guruvākyasya—of the words of the
units.note: Sraddha in guru and scripture as prerequisite for manana

units.sanskrit: नैवात्मािप प्राणमयो वायुिवकारो गन्तागन्ता वायुवदन्तबर्िहरेषः । यस्माित्कं िचत्क्वािप न वेत्तीष्टमिनष्टं स्वं वान्यं वा िकं चन िनत्यं परतन्त्रः ॥ १६६ ॥
units.transliteration: karmendriyaiḥ pañcabhirañcito'yaṃ prāṇo bhavetprāṇamāyāstu kośaḥ | yenātmavānannamayo'nupūrṇaḥ pravartate'sau sakalakriyāsu || 165 ||
units.translation: “This prāṇa in combination with the ﬁve
units.note: Prāṇa-kośa analysis — vital sheath is not the Self

units.sanskrit: त्वङ् मांसरुिधरस्नायुमेदोमज्जािस्थसंकु लम् । पूणर्ं मूत्रपुरीषाभ्यां स्थूलं िनन्द्यिमदं वपुः ॥ ८७ ॥
units.transliteration: tvaṅmāṃsarudhirasnāyumedomajjāsthisaṃkulam | pūrṇaṃ mūtrapurīṣābhyāṃ sthūlaṃ nindyamidaṃ vapuḥ || 87 ||
units.translation: This gross body is despicable as it consists of skin, ﬂesh, blood, blood-vessels, fat, marrow and bones, urine and feces.

units.sanskrit: स्वप्नो भवत्यस्य िवभक्त्यवस्था स्वमात्रशेषेण िवभाित यत्र । स्वप्ने तु बुिद्धः स्वयमेव जाग्रत् कालीननानािवधवासनािभः ॥ ९८ ॥
units.transliteration: idaṃ śarīraṃ śṛṇu sūkṣmasaṃjñitaṃ liṅgaṃ tvapañcīkṛtasaṃbhavam | savāsanaṃ karmaphalānubhāvakaṃ svājñānato'nādirupādhirātmanaḥ || 97 ||
units.translation: “Listen! This subtle body called the sūkṣma-śarīra is

units.sanskrit: भुङ् क्ते िविचत्रास्विप योिनषु व्रजन् नायाित िनयार्त्यध ऊध्वर्मेषः । अस्यैव िवज्ञानमायास्य जाग्रत् स्वप्नाद्यवस्थाः सुखदुःखभोगः ॥ १८७ ॥
units.transliteration: anādikālo'yamahaṃsvabhāvo jīvaḥ samastavyavahāravoḍhā | karoti karmāṇyapi pūrvavāsanaḥ puṇyānyapuṇyāni ca tatphalāni || 186 ||
units.translation: “It is without beginning, is of the nature of the ego (I- sense), and is called the embodied self, which carries out the entire range of activities on the relative plane. It performs good and evil actions according to its previous latent tendencies, and experiences their results.“

units.sanskrit: िववेकवैराग्यगुणाितरेकाच् छु द्धत्वमासाद्य मनो िवमुक्त्यै । भवत्यतो बुिद्धमतो मुमुक्षोस् ताभ्यां दृढाभ्यां भिवतव्यमग्रे ॥ १७५ ॥
units.transliteration: vivekavairāgyaguṇātirekāc chuddhatvamāsādya mano vimuktyai | bhavatyato buddhimato mumukṣos tābhyāṃ dṛḍhābhyāṃ bhavitavyamagre || 175 ||
units.translation: “By attaining purity through cultivation of ﬁrm discrimination and uncompromising renunciation, the mind gets qualiﬁed for liberaton. So, a seeker for liberation must be ﬁrmly established in them ﬁrst.“
units.note: Discrimination and renunciation purify the mind and qualify it for liberation

units.sanskrit: स्वािमन्नमस्ते नतलोकबन्धो कारुण्यिसन्धो पिततं भवाब्धौ । मामुद्धरात्मीयकटाक्षदृष्ट्या ऋज्व्याितकारुण्यसुधािभवृष्ट्या ॥ ३५ ॥
units.transliteration: svāminnamaste natalokabandho kāruṇyasindho patitaṃ bhavābdhau | māmuddharātmīyakaṭākṣadṛṣṭyā ṛjvyātikāruṇyasudhābhivṛṣṭyā || 35 ||
units.translation: “O Master, O kinsman of all who reverently surrender unto Thee, Thou ocean of mercy, I salute Thee; save me, fallen as I am into this sea of worldly existence, with a direct glance from Thine eyes which shower the nectarean Grace Supreme.”

units.sanskrit: अथ ते संप्रवक्ष्यािम स्वरूपं परमात्मनः । यिद्वज्ञाय नरो बन्धान्मुक्तः कै वल्यमश्नुते ॥ १२४ ॥
units.transliteration: atha te saṃpravakṣyāmi svarūpaṃ paramātmanaḥ | yadvijñāya naro bandhānmuktaḥ kaivalyamaśnute || 124 ||
units.translation: “I shall now tell you clearly the nature of the Paramātman, by knowing which a man is freed from all bonds and attains liberation.”
