units.translation: As in a mirror, so within one's self; as in dreams, so in the world of the *manes*; as indistinctly seen in water, so in the world of the *Gandharvas*; as of light and shade, so in the world of *Brahma.*

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणां पृथग्भावमुदयास्तमयौ च यत् ।  पृथगुत्पद्यमानानां मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: indriyāṇāṃ pṛthagbhāvamudayāstamayau ca yat |  pṛthagutpadyamānānāṃ matvā dhīro na śocati || 6 ||
units.translation: The intelligent man knowing that the senses separately produced are distinct (from the *atman*) and also their rising and setting, does not grieve.

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनो मनसः सत्त्वमुत्तमम् ।  सत्त्वादधि महानात्मा महतोऽव्यक्तमुत्तमम् ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: indriyebhyaḥ paraṃ mano manasaḥ sattvamuttamam |  sattvādadhi mahānātmā mahato'vyaktamuttamam || 7 ||
units.translation: Beyond the senses is the mind; higher than the mind is the intellect; above the intellect is the great *atman*; higher than the *mahat* is *Avyaktam.*

units.sanskrit: अव्यक्तात्तु परः पुरुषो व्यापकोऽलिङ्ग एव च ।  यं ज्ञात्वा मुच्यते जन्तुरमृतत्वं च गच्छति ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: avyaktāttu paraḥ puruṣo vyāpako'liṅga eva ca |  yaṃ jñātvā mucyate janturamṛtatvaṃ ca gacchati || 8 ||
units.translation: Beyond the *Avyaktam* is *Purusha,* all-pervading and devoid of *linga*(indicative mark), whom knowing the mortal is freed and attains immortality.

units.sanskrit: न संदृशे तिष्ठति रूपमस्य न चक्शुषा पश्यति कश्चनैनम् ।  हृदा मनीषा मनसाभिक्लृप्तो य एतद्विदुरमृतास्ते भवन्ति ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: na saṃdṛśe tiṣṭhati rūpamasya na cakśuṣā paśyati kaścanainam |  hṛdā manīṣā manasābhiklṛpto ya etadviduramṛtāste bhavanti || 9 ||
units.translation: His form stands not within the fold of vision. None sees him with the eye. By the intellect controlling the mind, and by constant meditation is he revealed. Whoso knows that becomes immortal.

units.sanskrit: यदा पञ्चावतिष्ठन्ते ज्ञानानि मनसा सह ।  बुद्धिश्च न विचेष्टते तामाहुः परमां गतिम् ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: yadā pañcāvatiṣṭhante jñānāni manasā saha |  buddhiśca na viceṣṭate tāmāhuḥ paramāṃ gatim || 10 ||
units.translation: When the five organs of perception become still, together with the mind, and the intellect ceases to be active: that is called the highest state.

units.sanskrit: तां योगमिति मन्यन्ते स्थिरामिन्द्रियधारणाम् ।  अप्रमत्तस्तदा भवति योगो हि प्रभवाप्ययौ ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: tāṃ yogamiti manyante sthirāmindriyadhāraṇām |  apramattastadā bhavati yogo hi prabhavāpyayau || 11 ||
units.translation: This firm holding back of the senses is what is known as Yoga. Then one should become watchful, for Yoga comes and goes.

units.sanskrit: नैव वाचा न मनसा प्राप्तुं शक्यो न चक्शुषा ।  अस्तीति ब्रुवतोऽन्यत्र कथं तदुपलभ्यते ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: naiva vācā na manasā prāptuṃ śakyo na cakśuṣā |  astīti bruvato'nyatra kathaṃ tadupalabhyate || 12 ||
units.translation: Not by speech, not by mind, not by the eye, can he be attained; except in his case who says ‘He is,’ how can that be known.

units.sanskrit: अस्तीत्येवोपलब्धव्यस्तत्त्वभावेन चोभयोः ।  अस्तीत्येवोपलब्धस्य तत्त्वभावः प्रसीदति ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: astītyevopalabdhavyastattvabhāvena cobhayoḥ |  astītyevopalabdhasya tattvabhāvaḥ prasīdati || 13 ||
units.translation: He should be known to exist and also as he really is. Of these two, to him who knows him to exist, his real nature becomes revealed.

units.sanskrit: यदा सर्वे प्रमुच्यन्ते कामा येऽस्य हृदि श्रिताः ।  अथ मर्त्यो'मृतो भवत्यत्र ब्रह्म समश्नुते ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: yadā sarve pramucyante kāmā ye'sya hṛdi śritāḥ |  atha martyo'mṛto bhavatyatra brahma samaśnute || 14 ||
units.translation: When all desires clinging to the heart of one fall off, then the mortal becomes immortal and here attains *Brahman.*

units.sanskrit: यथा सर्वे प्रभिद्यन्ते हृदयस्येह ग्रन्थयः ।  अथ मर्त्यो'मृतो भवत्येतावद्ध्यनुशासनम् ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: yathā sarve prabhidyante hṛdayasyeha granthayaḥ |  atha martyo'mṛto bhavatyetāvaddhyanuśāsanam || 15 ||
units.translation: When here all the knots of the heart are sundered, then the mortal becomes immortal. Thus, much, the instruction.

units.sanskrit: शतं चैका च हृदयस्य नाड्यस्तासां मूर्धानमभिनिःसृतैका ।  तयोर्ध्वमायन्नमृतत्वमेति विष्वङ्ङन्या उत्क्रमणे भवन्ति ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: śataṃ caikā ca hṛdayasya nāḍyastāsāṃ mūrdhānamabhiniḥsṛtaikā |  tayordhvamāyannamṛtatvameti viṣvaṅṅanyā utkramaṇe bhavanti || 16 ||
units.translation: A hundred and one are the nerves of the heart. Of them, one has gone out piercing the head; going up through it, one attains immortality; others at the time of death lead different ways.

units.sanskrit: अङ्गुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषोऽन्तरात्मा सदा जनानां हृदये संनिविष्टः ।  तं स्वाच्छरीरात्प्रवृहेन्मुञ्जादिवेषीकां धैर्येण ।  तं विद्याच्छुक्रममृतं तं विद्याच्छुक्रममृतमिति ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: aṅguṣṭhamātraḥ puruṣo'ntarātmā sadā janānāṃ hṛdaye saṃniviṣṭaḥ |  taṃ svāccharīrātpravṛhenmuñjādiveṣīkāṃ dhairyeṇa |  taṃ vidyācchukramamṛtaṃ taṃ vidyācchukramamṛtamiti || 17 ||
units.translation: The *Purusha* of the size of a thumb, the internal *atman*, is always seated in the heart of all living creatures; one should draw him out from one’s own body boldly, as stalk from grass; one should know him as pure and immortal; one should know him as pure and immortal.

units.sanskrit: मृत्युप्रोक्तान्नचिकेतोऽथ लब्ध्वा विद्यामेतां योगविधिं च कृत्स्नम् ।  ब्रह्मप्राप्तो विरजोऽभूद्विमृत्युरन्योऽप्येवं यो विदध्यात्ममेव ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: mṛtyuproktānnaciketo'tha labdhvā vidyāmetāṃ yogavidhiṃ ca kṛtsnam |  brahmaprāpto virajo'bhūdvimṛtyuranyo'pyevaṃ yo vidadhyātmameva || 18 ||
units.translation: *Nachiketas* then having acquired this knowledge imparted by Death and also all the instruction about *yoga,* attained *Brahman*, having become free from taint and death; so does another also, who thus knows the nature of the *atman.*

units.sanskrit: सह नाववतु । सह नौ भुनक्तु । सह वीर्यं करवावहै ।  तेजस्विनावधीतमस्तु मा विद्विषावहै ॥ १९ ॥ ओं शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः ॥
units.transliteration: saha nāvavatu | saha nau bhunaktu | saha vīryaṃ karavāvahai |  tejasvināvadhītamastu mā vidviṣāvahai || 19 || oṃ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ ||
units.translation: Let Him protect us both; let us exert together may what we study be well studied; may we not hate. Om! Peace! Peace! Peace!

units.sanskrit: ओं सहनावितिशान्तिः । ओं उशन्ह वै वाजश्रवसः सर्ववेदसंददौ । तस्य ह नचिकेता नाम पुत्र आस ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: oṃ sahanāvitiśāntiḥ | oṃ uśanha vai vājaśravasaḥ sarvavedasaṃdadau | tasya ha naciketā nāma putra āsa || 1 ||
units.translation: From desire, as story tells, the son of Vajasrava made a gift of all his wealth; he had a son, so the story goes, by name Nachiketas.

units.sanskrit: तँ ह कुमारँ सन्तं दक्षिणासु नीयमानासु श्रद्धाऽऽविवेश सोऽमन्यत ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: tam̐ ha kumāram̐ santaṃ dakṣiṇāsu nīyamānāsu śraddhā''viveśa so'manyata || 2 ||
units.translation: Him, though young, zeal possessed when rewards were being distributed; he thought:

units.sanskrit: पीतोदका जग्धतृणा दुग्धदोहा निरिन्द्रियाः । अनन्दा नाम ते लोकास्तान्स गच्छति ता ददत् ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: pītodakā jagdhatṛṇā dugdhadohā nirindriyāḥ | anandā nāma te lokāstānsa gacchati tā dadat || 3 ||
units.translation: (These cows) have drunk water for the last time, eaten grass for the last time, have yielded all their milk and are devoid of vigour. Joyless verily are those worlds; them he attains who gives these.

units.sanskrit: स होवाच पितरं तत कस्मै मां दास्यसीति । द्वितीयं तृतीयं तँ होवाच मृत्यवे त्वा ददामीति ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: sa hovāca pitaraṃ tata kasmai māṃ dāsyasīti | dvitīyaṃ tṛtīyaṃ tam̐ hovāca mṛtyave tvā dadāmīti || 4 ||
units.translation: He said unto his sire, 'father, to whom wilt thou give me'; he said this again and for the third time. To him, he said 'unto Death do I give thee.'

units.sanskrit: बहूनामेमि प्रथमो बहूनामेमि मध्यमः । किँ स्विद्यमस्य कर्तव्यं यन्मयाद्य करिष्यति ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: bahūnāmemi prathamo bahūnāmemi madhyamaḥ | kim̐ svidyamasya kartavyaṃ yanmayādya kariṣyati || 5 ||
units.translation: Of many I go the first; of many I go midmost; what is there for Death to do which he can now do by me?

units.sanskrit: अनुपश्य यथा पूर्वे प्रतिपश्य तथापरे । सस्यमिव मर्त्यः पच्यते सस्यमिवाजायते पुनः ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: anupaśya yathā pūrve pratipaśya tathāpare | sasyamiva martyaḥ pacyate sasyamivājāyate punaḥ || 6 ||
units.translation: Call to mind how our ancestors behaved and mark also how others now behave; like corn, decays the mortal and like corn is born again.

metadata.macro_prakriyā: body-symbolism → prāṇa-relativization → rebirth-mechanism → witness-assertion → non-attachment-clarification → light-of-lights-culmination
metadata.macro_arc: body-as-city → prāṇa-subordination → rebirth-logic → inner-witness → universal-metaphors → light-of-lights

units.sanskrit: पुरमेकादशद्वारमजस्यावक्रचेतसः ।  अनुष्ठाय न शोचति विमुक्तश्च विमुच्यते । एतद्वैतत् ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: puramekādaśadvāramajasyāvakracetasaḥ |  anuṣṭhāya na śocati vimuktaśca vimucyate | etadvaitat || 1 ||
units.translation: The city of the unborn, whose knowledge is permanent, has eleven gates; thinking on him, one does not grieve and being freed, becomes free. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: हँसः शुचिषद्वसुरान्तरिक्शसद्धोता वेदिषदतिथिर्दुरोणसत् ।  नृषद्वरसदृतसद्व्योमसदब्जा गोजा ऋतजा अद्रिजा ऋतं बृहत् ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: ham̐saḥ śuciṣadvasurāntarikśasaddhotā vediṣadatithirduroṇasat ।  nṛṣadvarasadṛtasadvyomasadabjā gojā ṛtajā adrijā ṛtaṃ bṛhat ॥ 2 ॥
units.translation: As mover, he dwells in heaven; as pervader, in inter-space; as fire, in the altar; as guest, in a house; he dwells in man, dwells in betters, dwells in truth and dwells in the *akas.* He is all that is born in water, all that is born of earth, all that is born of sacrifices and all that is born of mountain; true and great.

units.sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं प्राणमुन्नयत्यपानं प्रत्यगस्यति ।  मध्ये वामनमासीनं विश्वे देवा उपासते ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: ūrdhvaṃ prāṇamunnayatyapānaṃ pratyagasyati |  madhye vāmanamāsīnaṃ viśve devā upāsate || 3 ||
units.translation: He leads the *prana* upwards and casts the *apana* downwards; the dwarf seated in the middle, all *Devas* worship.

units.sanskrit: अस्य विस्रंसमानस्य शरीरस्थस्य देहिनः ।  देहाद्विमुच्यमानस्य किमत्र परिशिष्यते । एतद्वैतत् ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: asya visraṃsamānasya śarīrasthasya dehinaḥ |  dehādvimucyamānasya kimatra pariśiṣyate | etadvaitat || 4 ||
units.translation: When this *atman* seated in the body escapes from the body what here remains? This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: न प्राणेन नापानेन मर्त्यो जीवति कश्चन ।  इतरेण तु जीवन्ति यस्मिन्नेतावुपाश्रितौ ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: na prāṇena nāpānena martyo jīvati kaścana |  itareṇa tu jīvanti yasminnetāvupāśritau || 5 ||
units.translation: Not by *prana,* not by *apana,* does any mortal live; but it is by some other on which these two depend that men live.

units.sanskrit: हन्त त इदं प्रवक्श्यामि गुह्यं ब्रह्म सनातनम् ।  यथा च मरणं प्राप्य आत्मा भवति गौतम ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: hanta ta idaṃ pravakśyāmi guhyaṃ brahma sanātanam |  yathā ca maraṇaṃ prāpya ātmā bhavati gautama || 6 ||
units.translation: To thee, Oh *Gautama*, I will explain the secret ancient *Brahman* and also how after death, the *atman* becomes.

units.sanskrit: योनिमन्ये प्रपद्यन्ते शरीरत्वाय देहिनः ।  स्थाणुमन्येऽनुसंयन्ति यथाकर्म यथाश्रुतम् ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: yonimanye prapadyante śarīratvāya dehinaḥ ।  sthāṇumanye'nusaṃyanti yathākarma yathāśrutam ॥ 7 ॥
units.translation: Some *jivas* (*dehinah*) go into wombs to be embodied; others pass into the immoveable, according to their *karma* and to their knowledge.

units.sanskrit: य एष सुप्तेषु जागर्ति कामं कामं पुरुषो निर्मिमाणः ।  तदेव शुक्रं तद्ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते ।  तस्मिंल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन । एतद्वैतत् ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: ya eṣa supteṣu jāgarti kāmaṃ kāmaṃ puruṣo nirmimāṇaḥ |  tadeva śukraṃ tadbrahma tadevāmṛtamucyate |  tasmiṃllokāḥ śritāḥ sarve tadu nātyeti kaścana | etadvaitat || 8 ||
units.translation: This *Purusha* who wakes when all sleep, creating what was desired is certainly ‘the pure,’ that is *Brahman* and that is said to be immortal. All worlds are fastened on That; none passes beyond That. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: अग्निर्यथैको भुवनं प्रविष्टो रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बभूव ।  एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बहिश्च ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: agniryathaiko bhuvanaṃ praviṣṭo rūpaṃ rūpaṃ pratirūpo babhūva |  ekastathā sarvabhūtāntarātmā rūpaṃ rūpaṃ pratirūpo bahiśca || 9 ||
units.translation: As fire, though one, having entered the world, takes a separate form in respect of every form, so does the internal *atman* of all living things assume a form for every form and is outside all forms.

units.sanskrit: वायुर्यथैको भुवनं प्रविष्टो रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बभूव ।  एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बहिश्च ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: vāyuryathaiko bhuvanaṃ praviṣṭo rūpaṃ rūpaṃ pratirūpo babhūva |  ekastathā sarvabhūtāntarātmā rūpaṃ rūpaṃ pratirūpo bahiśca || 10 ||
units.translation: As wind, though one, having entered the world, assumes forms responsive to every form, so the internal *atman* of all living things, though one, assumes forms responsive to every form and is outside them all.

units.sanskrit: सूर्यो यथा सर्वलोकस्य चक्शुर्न लिप्यते चाक्शुषैर्बाह्यदोषैः ।  एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा न लिप्यते लोकदुःखेन बाह्यः ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: sūryo yathā sarvalokasya cakśurna lipyate cākśuṣairbāhyadoṣaiḥ |  ekastathā sarvabhūtāntarātmā na lipyate lokaduḥkhena bāhyaḥ || 11 ||
units.translation: As the sun, the eye of all the world, is not tainted with the stains in external objects seen by the eyes, so, the one internal *atman* of all living things is not tainted with the world’s grief, being external to it.

units.sanskrit: एको वशी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा एकं रूपं बहुधा यः करोति ।  तमात्मस्थं ये'नुपश्यन्ति धीरास्तेषां सुखं शाश्वतं नेतरेषाम् ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: eko vaśī sarvabhūtāntarātmā ekaṃ rūpaṃ bahudhā yaḥ karoti |  tamātmasthaṃ ye'nupaśyanti dhīrāsteṣāṃ sukhaṃ śāśvataṃ netareṣām || 12 ||
units.translation: Sole, controller, the internal *atman* of all living things who makes his own form diverse to the intelligent who realizes him as seated in the self, eternal bliss is theirs, not others.

units.sanskrit: नित्योऽनित्यानां चेतनश्चेतनानामेको बहूनां यो विदधाति कामान् ।  तमात्मस्थं येऽनुपश्यन्ति धीरास्तेषां शान्तिः शाश्वतीनेतरेषाम् ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: nityo'nityānāṃ cetanaścetanānāmeko bahūnāṃ yo vidadhāti kāmān |  tamātmasthaṃ ye'nupaśyanti dhīrāsteṣāṃ śāntiḥ śāśvatīnetareṣām || 13 ||
units.translation: Eternal among the ephemeral, conscious among the conscious, who, being one, dispenses desired objects to many, the intelligent who see him seated in their selves, to them, eternal peace, not to others.

units.sanskrit: तदेतदिति मन्यन्तेऽनिर्देश्यं परमं सुखम् ।  कथं नु तद्विजानीयां किमु भाति विभाति वा ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: tadetaditi manyante'nirdeśyaṃ paramaṃ sukham |  kathaṃ nu tadvijānīyāṃ kimu bhāti vibhāti vā || 14 ||
units.translation: They think of that as this—the indescribable highest bliss. How shall I know that, whether that which shines is seen clearly or not.

units.sanskrit: न तत्र सूर्यो भाति न चन्द्रतारकं नेमा विद्युतो भान्ति कुतोऽयमग्निः ।  तमेव भान्तमनुभाति सर्वं तस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: na tatra sūryo bhāti na candratārakaṃ nemā vidyuto bhānti kuto'yamagniḥ |  tameva bhāntamanubhāti sarvaṃ tasya bhāsā sarvamidaṃ vibhāti || 15 ||
units.translation: The sun does not shine there; nor do the moon and the stars, nor do these lightnings shine. How could this fire? Him shining, all shine after. All this shines by his light.

units.sanskrit: श्रेयश्च प्रेयश्च मनुष्यमेतः तौ सम्परीत्य विविनक्ति धीरः । श्रेयः हि धीरः अभि प्रेयसो वृणीते प्रेयो मन्दो योगक्षेमात् वृणीते ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: śreyaś ca preyaś ca manuṣyam etas tau samparītya vivinakti dhīraḥ | śreyaḥ hi dhīraḥ abhi preyaso vṛṇīte preyo mando yogakṣemāt vṛṇīte || 1 ||
units.translation: Both the good (śreyaḥ) and the pleasant (preyaḥ) approach a person. The wise, having examined them, discriminates between the two. The wise chooses the good over the pleasant; the dull chooses the pleasant for the sake of body and sense preservation.

units.sanskrit: स त्वं प्रियां प्रियारूपांश्च कामान् अभिध्यायन् नचिकेतोऽत्यस्राक्षीः । नैतां सृङ्कां वित्तमयीमवाप्तो यस्यामज्जन्ति बहवो मनुष्याः ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: sa tvaṃ priyān priyarūpāṃś ca kāmān abhidhyāyan naciketo 'tyasrākṣīḥ | naitāṃ sṛṅkāṃ vittamayīm avāpto yasyāṃ majjanti bahavo manuṣyāḥ || 2 ||
units.translation: O Nachiketas, you have renounced the desirable and pleasant objects, and the chain of wealth, in which many men sink.

units.sanskrit: दूरमेते विपरीते विषूची अविद्या या च विद्येति ज्ञाता । विद्याभीप्सिनं नचिकेतसं मन्ये न त्वा कामा बहवोऽलोलुपन्त ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: dūram ete viparīte viṣūcī avidyā yā ca vidyeti jñātā | vidyābhīpsinaṃ naciketaṃ manye na tvā kāmā bahavo 'lolupanta || 3 ||
units.translation: Widely divergent and leading to different ends are what are known as ignorance and knowledge. I consider Nachiketas to be one who seeks knowledge; many desires have not tempted you.

units.sanskrit: अविद्यायामन्तरे वर्तमानाः स्वयं धीराः पण्डितं मन्यमानाः । दन्द्रम्यमाणाः परियन्ति मूढाः अन्धेनैव नीयमाना यथान्धाः ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: avidyāyām antare vartamānāḥ svayaṃ dhīrāḥ paṇḍitaṃ manyamānāḥ | dandramyamāṇāḥ pariyanti mūḍhāḥ andhenaiva nīyamānā yathāndhāḥ || 4 ||
units.translation: Living in ignorance, yet thinking themselves wise and learned, the deluded wander about, following crooked paths, like the blind led by the blind.

units.sanskrit: न साम्परायः प्रतिभाति बालं प्रमाद्यन्तं वित्तमोहेन मूढम् । अयं लोको नास्ति पर इति मानी पुनः पुनर्वशमापद्यते मे ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: na sāmparāyaḥ pratibhāti bālaṃ pramādyantaṃ vittamohena mūḍham | ayaṃ loko nāsti para iti mānī punaḥ punar vaśam āpadyate me || 5 ||
units.translation: The hereafter does not shine before the ignorant, deluded by the fascination of wealth. Thinking "This world alone exists," he falls again and again under my sway.

units.sanskrit: श्रवणायापि बहुभिर्यो न लभ्यः शृण्वन्तोऽपि बहवो यं न विद्युः । आश्चर्यो वक्ता कुशलोऽस्य लब्धा आश्चर्यो ज्ञाता कुशलानुशिष्टः ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: śravaṇāyāpi bahubhir yo na labhyaḥ śṛṇvanto 'pi bahavo yaṃ na vidyuḥ | āścaryo vaktā kuśalo 'sya labdhā āścaryo jñātā kuśalānuśiṣṭaḥ || 6 ||
units.translation: The Self is not easily heard of by many; even when heard of, many do not understand it. Wonderful is the teacher, and skillful the knower, when instructed by a competent teacher.

units.sanskrit: न नरेणावरेण प्रोक्त एष सुविज्ञेयो बहुधा चिन्त्यमानः । अनन्यप्रोक्ते गतिरत्र नास्ति अणीयान् ह्यतर्क्यमणुप्रमाणात् ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: na nareṇāvareṇa prokta eṣa suvijñeyo bahudhā cintyamānaḥ | ananyaprokte gatir atra nāsti aṇīyān hy atarkyam aṇupramāṇāt || 7 ||
units.translation: When taught by an inferior person, this Self is not well understood, since It is thought of in many ways. When taught by one who has realized It, there is no further doubt. It is subtler than the subtle and beyond reasoning.

units.sanskrit: नैषा तर्केण मतिरापनेया प्रोक्तान्येनैव सुज्ञानाय प्रेष्ठ । यं त्वमापः सत्यधृतिः बतेदृक् तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूं स्वाम् ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: naiṣā tarkeṇa matir āpaneyā proktānyenaiva sujñānāya preṣṭha | yaṃ tvam āpaḥ satyadhṛtiḥ bata edṛk tasya eṣa ātmā vivṛṇute tanūṃ svām || 8 ||
units.translation: This understanding is not attained through reasoning. It is taught by another who knows It. O beloved Nachiketas, since you are steadfast in truth, the Self reveals Its own nature to you.

units.sanskrit: नैषा तर्केण मतिरापनेया प्रोक्तान्येनैव सुज्ञानाय प्रेष्ठ ।  यान्त्वमापः सत्यधृतिर्बतासि त्वादृङ्नो भूयान्नचिकेतः प्रष्टा ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: naiṣā tarkeṇa matirāpaneyā proktānyenaiva sujñānāya preṣṭha |  yāntvamāpaḥ satyadhṛtirbatāsi tvādṛṅno bhūyānnaciketaḥ praṣṭā || 9 ||
units.translation: This idea cannot be reached by mere reasoning. This idea, Oh dearest, leads to sound knowledge, only if taught by another; thou hast reached it; Oh, thou art fixed in truth. May we find, Oh Nachiketas, a questioner like thee!

units.sanskrit: जानाम्यहँ शेवधिरित्यनित्यं न ह्यध्रुवैः प्राप्यते हि ध्रुवं तत् ।  ततो मया नाचिकेतश्चितो'ग्निरनित्यैर्द्रव्यैः प्राप्तवानस्मि नित्यम् ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: jānāmyaham̐ śevadhirityanityaṃ na hyadhruvaiḥ prāpyate hi dhruvaṃ tat ।  tato mayā nāciketaścito'gniranityairdravyaiḥ prāptavānasmi nityam ॥ 10 ॥
units.translation: I know that the treasure is uncertain; for, that which is constant is never reached by things which change. Therefore, has *Nachiketa* fire been propitiated by me with the perishable things, and I have attained the eternal.

units.sanskrit: कामस्याप्तिं जगतः प्रतिष्ठां क्रतोरानन्त्यमभयस्य पारं ।  स्तोममहदुरुगायं प्रतिष्ठां दृष्ट्वा धृत्या धीरो नचिकेतोऽत्यस्राक्शीः ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: kāmasyāptiṃ jagataḥ pratiṣṭhāṃ kratorānantyamabhayasya pāraṃ ।  stomamahadurugāyaṃ pratiṣṭhāṃ dṛṣṭvā dhṛtyā dhīro naciketo'tyasrākśīḥ ॥ 11 ॥
units.translation: The end of all desires, the stay of all the universe, the endless fruit of worship, the other shore of fearlessness, the praiseworthy, the great and boundless goal, all these hast thou beheld, and being intelligent, Oh *Nachiketas,* hast boldly rejected all.

units.sanskrit: तं दुर्दर्शं गूढमनुप्रविष्टं गुहाहितं गह्वरेष्ठं पुराणम् ।  अध्यात्मयोगाधिगमेन देवं मत्वा धीरो हर्षशोकौ जहाति ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: taṃ durdarśaṃ gūḍhamanupraviṣṭaṃ guhāhitaṃ gahvareṣṭhaṃ purāṇam |  adhyātmayogādhigamena devaṃ matvā dhīro harṣaśokau jahāti || 12 ||
units.translation: Contemplating with a concentrated mind, weaned from all external objects on the Ātman, ancient, hard to see, lodged in the inmost recess, located in intelligence, and seated amidst miserable surroundings, the intelligent man renounces joy and grief.

units.sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा संपरिगृह्य मर्त्यः प्रवृह्य धर्म्यमणुमेतमाप्य ।  स मोदते मोदनीयँ हि लब्ध्वा विवृतँ सद्म नचिकेतसं मन्ये ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: etacchrutvā saṃparigṛhya martyaḥ pravṛhya dharmyamaṇumetamāpya ।  sa modate modanīyam̐ hi labdhvā vivṛtam̐ sadma naciketasaṃ manye ॥ 13 ॥
units.translation: Having heard and well-grasped this, the mortal abstracting the virtuous Ātman, attaining this subtle Ātman, rejoices having obtained what causes joy. I think that the mansion is wide open for *Nachiketas*.

units.sanskrit: सर्वे वेदा यत्पदमामनन्ति तपाँ सि सर्वाणि च यद्वदन्ति ।  यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति तत्ते पद्ँ संग्रहेण ब्रवीम्योमित्येतत् ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: sarve vedā yatpadamāmananti tapām̐si sarvāṇi ca yadvadanti |  yadicchanto brahmacaryaṃ caranti tatte padm̐ saṃgraheṇa bravīmyomityetat || 14 ||
units.translation: What thou seest other than virtue and vice, other than what is made and what is not, other than the past and the future, tell me that.

units.sanskrit: एतद्ध्येवाक्शरं ब्रह्म एतद्ध्येवाक्शरं परम् ।  एतद्ध्येवाक्शरं ज्ञात्वा यो यदिच्छति तस्य तत् ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: etaddhyevākśaraṃ brahma etaddhyevākśaraṃ param |  etaddhyevākśaraṃ jñātvā yo yadicchati tasya tat || 15 ||
units.translation: The goal which all the *Vedas* uniformly extol, which all acts of *tapas* speak of, and wishing for which men lead the life of a *Brahmacharin*, that goal I tell you briefly—It is this—ĀŪṀ.

units.sanskrit: एतद्ध्येवाक्शरं ब्रह्म एतद्ध्येवाक्शरं परम् ।  एतद्ध्येवाक्शरं ज्ञात्वा यो यदिच्छति तस्य तत् ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: etaddhyevākśaraṃ brahma etaddhyevākśaraṃ param |  etaddhyevākśaraṃ jñātvā yo yadicchati tasya tat || 16 ||
units.translation: This word is, indeed, *Brahman,* this word is, indeed, the highest; whoso knows this word obtains, indeed, whatever he wishes for.

units.sanskrit: एतदालम्बनँ श्रेष्ठमेतदालम्बनं परं ।  एतदालम्बनं ज्ञात्वा ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: etadālambanam̐ śreṣṭhametadālambanaṃ paraṃ ।  etadālambanaṃ jñātvā brahmaloke mahīyate ॥ 17 ॥
units.translation: This letter (ĀŪṀ), indeed, is the (inferior)  Brahman (Hiranyagarbha); and this letter is, indeed, the supreme Brahman. Anybody, who, (while) meditating on this letter, wants any of the two, that comes to him.

units.sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा विपश्चिन्नायं कुतश्चिन्न बभूव कश्चित् ।  अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: na jāyate mriyate vā vipaścinnāyaṃ kutaścinna babhūva kaścit |  ajo nityaḥ śāśvato'yaṃ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre || 18 ||
units.translation: The intelligent Ātman is not born, nor does he die; he did not come from anywhere nor was he anything, unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient; he is not slain though the body is slain.

units.sanskrit: हन्ता चेन्मन्यते हन्तुँ हतश्चेन्मन्यते हतं ।  उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायँ हन्ति न हन्यते ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: hantā cenmanyate hantum̐ hataścenmanyate hataṃ ।  ubhau tau na vijānīto nāyam̐ hanti na hanyate ॥ 19 ॥
units.translation: The slayer who thinks of slaying this and the slain who thinks this slain, both these do not know. This slays not, nor is slain.

units.sanskrit: अणोरणीयान्महतो महीयानात्मास्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायां ।  तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको धातुः प्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: aṇoraṇīyānmahato mahīyānātmāsya jantornihito guhāyāṃ |  tamakratuḥ paśyati vītaśoko dhātuḥ prasādānmahimānamātmanaḥ || 20 ||
units.translation: Subtler than the subtle, greater than the great, in the heart of each living being, the Ātman reposes. One free from desire, with his mind and the senses composed, sees the glory of the Ātman and becomes absolved from grief.

units.sanskrit: आसीनो दूरं व्रजति शयानो याति सर्वतः ।  कस्तं मदामदं देवं मदन्यो ज्ञातुमर्हति ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: āsīno dūraṃ vrajati śayāno yāti sarvataḥ |  kastaṃ madāmadaṃ devaṃ madanyo jñātumarhati || 21 ||
units.translation: Sitting, he goes far; lying, he goes everywhere. Who else but me deserves to know the God, who is joyful and joyless.

units.sanskrit: अशरीरँ शरीरेष्वनवस्थेष्ववस्थितं ।  महान्तं विभुमात्मानं मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: aśarīram̐ śarīreṣvanavastheṣvavasthitaṃ |  mahāntaṃ vibhumātmānaṃ matvā dhīro na śocati || 22 ||
units.translation: The intelligent man knowing the Ātman, bodiless, seated firmly in perishable bodies, great and all-pervading, does not grieve.

units.sanskrit: नायमात्मा प्रवचनेन लभ्यो न मेधया न बहुना श्रुतेन ।  यमेवैष वृणुते तेन लभ्यस्तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूँ स्वाम् ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: nāyamātmā pravacanena labhyo na medhayā na bahunā śrutena |  yamevaiṣa vṛṇute tena labhyastasyaiṣa ātmā vivṛṇute tanūm̐ svām || 23 ||
units.translation: This Ātman is not to be attained by a study of the *Vedas,* nor by intelligence, nor by much hearing; but the Ātman can be attained only by him who seeks to know it. To him, this Ātman reveals its true nature.

units.sanskrit: नाविरतो दुश्चरितान्नाशान्तो नासमाहितः ।  नाशान्तमानसो वापि प्रज्ञानेनैनमाप्नुयात् ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: nāvirato duścaritānnāśānto nāsamāhitaḥ |  nāśāntamānaso vāpi prajñānenainamāpnuyāt || 24 ||
units.translation: None who has not turned away from bad conducts whose senses are not under control, whose mind is not collected, or whose mind is not at rest, can attain this Ātman by knowledge.

units.sanskrit: यस्य ब्रह्म च क्शत्रं च उभे भवत ओदनः ।  मृत्युर्यस्योपसेचनं क इत्था वेद यत्र सः ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: yasya brahma ca kśatraṃ ca ubhe bhavata odanaḥ |  mṛtyuryasyopasecanaṃ ka itthā veda yatra saḥ || 25 ||
units.translation: Of whom, the *Brahma* and the *Kshatriya* classes are the food, and Death but pickles (to supplement it); how can one thus know where that Ātman is.

units.sanskrit: पराञ्चि खानि व्यतृणत्स्वयम्भूस्तस्मात्पराङ्पश्यति नान्तरात्मन् ।  कश्चिद्धीरः प्रत्यगात्मानमैक्षदावृत्तचक्शुरमृतत्वमिच्छन् ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: parāñci khāni vyatṛṇatsvayambhūstasmātparāṅpaśyati nāntarātman |  kaściddhīraḥ pratyagātmānamaikṣadāvṛttacakśuramṛtatvamicchan || 1 ||
units.translation: The self-existent created the senses out-going: therefore, one sees outside and not the Ātman within. Some intelligent man, with his senses turned away, (from their object), desirous of immortality, sees the Ātman within.

units.sanskrit: पराचः कामाननुयन्ति बालास्ते मृत्योर्यन्ति विततस्य पाशं ।  अथ धीरा अमृतत्वं विदित्वा ध्रुवमध्रुवेष्विह न प्रार्थयन्ते ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: parācaḥ kāmānanuyanti bālāste mṛtyoryanti vitatasya pāśaṃ |  atha dhīrā amṛtatvaṃ viditvā dhruvamadhruveṣviha na prārthayante || 2 ||
units.translation: The ignorant pursue external objects of desire; they get into the meshes of widespread death: but the intelligent, knowing sure immortality, do not covet the uncertain things here.

units.sanskrit: येन रूपं रसं गन्धं शब्दान्स्पर्शाँश्च मैथुनान् ।  एतेनैव विजानाति किमत्र परिशिष्यते । एतद्वैतत् ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: yena rūpaṃ rasaṃ gandhaṃ śabdānsparśām̐śca maithunān |  etenaiva vijānāti kimatra pariśiṣyate | etadvaitat || 3 ||
units.translation: By which alone, one knows form, taste, smell, sounds, touch and the pleasures of the sexes; what remains here unknown to that. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: स्वप्नान्तं जागरितान्तं चोभौ येनानुपश्यति । महान्तं विभुमात्मानं मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: svapnāntaṃ jāgaritāntaṃ cobhau yenānupaśyati । mahāntaṃ vibhumātmānaṃ matvā dhīro na śocati ॥ 4 ॥
units.translation: That by which a mortal perceives, both in dream and in waking, by knowing that great all-pervading Ātman the wise man grieves no more.

units.sanskrit: य इमं मध्वदं वेद आत्मानं जीवमन्तिकात् । ईशानं भूतभव्यस्य न ततो विजुगुप्सते । एतद्वै तत् ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: ya imaṃ madhvadaṃ veda ātmānaṃ jīvamantikāt |  īśānaṃ bhūtabhavyasya na tato vijugupsate | etadvai tat || 5 ||
units.translation: He who knows this Ātman, the honey-eater (perceiver and enjoyer of objects), ever near, as the lord of the past and future, fears no more. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: यः पूर्वं तपसो जातमद्भ्यः पूर्वमजायत ।  गुहां प्रविश्य तिष्ठन्तं यो भूतेभिर्व्यपश्यत । एतद्वै तत् ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: yaḥ pūrvaṃ tapaso jātamadbhyaḥ pūrvamajāyata |  guhāṃ praviśya tiṣṭhantaṃ yo bhūtebhirvyapaśyata | etadvai tat || 6 ||
units.translation: Who sees him seated within the five elements—him who was born of tapas (Brahman) who was born before the waters and who having entered the cavity of the heart, is therein seated. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: या प्राणेन संभवत्यदितिर्देवतामयी ।  गुहां प्रविश्य तिष्ठन्तीं या भूतेभिर्व्यजायत । एतद्वैतत् ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: yā prāṇena saṃbhavatyaditirdevatāmayī |  guhāṃ praviśya tiṣṭhantīṃ yā bhūtebhirvyajāyata | etadvaitat || 7 ||
units.translation: Who is born along with *prapa* manifested as all *Devas,* the eater, seated, having entered the heart, who was born with the elements. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: अरण्योर्निहितो जातवेदा गर्भ इव सुभृतो गर्भिणीभिः ।  दिवे दिवे ईड्यो जागृवद्भिर्हविष्मद्भिर्मनुष्येभिरग्निः । एतद्वैतत् ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: araṇyornihito jātavedā garbha iva subhṛto garbhiṇībhiḥ |  dive dive īḍyo jāgṛvadbhirhaviṣmadbhirmanuṣyebhiragniḥ | etadvaitat || 8 ||
units.translation: The fire lodged in the araṇis, as the foetus is well-borne by the pregnant woman fit to be worshipped every day by watchful offerers and other men. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: यतश्चोदेति सूर्योऽस्तं यत्र च गच्छति ।  तं देवाःसर्वे अर्पितास्तदु नात्येति कश्चन । एतद्वैतत् ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: yataścodeti sūryo'staṃ yatra ca gacchati |  taṃ devāḥsarve arpitāstadu nātyeti kaścana | etadvaitat || 9 ||
units.translation: Whence also the sun rises and where he sets, on that, all the Devas depend. None certainly passes beyond that. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: यदेवेह तदमुत्र यदमुत्र तदन्विह ।  मृत्योः स मृत्युमाप्नोति य इह नानेव पश्यति ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: yadeveha tadamutra yadamutra tadanviha |  mṛtyoḥ sa mṛtyumāpnoti ya iha nāneva paśyati || 10 ||
units.translation: What indeed is here, is there; what there, that here again; from Death to Death he goes; who here sees, as if different.

units.sanskrit: मनसैवेदमाप्तव्यन्नेह नानास्ति किंचन ।  मृत्योः स मृत्युं गच्छति य इह नानेव पश्यति ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: manasaivedamāptavyanneha nānāsti kiṃcana |  mṛtyoḥ sa mṛtyuṃ gacchati ya iha nāneva paśyati || 11 ||
units.translation: By mind alone could this be obtained, there is no difference here at all. He goes from death to death who sees as if there were difference here.

units.sanskrit: अङ्गुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषो मध्य आत्मनि तिष्ठति । ईशानं भूतभव्यस्य न ततो विजुगुप्सते । एतद्वै तत् ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: aṅguṣṭhamātraḥ puruṣo madhya ātmani tiṣṭhati | īśānaṃ bhūtabhavyasya na tato vijugupsate | etadvai tat || 12 ||
units.translation: The Puruṣa (Self), of the size of a thumb, resides in the middle of the body as the lord of the past and the future, (he who knows Him) fears no more. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: अङ्गुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषो ज्योतिरिवाधूमकः । ईशानो भूतभव्यस्य स एवाद्य स उ श्वः । एतद्वै तत् ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: aṅguṣṭhamātraḥ puruṣo jyotirivādhūmakaḥ | īśāno bhūtabhavyasya sa evādya sa u śvaḥ | etadvai tat || 13 ||
units.translation: That Puruṣa, of the size of a thumb, is like a light without smoke, lord of the past and the future. He is the same today and tomorrow. This verily is That.

units.sanskrit: यथोदकं दुर्गे वृष्टं पर्वतेषु विधावति । एवं धर्मान् पृथक् पश्यंस्तानेवानुविधावति ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: yathodakaṃ durge vṛṣṭaṃ parvateṣu vidhāvati | evaṃ dharmān pṛthak paśyaṃstānevānuvidhāvati || 14 ||
units.translation: As rainwater, (falling) on the mountain top, runs down over the rocks on all sides; similarly, he who sees difference (between visible forms) runs after them in various directions.

units.sanskrit: यथोदकं शुद्धे शुद्धमासिक्तं तादृगेव भवति । एवं मुनेर्विजानत आत्मा भवति गौतम ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: yathodakaṃ śuddhe śuddhamāsiktaṃ tādṛgeva bhavati । evaṃ munervijānata ātmā bhavati gautama ॥ 15 ॥
units.translation: As water pure poured into pure becomes the same only, so the Ātman of the thinker who knows thus, becomes; Oh *Gautama*.

units.sanskrit: ऋतं पिबन्तौ सुकृतस्य लोके गुहां प्रविष्टौ परमे परार्धे ।  छायातपौ ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति पञ्चाग्नयो ये च त्रिणाचिकेताः ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: ṛtaṃ pibantau sukṛtasya loke guhāṃ praviṣṭau parame parārdhe |  chāyātapau brahmavido vadanti pañcāgnayo ye ca triṇāciketāḥ || 1 ||
units.translation: The two, who enjoy the fruits of their good deeds, being lodged in the cavity of the seat of the supreme, the knowers of *Brahman* call shadow and light, as also those who maintain five fires and have thrice propitiated the *Nachiketa* fire.

units.sanskrit: यः सेतुरीजानानामक्शरं ब्रह्म यत्परम् ।  अभयं तितीर्षतां पारं नाचिकेतँ शकेमहि ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: yaḥ seturījānānāmakśaraṃ brahma yatparam |  abhayaṃ titīrṣatāṃ pāraṃ nāciketam̐ śakemahi || 2 ||
units.translation: We are able to know the fire which is the bridge of those who perform sacrifices, and also the highest immortal *Brahman*, fearless, and the other shore for those, who wish to cross the ocean of *Samsara.*

units.sanskrit: आत्मानँ रथितं विद्धि शरीरँ रथमेव तु ।  बुद्धिं तु सारथिं विद्धि मनः प्रग्रहमेव च ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: ātmānam̐ rathitaṃ viddhi śarīram̐ rathameva tu |  buddhiṃ tu sārathiṃ viddhi manaḥ pragrahameva ca || 3 ||
units.translation: Know the Ātman as the lord of the chariot, the body as only the chariot, know also intelligence as the driver; know the mind as the reins.

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि हयानाहुर्विषयाँ स्तेषु गोचरान् ।  आत्मेन्द्रियमनोयुक्तं भोक्तेत्याहुर्मनीषिणः ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: indriyāṇi hayānāhurviṣayām̐ steṣu gocarān ।  ātmendriyamanoyuktaṃ bhoktetyāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
units.translation: The senses, they say, are the horses; the objects which they perceive, the way; the Ātman, the senses and the mind combined, the intelligent call the enjoyer.

units.sanskrit: यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्ययुक्तेन मनसा सदा ।  तस्येन्द्रियाण्यवश्यानि दुष्टाश्वा इव सारथेः ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: yastvavijñānavānbhavatyayuktena manasā sadā |  tasyendriyāṇyavaśyāni duṣṭāśvā iva sāratheḥ || 5 ||
units.translation: But of him who is not possessed of discrimination, and whose mind is always uncontrolled, the senses are not controllable as vicious horses of a driver.

units.sanskrit: यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति युक्तेन मनसा सदा ।  तस्येन्द्रियाणि वश्यानि सदश्वा इव सारथेः ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: yastu vijñānavānbhavati yuktena manasā sadā |  tasyendriyāṇi vaśyāni sadaśvā iva sāratheḥ || 6 ||
units.translation: But of him who knows and has a mind always controlled, the senses are always controllable as the good horses of the driver.

units.sanskrit: यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्यमनस्कः सदाऽशुचिः ।  न स तत्पदमाप्नोति सँ सारं चाधिगच्छति ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: yastvavijñānavānbhavatyamanaskaḥ sadā'śuciḥ ।  na sa tatpadamāpnoti sam̐ sāraṃ cādhigacchati ॥ 7 ॥
units.translation: But he, whose intellect has no discrimination and whose thind is not under control and who is always unclean, does not reach that goal and falls into *Samsara.*

units.sanskrit: यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति समनस्कः सदा शुचिः ।  स तु तत्पदमाप्नोति यस्माद्भूयो न जायते ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: yastu vijñānavānbhavati samanaskaḥ sadā śuciḥ |  sa tu tatpadamāpnoti yasmādbhūyo na jāyate || 8 ||
units.translation: But he, who knows, who has his mind always under control and who is clean, reaches that goal from which he is not born again.

units.sanskrit: विज्ञानसारथिर्यस्तु मनःप्रग्रहवान्नरः ।  सोऽध्वनः पारमाप्नोति तद्विष्णोः परमं पदम् ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: vijñānasārathiryastu manaḥpragrahavānnaraḥ |  so'dhvanaḥ pāramāpnoti tadviṣṇoḥ paramaṃ padam || 9 ||
units.translation: But the man who has a discriminating intellect for the driver and a controlling mind for the reins, reaches the end of the road, *i.e.,* that highest place of Vishnu.

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियेभ्यः परा ह्यर्था अर्थेभ्यश्च परं मनः ।  मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्बुद्धेरात्मा महान्परः ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: indriyebhyaḥ parā hyarthā arthebhyaśca paraṃ manaḥ |  manasastu parā buddhirbuddherātmā mahānparaḥ || 10 ||
units.translation: Beyond the senses, are the rudiments of its objects; beyond these rudiments is the mind; beyond the mind is Ātman known as Māhāt (great).

units.sanskrit: महतः परमव्यक्तमव्यक्तात्पुरुषः परः । पुरुषान्न परं किंचित्सा काष्ठा सा परा गतिः ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: mahataḥ paramavyaktamavyaktātpuruṣaḥ paraḥ | puruṣānna paraṃ kiṃcitsā kāṣṭhā sā parā gatiḥ || 11 ||
units.translation: Beyond the great Ātman is the Unmanifested; beyond the Unmanifested is the Puruṣa (the Cosmic Soul); beyond the Puruṣa there is nothing. That is the end that is the final goal.

units.sanskrit: एष सर्वेषु भूतेषु गूढोऽऽत्मा न प्रकाशते ।  दृश्यते त्वग्र्यया बुद्ध्या सूक्श्मया सूक्श्मदर्शिभिः ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: eṣa sarveṣu bhūteṣu gūḍho''tmā na prakāśate |  dṛśyate tvagryayā buddhyā sūkśmayā sūkśmadarśibhiḥ || 12 ||
units.translation: This Ātman (Self), hidden in all beings, does not shine forth; but It is seen by subtle seers through keen and subtle understanding.

units.sanskrit: यच्छेद्वाङ्मनसी प्राज्ञस्तद्यच्छेज्ज्ञान आत्मनि ।  ज्ञानमात्मनि महति नियच्छेत्तद्यच्छेच्छान्त आत्मनि ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: yacchedvāṅmanasī prājñastadyacchejjñāna ātmani |  jñānamātmani mahati niyacchettadyacchecchānta ātmani || 13 ||
units.translation: Let the intelligent man sink speech into mind, sink that into intelligence and intelligence into the great ātman, and sink that into the peaceful ātman.

units.sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत ।  क्शुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: uttiṣṭhata jāgrata prāpya varānnibodhata |  kśurasya dhārā niśitā duratyayā durgaṃ pathastatkavayo vadanti || 14 ||
units.translation: Arise, awake; having reached the great, learn; the edge of a razor is sharp and impassable; that path, the intelligent say, is hard to go by.

units.sanskrit: अशब्दमस्पर्शमरूपमव्ययं तथाऽरसन्नित्यमगन्धवच्च यत् ।  अनाद्यनन्तं महतः परं ध्रुवं निचाय्य तन्मृत्युमुखात्प्रमुच्यते ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: aśabdamasparśamarūpamavyayaṃ tathā'rasannityamagandhavacca yat |  anādyanantaṃ mahataḥ paraṃ dhruvaṃ nicāyya tanmṛtyumukhātpramucyate || 15 ||
units.translation: Which is soundless, touchless, formless, undecaying, so tasteless, eternal and scentless, beginningless, endless, beyond the *Mahat*, and constant, knowing that, man escapes from the mouth of Death.

units.sanskrit: नाचिकेतमुपाख्यानं मृत्युप्रोक्तँ सनातनम् ।  उक्त्वा श्रुत्वा च मेधावी ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: nāciketamupākhyānaṃ mṛtyuproktam̐ sanātanam |  uktvā śrutvā ca medhāvī brahmaloke mahīyate || 16 ||
units.translation: Hearing and repeating the old Nachiketa’s story told by Death, the intelligent man attains glory in the world of Brahman.

units.sanskrit: य इमं परमं गुह्यं श्रावयेद्ब्रह्मसंसदि ।  प्रयतः श्राद्धकाले वा तदानन्त्याय कल्पते तदानन्त्याय कल्पत इति ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: ya imaṃ paramaṃ guhyaṃ śrāvayedbrahmasaṃsadi |  prayataḥ śrāddhakāle vā tadānantyāya kalpate tadānantyāya kalpata iti || 17 ||
units.translation: Whoever with zeal, causes to be recited before an assembly of brāhmaṇas or at the time of śraddhā of the ancestors, this highest secret, that secures immortality, secures immortality.

metadata.macro_arc: oṁ-totality → avasthā-analysis → causal-integration → negation → symbolic-collapse → silence

units.sanskrit: हरिः ओम् । ओमित्येतदक्षरमिदं सर्वं तस्योपव्याख्यानं भूतं भवद्भविष्यदिति सर्वमोङ्कार एव | यच्चान्यत्त्रिकालातीतं तदप्योङ्कार एव ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: hariḥ oṃ | om ity etad akṣaram idaṃ sarvaṃ tasyopavyākhyānaṃ bhūtaṃ bhavadbhaviṣyaditi sarvamoṅkāra eva  | yaccānyattrikālātītaṃ tadapyoṅkāra eva || 1 ||
units.translation: Hariḥ Aum. *Aum*, the word, is all this. A clear explanation of it (is the following). All that is past, present and future is verily *Aum*. That which is beyond the triple conception of time, is also truly *Aum*.

units.sanskrit: सर्वं ह्येतद् ब्रह्मायमात्मा ब्रह्म सोऽयमात्मा चतुष्पात् ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: sarvaṃ hyetad brahmāyam ātmā brahma so 'yamātmā catuṣpāt || 2 ||
units.translation: All this is verily Brahman. This *ātman* is Brahman. This *ātman* has four quarters.

units.sanskrit: जागरितस्थानो बहिष्प्रज्ञः सप्ताङ्ग एकोनविंशतिमुखः स्थूलभुग्वैश्वानरः प्रथमः पादः ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: jāgarita-sthānaḥ bahiḥ-prajñaḥ saptāṅga ekonaviṃśatimukhaḥ sthūlabhugvaiśvānaraḥ prathamaḥ pādaḥ || 3 ||
units.translation: The first quarter (pāda) is Vaiśvānara, whose sphere is the waking state, who is conscious of external objects, who has seven limbs and nineteen mouths, and whose experience consists of gross objects.

units.sanskrit: स्वप्नस्थानोऽन्तः प्रज्ञाः सप्ताङ्ग एकोनविंशतिमुखः प्रविविक्तभुक्तैजसो द्वितीयः पादः ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: svapna-sthānaḥ antaḥ-prajñaḥ saptāṅga ekonaviṃśati-mukhaḥ praviviktabhuk taijasaḥ dvitīyaḥ pādaḥ || 4 ||
units.translation: The second quarter (pāda) is Taijasa, whose sphere is the dream state, who is conscious of internal objects, who has seven limbs and nineteen mouths, and who experiences subtle objects.

units.sanskrit: यत्र सुप्तो न कञ्चन कामं कामयते न कञ्चन स्वप्नं पश्यति तत्सुषुप्तम् । सुषुप्तस्थान एकीभूतः प्रज्ञानघन एवानन्दमयो ह्यानन्दभुक् चेतोमुखः प्राज्ञस्तृतीयः पादः ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: yatra suptaḥ na kañcana kāmaṁ kāmayate na kañcana svapnaṁ paśyati tat suṣuptam | suṣupta-sthānaḥ ekībhūtaḥ prajñāna-ghana eva ānanda-mayaḥ hi ānanda-bhuk ceto-mukhaḥ prājñaḥ tṛtīyaḥ pādaḥ || 5 ||
units.translation: That is the state of deep sleep, wherein the sleeper does not desire any objects nor see any dream. The third quarter (pāda) is Prājña, whose sphere is deep sleep, in whom all experiences become undifferentiated, who is a mass of consciousness, who is full of bliss and who experiences bliss, and who is the gateway to the knowledge of the other states.

units.sanskrit: एष सर्वेश्वरः एष सर्वज्ञ एषोऽन्तर्याम्येष योनिः सर्वस्य प्रभवाप्ययौ हि भूतानाम् ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: eṣa sarveśvaraḥ eṣa sarvajñaḥ eṣaḥ antaryāmī eṣa yoniḥ sarvasya prabhava-apyayau hi bhūtānām || 6 ||
units.translation: This is the Lord of all; this is the knower of all; this is the inner controller; this is the source of all; and this is that from which all beings arise and into which they dissolve.

units.sanskrit: नान्तःप्रज्ञं न बहिःप्रज्ञं नोभयतःप्रज्ञं न प्रज्ञानघनं न प्रज्ञं नाप्रज्ञम् । अदृश्यमव्यवहार्यमग्राह्यमलक्षणमचिन्त्यमव्यपदेश्यमेकात्मप्रत्ययसारं प्रपञ्चोपशमं शान्तं शिवमद्वैतं चतुर्थं मन्यन्ते स आत्मा स विज्ञेयः ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: na antaḥ-prajñam na bahiḥ-prajñam na ubhayataḥ-prajñam na prajñāna-ghanam na prajñam na aprajñam | adṛśyam avyavahāryam agrāhyam alakṣaṇam acintyam avyapadeśyam eka-ātma-pratyaya-sāram prapañca-upaśamam śāntam śivam advaitam caturtham manyante sa ātma sa vijñeyaḥ || 7 ||
units.translation: Turīya is not that which is conscious of the internal world, nor that which is conscious of the external world, nor that which is conscious of both, nor that which is a mass of consciousness, nor that which is simple consciousness, nor that which is insentient. It is unseen, beyond empirical dealings, incomprehensible, not graspable, without characteristics, unthinkable, indescribable, essentially of the nature of the Self alone, the cessation of all phenomena, peaceful, auspicious, and non-dual. This is known as the fourth (turīya). This is the ātman and it is to be realized.

units.sanskrit: सोऽयमात्माध्यक्षरमोङ्कारोऽधिमात्रं पादा मात्रा मात्राश्च पादा अकार उकारो मकार इति ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: saḥ ayam ātmā adhyakṣaram oṅkāraḥ adhīmātram pādā mātrā mātrāḥ ca pādāḥ akāra ukāraḥ makāra iti || 8 ||
units.translation: This very ātman is also Oṁ from the standpoint of syllables. The quarters are the letters, and the letters are the quarters. The letters are A, U, and M.

units.sanskrit: जागरितस्थानो वैश्वानरोऽकारः प्रथमा मात्राऽऽप्तेरादिमत्त्वाद्वाप्नोति ह वै सर्वान्कामानादिश्च भवति य एवं वेद ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: jāgarita-sthānaḥ vaiśvānaraḥ akāraḥ prathamā mātrā āpteḥ ādimattvāt vā āpnoti ha vai sarvān kāmān ādiś ca bhavati yaḥ evaṁ veda || 9 ||
units.translation: Vaiśvānara, whose sphere is the waking state, is A, the first letter of Oṁ, because of pervasiveness and primacy. One who knows this attains all desires and becomes foremost.

units.sanskrit: स्वप्नस्थानस्तैजस उकारो द्वितीया मात्रोत्कर्षादुभयत्वाद्वोत्कर्षति ह वै ज्ञानसन्ततिं समानश्च भवति नास्याब्रह्मवित्कुले भवति य एवं वेद ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: svapna-sthānaḥ taijasaḥ ukāraḥ dvitīyā mātrā utkarṣāt ubhayatvāt vā utkarṣati ha vai jñāna-santatim samānaḥ ca bhavati na asya abrahmavit kule bhavati yaḥ evaṁ veda || 10 ||
units.translation: Taijasa, whose sphere is the dream state, is U, the second letter of Oṁ, because of superiority and intermediate position. One who knows this attains a continuity of knowledge, becomes equal to all, and no one in his lineage remains ignorant of Brahman.

units.sanskrit: सुषुप्तस्थानः प्राज्ञो मकारस्तृतीया मात्रा मितेरपीतेर्वा मिनोति ह वा इदं सर्वमपीतिश्च भवति य एवं वेद ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: suṣupta-sthānaḥ prājñaḥ makāraḥ tṛtīyā mātrā miteḥ apīteḥ vā minoti ha vā idaṁ sarvam apītiḥ ca bhavati yaḥ evaṁ veda || 11 ||
units.translation: Prājña, whose sphere is deep sleep, is M, the third letter of Oṁ, because it is the measure and that in which all dissolves. One who knows this measures all and comprehends all within oneself.

units.sanskrit: अमात्रश्चतुर्थोऽव्यवहार्यः प्रपञ्चोपशमः शिवोऽद्वैत एवमोङ्कार आत्मैव संविशत्यात्मनाऽऽत्मानं य एवं वेद ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: amātraḥ caturthaḥ avyavahāryaḥ prapañca-upaśamaḥ śivaḥ advaitaḥ evam oṅkāraḥ ātma eva saṁviśati ātmanā ātmānam yaḥ evaṁ veda || 12 ||
units.translation: That which is without parts, beyond empirical dealings, the cessation of all phenomena, peaceful, auspicious, and non-dual—this is Oṁ and this is the ātman. One who knows this merges the self into the Self.

metadata.title: Chāndogya Upaniṣad — Chapter 6
metadata.structure_note: Dialogue between Uddālaka Āruṇi and Śvetaketu in which a sequence of analogies culminates in repeated revelation of the identity expressed in the mahāvākya "tat tvam asi".

units.sanskrit: श्वेतकेतुर्हारुणेय आस तं ह पितोवाच श्वेतकेतो वस ब्रह्मचर्यं  न वै सोम्यास्मत्कुलीनोऽननूच्य ब्रह्मबन्धुरिव भवतीति ॥ ६.१.१ ॥
units.transliteration: śvetaketurhāruṇeya āsa taṃ ha pitovāca śvetaketo vasa brahmacaryaṃ  na vai somyāsmatkulīno'nanūcya brahmabandhuriva bhavatīti || 6.1.1 ||
units.translation: Āruṇi had a son named Śvetaketu. Once Āruṇi told him: ‘Śvetaketu, you should now live as a brahmacārin. No one in our family has not studied the scriptures and has not been a good brāhmin.’

units.sanskrit: स ह द्वादशवर्ष उपेत्य चतुर्विंशतिवर्षः सर्वान्वेदानधीत्य  महामना अनूचानमानी स्तब्ध एयाय तंह पितोवाच ॥ ६.१.२ ॥
units.transliteration: sa ha dvādaśavarṣa upetya caturviṃśativarṣaḥ sarvānvedānadhītya  mahāmanā anūcānamānī stabdha eyāya taṃha pitovāca || 6.1.2 ||
units.translation: Śvetaketu went to his teacher’s house at the age of twelve. After studying all the Vedas, he returned home when he was twenty-four, having become very serious and vain, and thinking himself to be a great scholar. [Noticing this,] his father said to him: ‘O Śvetaketu, you have now become very serious and vain, and you think you are a great scholar. But did you ask your teacher for that teaching [about Brahman]?

units.sanskrit: श्वेतकेतो यन्नु सोम्येदं महामना अनूचानमानी स्तब्धोऽस्युत  तमादेशमप्राक्ष्यः येनाश्रुतं श्रुतं भवत्यमतं मतमविज्ञातं  विज्ञातमिति कथं नु भगवः स आदेशो भवतीति ॥ ६.१.३ ॥
units.transliteration: śvetaketo yannu somyedaṃ mahāmanā anūcānamānī stabdho'syuta tamādeśamaprākṣyaḥ yenāśrutaṃ śrutaṃ bhavatyamataṃ matamavijñātaṃ vijñātamiti kathaṃ nu bhagavaḥ sa ādeśo bhavatīti || 6.1.3 ||
units.translation: ‘—that teaching by which what is never heard becomes heard, what is never thought of becomes thought of, what is never known becomes known?’ [Śvetaketu asked,] ‘Sir, what is that teaching?’

units.sanskrit: यथा सोम्यैकेन मृत्पिण्डेन सर्वं मृन्मयं विज्ञातं  स्याद्वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं मृत्तिकेत्येव सत्यम् ॥ ६.१.४ ॥
units.transliteration: yathā somyaikena mṛtpiṇḍena sarvaṃ mṛnmayaṃ vijñātaṃ  syādvācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ mṛttiketyeva satyam || 6.1.4 ||
units.translation: ‘O Somya, it is like this: By knowing a single lump of earth you know all objects made of earth. All changes are mere words, in name only. But earth is the reality.

units.sanskrit: यथा सोम्यैकेन लोहमणिना सर्वं लोहमयं विज्ञातं  स्याद्वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं लोहमित्येव सत्यम् ॥ ६.१.५ ॥
units.transliteration: yathā somyaikena lohamaṇinā sarvaṃ lohamayaṃ vijñātaṃ  syādvācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ lohamityeva satyam || 6.1.5 ||
units.translation: ‘O Somya, it is like this: By knowing a single lump of gold you know all objects made of gold. All changes are mere words, in name only. But gold is the reality.

units.sanskrit: यथा सोम्यिकेन नखनिकृन्तनेन सर्वं कार्ष्णायसं विज्ञातं  स्याद्वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं कृष्णायसमित्येव  सत्यमेवंसोम्य स आदेशो भवतीति ॥ ६.१.६ ॥
units.transliteration: yathā somyikena nakhanikṛntanena sarvaṃ kārṣṇāyasaṃ vijñātaṃ syādvācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ kṛṣṇāyasamityeva  satyamevaṃsomya sa ādeśo bhavatīti || 6.1.6 ||
units.translation: ‘O Somya, it is like this: By knowing a single nail-cutter you know all objects made of iron. All changes are mere words, in name only. But iron is the reality. O Somya, this is the teaching I spoke of.’

units.sanskrit: न वै नूनं भगवन्तस्त एतदवेदिषुर्यद्ध्येतदवेदिष्यन्कथं मे  नावक्ष्यन्निति भगवांस्त्वेव मे तद्ब्रवीत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ६.१.७ ॥
units.transliteration: na vai nūnaṃ bhagavantasta etadavediṣuryaddhyetadavediṣyankathaṃ me nāvakṣyanniti bhagavāṃstveva me tadbravītviti tathā somyeti hovāca || 6.1.7 ||
units.translation: [Śvetaketu said:] ‘Surely my revered teachers did not know this truth. If they knew it, why should they not have told me? So please explain it to me, sir.’ His father said, ‘Let it be so, my son.’

units.sanskrit: सदेव सोम्येदमग्र आसीदेकमेवाद्वितीयम् ।  तद्धैक आहुरसदेवेदमग्र आसीदेकमेवाद्वितीयं तस्मादसतः सज्जायत ॥ ६.२.१ ॥
units.transliteration: sadeva somyedamagra āsīdekamevādvitīyam |  taddhaika āhurasadevedamagra āsīdekamevādvitīyaṃ tasmādasataḥ sajjāyata || 6.2.1 ||
units.translation: ‘Somya, before this world was manifest there was only existence, one without a second. On this subject, some maintain that before this world was manifest there was only non-existence, one without a second. Out of that non-existence, existence emerged.’

units.sanskrit: कुतस्तु खलु सोम्यैवंस्यादिति होवाच कथमसतः सज्जायेतेति ।  सत्त्वेव सोम्येदमग्र आसीदेकमेवाद्वितीयम् ॥ ६.२.२ ॥
units.transliteration: kutastu khalu somyaivaṃsyāditi hovāca kathamasataḥ sajjāyeteti |  sattveva somyedamagra āsīdekamevādvitīyam || 6.2.2 ||
units.translation: The father said: ‘O Somya, what proof is there for this—that from nothing something has emerged? Rather, before this world came into being, O Somya, there was only existence, one without a second.’

units.sanskrit: तदैक्षत बहु स्यां प्रजायेयेति तत्तेजोऽसृजत तत्तेज  ऐक्षत बहु स्यां प्रजायेयेति तदपोऽसृजत ।  तस्माद्यत्र क्वच शोचति स्वेदते वा  पुरुषस्तेजस एव तदध्यापो जायन्ते ॥ ६.२.३ ॥
units.transliteration: tadaikṣata bahu syāṃ prajāyeyeti tattejo'sṛjata tatteja  aikṣata bahu syāṃ prajāyeyeti tadapo'sṛjata |  tasmādyatra kvaca śocati svedate vā  puruṣastejasa eva tadadhyāpo jāyante || 6.2.3 ||
units.translation: That Existence decided: ‘I shall be many. I shall be born.’ He then created fire. That fire also decided: ‘I shall be many. I shall be born.’ Then fire produced water. That is why whenever or wherever a person mourns or perspires, he produces water.

units.sanskrit: ता आप ऐक्षन्त बह्व्यः स्याम प्रजायेमहीति ता  अन्नमसृजन्त तस्माद्यत्र क्व च वर्षति तदेव भूयिष्ठमन्नं  भवत्यद्भ्य एव तदध्यन्नाद्यं जायते ॥ ६.२.४ ॥
units.transliteration: tā āpa aikṣanta bahvyaḥ syāma prajāyemahīti tā  annamasṛjanta tasmādyatra kva ca varṣati tadeva bhūyiṣṭhamannaṃ bhavatyadbhya eva tadadhyannādyaṃ jāyate || 6.2.4 ||
units.translation: That water decided: ‘I shall be many; I shall be born.’ That water then created food. This is why whenever and wherever there is rain, at once food grows in great abundance. It is from water that food is produced.

units.sanskrit: तेषां खल्वेषां भूतानां त्रीण्येव बीजानि  भवन्त्याण्डजं जीवजमुद्भिज्जमिति ॥ ६.३.१ ॥
units.transliteration: teṣāṃ khalveṣāṃ bhūtānāṃ trīṇyeva bījāni  bhavantyāṇḍajaṃ jīvajamudbhijjamiti || 6.3.1 ||
units.translation: Living beings have their origin in three ways: from eggs, from parents, and from plants.

units.sanskrit: सेयं देवतैक्षत हन्ताहमिमास्तिस्रो देवता अनेन  जीवेनात्मनानुप्रविश्य नामरूपे व्याकरवाणीति ॥ ६.३.२ ॥
units.transliteration: seyaṃ devataikṣata hantāhamimāstisro devatā anena  jīvenātmanānupraviśya nāmarūpe vyākaravāṇīti || 6.3.2 ||
units.translation: That god [Existence] decided: ‘Entering into these three deities [fire, water, and earth], as the individual self, I shall manifest myself in many names and forms’.

units.sanskrit: तासां त्रिवृतं त्रिवृतमेकैकां करवाणीति सेयं देवतेमास्तिस्रो  देवता अनेनैव जीवेनात्मनानुप्रविश्य नामरूपे व्याकरोत् ॥ ६.३.३ ॥
units.transliteration: tāsāṃ trivṛtaṃ trivṛtamekaikāṃ karavāṇīti seyaṃ devatemāstisro  devatā anenaiva jīvenātmanānupraviśya nāmarūpe vyākarot || 6.3.3 ||
units.translation: Sat [Existence] thought, ‘I shall divide each of these three deities threefold.’ Then, having entered into these three deities as the individual self, he manifested himself as names and forms.

units.sanskrit: तासां त्रिवृतं त्रिवृतमेकैकामकरोद्यथा तु खलु सोम्येमास्तिस्रो  देवतास्त्रिवृत्त्रिवृदेकैका भवति तन्मे विजानीहीति ॥ ६.३.४ ॥
units.transliteration: tāsāṃ trivṛtaṃ trivṛtamekaikāmakarodyathā tu khalu somyemāstisro devatāstrivṛttrivṛdekaikā bhavati tanme vijānīhīti || 6.3.4 ||
units.translation: [Having so decided,] he made each of these three elements threefold. But as to how each of these three deities becomes threefold, O Somya, learn this from me.

units.sanskrit: यदग्ने रोहितंरूपं तेजसस्तद्रूपं यच्छुक्लं तदपां  यत्कृष्णं तदन्नस्यापागादग्नेरग्नित्वं वाचारम्भणं  विकारो नामधेयं त्रीणि रूपाणीत्येव सत्यम् ॥ ६.४.१ ॥
units.transliteration: yadagne rohitaṃrūpaṃ tejasastadrūpaṃ yacchuklaṃ tadapāṃ  yatkṛṣṇaṃ tadannasyāpāgādagneragnitvaṃ vācārambhaṇaṃ  vikāro nāmadheyaṃ trīṇi rūpāṇītyeva satyam || 6.4.1 ||
units.translation: The red colour of gross fire is from subtle fire, the white colour is from subtle water, and the dark colour is from subtle earth. Thus that which constitutes the ‘fire-ness’ of fire is gone. All changes are mere words, in name only [i.e., fire is only a name indicating a certain condition]. The three colours are the reality.

units.sanskrit: यदादित्यस्य रोहितंरूपं तेजसस्तद्रूपं यच्छुक्लं तदपां  यत्कृष्णं तदन्नस्यापागादादित्यादादित्यत्वं वाचारम्भणं  विकारो नामधेयं त्रीणि रूपाणीत्येव सत्यम् ॥ ६.४.२ ॥
units.transliteration: yadādityasya rohitaṃrūpaṃ tejasastadrūpaṃ yacchuklaṃ tadapāṃ  yatkṛṣṇaṃ tadannasyāpāgādādityādādityatvaṃ vācārambhaṇaṃ  vikāro nāmadheyaṃ trīṇi rūpāṇītyeva satyam || 6.4.2 ||
units.translation: The red colour of the sun is from fire, the white colour is from water, and the dark colour is from earth. Thus that which constitutes the ‘sun-ness’ of the sun is gone. All changes are mere words, in name only. The three colours are the reality.

units.sanskrit: यच्छन्द्रमसो रोहितंरूपं तेजसस्तद्रूपं यच्छुक्लं  तदपां यत्कृष्णं तदन्नस्यापागाच्चन्द्राच्चन्द्रत्वं वाचारम्भणं  विकारो नामधेयं त्रीणि रूपाणीत्येव सत्यम् ॥ ६.४.३ ॥
units.transliteration: yacchandramaso rohitaṃrūpaṃ tejasastadrūpaṃ yacchuklaṃ  tadapāṃ yatkṛṣṇaṃ tadannasyāpāgāccandrāccandratvaṃ vācārambhaṇaṃ  vikāro nāmadheyaṃ trīṇi rūpāṇītyeva satyam || 6.4.3 ||
units.translation: The red colour of the moon is from fire, the white colour is from water, and the dark colour is from earth. Thus that which constitutes the ‘moon-ness’ of the moon is gone. All changes are mere words, in name only. The three colours are the reality.

units.sanskrit: यद्विद्युतो रोहितंरूपं तेजसस्तद्रूपं यच्छुक्लं  तदपां यत्कृष्णं तदन्नस्यापागाद्विद्युतो विद्युत्त्वं  वाचारम्भणं विकारो नामधेयं त्रीणि रूपाणीत्येव सत्यम् ॥ ६.४.४ ॥
units.transliteration: yadvidyuto rohitaṃrūpaṃ tejasastadrūpaṃ yacchuklaṃ  tadapāṃ yatkṛṣṇaṃ tadannasyāpāgādvidyuto vidyuttvaṃ  vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ trīṇi rūpāṇītyeva satyam || 6.4.4 ||
units.translation: The red colour of lightning is from fire, the white colour is from water, and the dark colour is from earth. Thus that which constitutes the ‘lightning-ness’ of lightning is gone. All changes are mere words, in name only. The three colours are the reality.

units.sanskrit: एतद्ध स्म वै तद्विद्वांस आहुः पूर्वे महाशाला महाश्रोत्रिया न नोऽद्य  कश्चनाश्रुतममतमविज्ञातमुदाहरिष्यतीति ह्येभ्यो विदांचक्रुः ॥ ६.४.५ ॥
units.transliteration: etaddha sma vai tadvidvāṃsa āhuḥ pūrve mahāśālā mahāśrotriyā na no'dya kaścanāśrutamamatamavijñātamudāhariṣyatīti hyebhyo vidāṃcakruḥ || 6.4.5 ||
units.translation: The earlier great householders, who were well-read in the Vedas, knew this. They said, ‘There is nothing anyone can mention that is not heard of or thought of or already known to us.’ This is because they came to know about the three colours.

units.sanskrit: यदु रोहितमिवाभूदिति तेजसस्तद्रूपमिति तद्विदांचक्रुर्यदु  शुक्लमिवाभूदित्यपांरूपमिति तद्विदांचक्रुर्यदु  कृष्णमिवाभूदित्यन्नस्य रूपमिति तद्विदांचक्रुः ॥ ६.४.६ ॥
units.transliteration: yadu rohitamivābhūditi tejasastadrūpamiti tadvidāṃcakruryadu śuklamivābhūdityapāṃrūpamiti tadvidāṃcakruryadu  kṛṣṇamivābhūdityannasya rūpamiti tadvidāṃcakruḥ || 6.4.6 ||
units.translation: They knew that whatever else was seen as red was the colour of fire; whatever else was seen as white was the colour of water; and whatever else was seen as dark was the colour of earth.

units.sanskrit: यद्वविज्ञातमिवाभूदित्येतासामेव देवतानांसमास इति त द्विदांचक्रुर्यथा तु खलु सोम्येमास्तिस्रो देवताः  पुरुषं प्राप्य त्रिवृत्त्रिवृदेकैका भवति तन्मे विजानीहीति ॥ ६.४.७ ॥
units.transliteration: yadvavijñātamivābhūdityetāsāmeva devatānāṃsamāsa iti  tadvidāṃcakruryathā tu khalu somyemāstisro devatāḥ  puruṣaṃ prāpya trivṛttrivṛdekaikā bhavati tanme vijānīhīti || 6.4.7 ||
units.translation: And whatever else was not properly known they understood was the combination of those three deities [fire, water, and earth]. O Somya, now learn from me how these three deities enter into a person and become threefold.

units.sanskrit: अन्नमशितं त्रेधा विधीयते तस्य यः स्थविष्ठो धातुस्तत्पुरीषं  भवति यो मध्यमस्तन्मांसं योऽणिष्ठस्तन्मनः ॥ ६.५.१ ॥
units.transliteration: annamaśitaṃ tredhā vidhīyate tasya yaḥ sthaviṣṭho dhātustatpurīṣaṃ  bhavati yo madhyamastanmāṃsaṃ yo'ṇiṣṭhastanmanaḥ || 6.5.1 ||
units.translation: When we eat food, it divides itself into three parts. The grossest part of it becomes excreta; that which is less gross becomes our flesh; and the finest part becomes our mind.

units.sanskrit: आपः पीतास्त्रेधा विधीयन्ते तासां यः स्थविष्ठो धातुस्तन्मूत्रं  भवति यो मध्यमस्तल्लोहितं योऽणिष्ठः स प्राणः ॥ ६.५.२ ॥
units.transliteration: āpaḥ pītāstredhā vidhīyante tāsāṃ yaḥ sthaviṣṭho dhātustanmūtraṃ  bhavati yo madhyamastallohitaṃ yo'ṇiṣṭhaḥ sa prāṇaḥ || 6.5.2 ||
units.translation: When we drink water, it becomes divided in three parts. The grossest part of it becomes urine; that which is less gross becomes blood; and the finest part becomes prāṇa, the vital force.

units.sanskrit: तेजोऽशितं त्रेधा विधीयते तस्य यः स्थविष्ठो धातुस्तदस्थि  भवति यो मध्यमः स मज्जा योऽणिष्ठः सा वाक् ॥ ६.५.३ ॥
units.transliteration: tejo'śitaṃ tredhā vidhīyate tasya yaḥ sthaviṣṭho dhātustadasthi  bhavati yo madhyamaḥ sa majjā yo'ṇiṣṭhaḥ sā vāk || 6.5.3 ||
units.translation: When we eat fire [i.e., butter, oil, etc.], it divides itself into three parts. The grossest part of it becomes bone; that which is less gross becomes marrow; and the subtlest part becomes speech.

units.sanskrit: अन्नमयंहि सोम्य मनः आपोमयः प्राणस्तेजोमयी वागिति  भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ६.५.४ ॥
units.transliteration: annamayaṃhi somya manaḥ āpomayaḥ prāṇastejomayī vāgiti  bhūya eva mā bhagavānvijñāpayatviti tathā somyeti hovāca || 6.5.4 ||
units.translation: ‘O Somya, the mind is nourished by food, prāṇa by water, and speech by fire.’ [Śvetaketu then said,] ‘Sir, will you please explain this to me again?’ ‘Yes, Somya, I will explain again,’ replied his father.

units.sanskrit: दध्नः सोम्य मथ्यमानस्य योऽणिमा स  उर्ध्वः समुदीषति तत्सर्पिर्भवति ॥ ६.६.१ ॥
units.transliteration: dadhnaḥ somya mathyamānasya yo'ṇimā sa  urdhvaḥ samudīṣati tatsarpirbhavati || 6.6.1 ||
units.translation: When curd is churned, O Somya, the finest part of it rises to the surface. That becomes butter.

units.sanskrit: एवमेव खलु सोम्यान्नस्याश्यमानस्य योऽणिमा स उर्ध्वः  समुदीषति तन्मनो भवति ॥ ६.६.२ ॥
units.transliteration: evameva khalu somyānnasyāśyamānasya yo'ṇimā sa urdhvaḥ  samudīṣati tanmano bhavati || 6.6.2 ||
units.translation: O Somya, in the same way, the finest part of the food that is eaten rises to the surface and becomes the mind.

units.sanskrit: अपांसोम्य पीयमानानां योऽणिमा स उर्ध्वः  समुदीषति सा प्राणो भवति ॥ ६.६.३ ॥
units.transliteration: apāṃsomya pīyamānānāṃ yo'ṇimā sa urdhvaḥ  samudīṣati sā prāṇo bhavati || 6.6.3 ||
units.translation: O Somya, the finest part of water that is drunk rises to the surface and becomes our prāṇa.

units.sanskrit: तेजसः सोम्याश्यमानस्य योऽणिमा स उर्ध्वः समुदीषति सा वाग्भवति ॥ ६.६.४ ॥
units.transliteration: tejasaḥ somyāśyamānasya yo'ṇimā sa urdhvaḥ samudīṣati sā vāgbhavati || 6.6.4 ||
units.translation: O Somya, the finest part of fire [butter, etc.] that is eaten rises to the surface and becomes our speech.

units.sanskrit: अन्नमयं हि सोम्य मन आपोमयः प्राणस्तेजोमयी वागिति  भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ६.६.५ ॥
units.transliteration: annamayaṃ hi somya mana āpomayaḥ prāṇastejomayī vāgiti  bhūya eva mā bhagavānvijñāpayatviti tathā somyeti hovāca || 6.6.5 ||
units.translation: ‘O Somya, the mind is certainly nourished by food, pṛāṇa by water, and speech by fire.’ [Śvetaketu then said,] ‘Sir, will you please explain this to me again?’ ‘Yes, I will explain again, O Somya,’ replied his father.

units.sanskrit: षोडशकलः सोम्य पुरुषः पञ्चदशाहानि माशीः काममपः  पिबापोमयः प्राणो नपिबतो विच्छेत्स्यत इति ॥ ६.७.१ ॥
units.transliteration: ṣoḍaśakalaḥ somya puruṣaḥ pañcadaśāhāni māśīḥ kāmamapaḥ  pibāpomayaḥ prāṇo napibato vicchetsyata iti || 6.7.1 ||
units.translation: O Somya, a person has sixteen parts, [and all your sixteen parts are intact]. Do not eat anything for fifteen days, but drink as much water as you like. Life is dependent on water. If you do not drink water, you will lose your life.

units.sanskrit: स ह पञ्चदशाहानि नशाथ हैनमुपससाद किं ब्रवीमि भो इत्यृचः  सोम्य यजूंषि सामानीति स होवाच न वै मा प्रतिभान्ति भो इति ॥ ६.७.२ ॥
units.transliteration: sa ha pañcadaśāhāni naśātha hainamupasasāda kiṃ bravīmi bho ityṛcaḥ  somya yajūṃṣi sāmānīti sa hovāca na vai mā pratibhānti bho iti || 6.7.2 ||
units.translation: Śvetaketu did not eat anything for fifteen days. After that he came to his father and said, ‘O Father, what shall I recite?’ His father said, ‘Recite the Ṛk, Yajuḥ , and Sāma mantras.’ Śvetaketu replied, ‘I can’t recall any of them, Sir.’

units.sanskrit: तं होवाच यथा सोम्य महतोऽभ्या हितस्यैकोऽङ्गारः खद्योतमात्रः  परिशिष्टः स्यात्तेन ततोऽपि न बहु दहेदेवंसोम्य ते षोडशानां कलानामेका  कलातिशिष्टा स्यात्तयैतर्हि वेदान्नानुभवस्यशानाथ मे विज्ञास्यसीति ॥ ६.७.३ ॥
units.transliteration: taṃ hovāca yathā somya mahato'bhyā hitasyaiko'ṅgāraḥ khadyotamātraḥ pariśiṣṭaḥ syāttena tato'pi na bahu dahedevaṃsomya te ṣoḍaśānāṃ kalānāmekā kalātiśiṣṭā syāttayaitarhi vedānnānubhavasyaśānātha me vijñāsyasīti || 6.7.3 ||
units.translation: The father said to Śvetaketu: ‘O Somya, from a blazing fire, if there is but a small piece of ember left, the size of a firefly, it cannot bum anything bigger than that. Similarly, O Somya, because only one small part of your sixteen parts remains, you cannot remember the Vedas. Eat something and then you will understand what I am saying.’

units.sanskrit: स हशाथ हैनमुपससाद तं ह यत्किंच पप्रच्छ सर्वंह प्रतिपेदे ॥ ६.७.४ ॥
units.transliteration: sa haśātha hainamupasasāda taṃ ha yatkiṃca papraccha sarvaṃha pratipede || 6.7.4 ||
units.translation: Śvetaketu ate something and then went to his father. Whatever his father asked him, he was able to follow.

units.sanskrit: तं होवाच यथा सोम्य महतोऽभ्याहितस्यैकमङ्गारं खद्योतमात्रं  परिशिष्टं तं तृणैरुपसमाधाय प्राज्वलयेत्तेन ततोऽपि बहु दहेत् ॥ ६.७.५ ॥
units.transliteration: taṃ hovāca yathā somya mahato'bhyāhitasyaikamaṅgāraṃ khadyotamātraṃ pariśiṣṭaṃ taṃ tṛṇairupasamādhāya prājvalayettena tato'pi bahu dahet || 6.7.5 ||
units.translation: The father said to him: ‘O Somya, from a blazing fire, if there is but a small piece of ember left, the size of a firefly, the fire can again blaze up when you add some grass. The fire, in fact, can then blaze up even more than it did before.’

units.sanskrit: एवं सोम्य ते षोडशानां कलानामेका कलातिशिष्टाभूत्सान्नेनोपसमाहिता  प्राज्वाली तयैतर्हि वेदाननुभवस्यन्नमयंहि सोम्य मन आपोमयः  प्राणस्तेजोमयी वागिति तद्धास्य विजज्ञाविति विजज्ञाविति ॥ ६.७.६ ॥
units.transliteration: evaṃ somya te ṣoḍaśānāṃ kalānāmekā kalātiśiṣṭābhūtsānnenopasamāhitā  prājvālī tayaitarhi vedānanubhavasyannamayaṃhi somya mana āpomayaḥ prāṇastejomayī vāgiti taddhāsya vijajñāviti vijajñāviti || 6.7.6 ||
units.translation: ‘In the same way, O Somya, of your sixteen parts, only one remained. But that, when nourished by food, has revived, and by that you are now able to follow the Vedas. O Somya, this is why I said that the mind was nourished by food, prāṇa was nourished by water, and speech was nourished by fire.’ Śvetaketu now understood what his father was saying.

units.sanskrit: उद्दालको हारुणिः श्वेतकेतुं पुत्रमुवाच स्वप्नान्तं मे सोम्य विजानीहीति  यत्रैतत्पुरुषः स्वपिति नाम सता सोम्य तदा सम्पन्नो भवति स्वमपीतो  भवति तस्मादेनं स्वपितीत्याचक्षते स्वंह्यपीतो भवति ॥ ६.८.१ ॥
units.transliteration: uddālako hāruṇiḥ śvetaketuṃ putramuvāca svapnāntaṃ me somya vijānīhīti yatraitatpuruṣaḥ svapiti nāma satā somya tadā sampanno bhavati svamapīto bhavati tasmādenaṃ svapitītyācakṣate svaṃhyapīto bhavati || 6.8.1 ||
units.translation: Uddālaka Āruṇi said to his son Śvetaketu: ‘O Somya, let me explain to you the concept of deep sleep. When a person is said to be sleeping, O Somya, he becomes one with Sat [Existence], and he attains his real Self. That is why people say about him, “He is sleeping,” because he is then in his Self.’

units.sanskrit: स यथा शकुनिः सूत्रेण प्रबद्धो दिशं दिशं पतित्वान्यत्रायतनमलब्ध्वा  बन्धनमेवोपश्रयत एवमेव खलु सोम्य तन्मनो दिशं दिशं  पतित्वान्यत्रायतनमलब्ध्वा प्राणमेवोपश्रयते प्राणबन्धनं हि सोम्य मन इति ॥ ६.८.२ ॥
units.transliteration: sa yathā śakuniḥ sūtreṇa prabaddho diśaṃ diśaṃ patitvānyatrāyatanamalabdhvā bandhanamevopaśrayata evameva khalu somya tanmano diśaṃ diśaṃ patitvānyatrāyatanamalabdhvā prāṇamevopaśrayate prāṇabandhanaṃ hi somya mana iti || 6.8.2 ||
units.translation: Just as a bird tied to a rope flutters here and there, and when it cannot get any shelter anywhere, it surrenders itself to its bondage; in the same way, O Somya, the mind runs in every direction, and when it fails to get a resting place anywhere, it surrenders itself to prāṇa, the vital force. The mind, O Somya, is tied to prāṇa.

units.sanskrit: अशनापिपासे मे सोम्य विजानीहीति यत्रैतत्पुरुषोऽशिशिषति  नामाप एव तदशितं नयन्ते तद्यथा गोनायोऽश्वनायः पुरुषनाय इत्येवं तदप आचक्षतेऽशनायेति तत्रितच्छुङ्गमुत्पतितं सोम्य विजानीहि नेदममूलं भविष्यतीति ॥ ६.८.३ ॥
units.transliteration: aśanāpipāse me somya vijānīhīti yatraitatpuruṣo'śiśiṣati nāmāpa eva tadaśitaṃ nayante tadyathā gonāyo'śvanāyaḥ puruṣanāya ityevaṃ tadapa ācakṣate'śanāyeti tatritacchuṅgamutpatitaṃ somya vijānīhi nedamamūlaṃ bhaviṣyatīti || 6.8.3 ||
units.translation: O Somya, now learn from me about hunger and thirst. When a person is said to be hungry, it is to be understood that the food he ate has been worn away by water. Just as people refer to a leader of cows, or a leader of horses, or a leader of people, similarly, people say that water is the leader of food. So also, O Somya, know that this sprout [i.e., the body] is the product of something [i.e., of food and drink]. It cannot be without a root.

units.sanskrit: तस्य क्व मूलं स्यादन्यत्रान्नादेवमेव खलु सोम्यान्नेन शुङ्गेनापो  मूलमन्विच्छाद्भिः सोम्य शुङ्गेन तेजो मूलमन्विच्छ तेजसा सोम्य शुङ्गेन  सन्मूलमन्विच्छ सन्मूलाः सोम्येमाः सर्वाः प्रजाः सदायतनाः सत्प्रतिष्ठाः ॥ ६.८.४ ॥
units.transliteration: tasya kva mūlaṃ syādanyatrānnādevameva khalu somyānnena śuṅgenāpo mūlamanvicchādbhiḥ somya śuṅgena tejo mūlamanviccha tejasā somya śuṅgena sanmūlamanviccha sanmūlāḥ somyemāḥ sarvāḥ prajāḥ sadāyatanāḥ satpratiṣṭhāḥ || 6.8.4 ||
units.translation: Where else, except in food, can the body have its root? In the same way, O Somya, when food is the sprout, search for water as the root; when water is the sprout, O Somya, search for fire as the root; when fire is the sprout, O Somya, search for Sat [Existence] as the root. O Somya, Sat is the root, Sat is the abode, and Sat is the support of all these beings.

units.sanskrit: अथ यत्रैतत्पुरुषः पिपासति नाम तेज एव तत्पीतं नयते तद्यथा  गोनायोऽश्वनायः पुरुषनाय इत्येवं तत्तेज आचष्ट उदन्येति तत्रैतदेव  शुङ्गमुत्पतितं सोम्य विजानीहि नेदममूलं भविष्यतीति ॥ ६.८.५ ॥
units.transliteration: atha yatraitatpuruṣaḥ pipāsati nāma teja eva tatpītaṃ nayate tadyathā gonāyo'śvanāyaḥ puruṣanāya ityevaṃ tatteja ācaṣṭa udanyeti tatraitadeva śuṅgamutpatitaṃ somya vijānīhi nedamamūlaṃ bhaviṣyatīti || 6.8.5 ||
units.translation: Then when a person is said to be thirsty, it is to be understood that the water he drank has been carried away by fire. Just as people refer to a leader of cows, or a leader of horses, or a leader of people, similarly, people say that fire is the leader of water. So also, O Somya, know that this sprout [i.e., the body] is the product of something [i.e., of food and drink]. It cannot be without a root.

units.sanskrit: तस्य क्व मूलं स्यादन्यत्राद्भ्य्ऽद्भिः सोम्य शुङ्गेन तेजो मूलमन्विच्छ  तेजसा सोम्य शुङ्गेन सन्मूलमन्विच्छ सन्मूलाः सोम्येमाः सर्वाः प्रजाः  सदायतनाः सत्प्रतिष्ठा यथा तु खलु सोम्येमास्तिस्रो देवताः पुरुषं प्राप्य  त्रिवृत्त्रिवृदेकैका भवति तदुक्तं पुरस्तादेव भवत्यस्य सोम्य पुरुषस्य  प्रयतो वाङ्मनसि सम्पद्यते मनः प्राणे प्राणस्तेजसि तेजः परस्यां देवतायाम् ॥ ६.८.६ ॥
units.transliteration: tasya kva mūlaṃ syādanyatrādbhy'dbhiḥ somya śuṅgena tejo mūlamanviccha tejasā somya śuṅgena sanmūlamanviccha sanmūlāḥ somyemāḥ sarvāḥ prajāḥ sadāyatanāḥ satpratiṣṭhā yathā tu khalu somyemāstisro devatāḥ puruṣaṃ prāpya trivṛttrivṛdekaikā bhavati taduktaṃ purastādeva bhavatyasya somya puruṣasya prayato vāṅmanasi sampadyate manaḥ prāṇe prāṇastejasi tejaḥ parasyāṃ devatāyām || 6.8.6 ||
units.translation: Where else, except in water, can the body have its root? O Somya, when water is the sprout, search for fire as the root; when fire is the sprout, O Somya, search for Sat [Existence] as the root. O Somya, Sat is the root, Sat is the abode, and Sat is the support of all these beings. As to how, O Somya, these three deities [fire, water, and earth] enter a body and each becomes threefold, this has already been explained. O Somya, as this person is dying, his speech merges into the mind, his mind into prāṇa, his prāṇa into fire, and then fire merges into Brahman, the Supreme Deity.

units.sanskrit: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि  श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ६.८.७ ॥
units.transliteration: sa ya eṣo'ṇimaitadātmyamidaṃ sarvaṃ tatsatyaṃ sa ātmā tattvamasi  śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavānvijñāpayatviti tathā somyeti hovāca || 6.8.7 ||
units.translation: ‘That which is the subtlest of all is the Self of all this. It is the Truth. It is the Self. That thou art, O Śvetaketu.’ [Śvetaketu then said,] ‘Sir, please explain this to me again.’ ‘Yes, Somya, I will explain again,’ replied his father.

units.sanskrit: यथा सोम्य मधु मधुकृतो निस्तिष्ठन्ति नानात्ययानां वृक्षाणांरसान्समवहारमेकतांरसं गमयन्ति ॥ ६.९.१ ॥
units.transliteration: yathā somya madhu madhukṛto nistiṣṭhanti nānātyayānāṃ vṛkṣāṇāṃrasānsamavahāramekatāṃrasaṃ gamayanti || 6.9.1 ||
units.translation: O Somya, as bees produce honey by collecting the juice from various trees and mixing them together to make one juice—

units.sanskrit: ते यथा तत्र न विवेकं लभन्तेऽमुष्याहं वृक्षस्य रसोऽस्म्यमुष्याहं  वृक्षस्य रसोऽस्मीत्येवमेव खलु सोम्येमाः सर्वाः प्रजाः सति सम्पद्य  न विदुः सति सम्पद्यामह इति ॥ ६.९.२ ॥
units.transliteration: te yathā tatra na vivekaṃ labhante'muṣyāhaṃ vṛkṣasya raso'smyamuṣyāhaṃ vṛkṣasya raso'smītyevameva khalu somyemāḥ sarvāḥ prajāḥ sati sampadya  na viduḥ sati sampadyāmaha iti || 6.9.2 ||
units.translation: —O Somya, and just as those juices now are no longer conscious of their separate identities, thinking, ‘I am the juice from such-and-such tree,’ and ‘I am the juice from such-and-such tree’; similarly, when all these beings attain unity in the Self, they are not conscious of it. They do not think, ‘We [were once separate, but] now we are all one with the Self’.

units.sanskrit: त इह व्यघ्रो वा सिंहो वा वृको वा वराहो वा कीटो वा पतङ्गो  वा दंशो वा मशको वा यद्यद्भवन्ति तदाभवन्ति ॥ ६.९.३ ॥
units.transliteration: ta iha vyaghro vā siṃho vā vṛko vā varāho vā kīṭo vā pataṅgo  vā daṃśo vā maśako vā yadyadbhavanti tadābhavanti || 6.9.3 ||
units.translation: Whatever they were before in this world—whether a tiger or lion or leopard or boar or bug or insect or flea or mosquito—they are born again. [They never know that they came from Sat].

units.sanskrit: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि  श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ६.९.४ ॥
units.transliteration: sa ya eṣo'ṇimaitadātmyamidaṃ sarvaṃ tatsatyaṃ sa ātmā tattvamasi  śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavānvijñāpayatviti tathā somyeti hovāca || 6.9.4 ||
units.translation: ‘That which is the subtlest of all is the Self of all this. It is the Truth. It is the Self. That thou art, O Śvetaketu.’ [Śvetaketu then said,] ‘Sir, please explain this to me again.’ ‘Yes, Somya, I will explain it again,’ replied his father.

units.sanskrit: इमाः सोम्य नद्यः पुरस्तात्प्राच्यः स्यन्दन्ते पश्चात्प्रतीच्यस्ताः  समुद्रात्समुद्रमेवापियन्ति स समुद्र एव भवति ता यथा तत्र  न विदुरियमहमस्मीयमहमस्मीति ॥ ६.१०.१ ॥
units.transliteration: imāḥ somya nadyaḥ purastātprācyaḥ syandante paścātpratīcyastāḥ samudrātsamudramevāpiyanti sa samudra eva bhavati tā yathā tatra  na viduriyamahamasmīyamahamasmīti || 6.10.1 ||
units.translation: O Somya, those rivers belonging to the east run to the east, and those belonging to the west run to the west. Rising from the sea, they go back to it and become one with it. Just as, when they reach the sea, they do not know their separate identities—‘I am this river,’ or ‘I am that river’—

units.sanskrit: एवमेव खलु सोम्येमाः सर्वाः प्रजाः सत आगम्य न विदुः सत  आगच्छामह इति त इह व्याघ्रो वा सिंहो वा वृको वा वराहो वा कीटो वा  पतङ्गो वा दंशो वा मशको वा यद्यद्भवन्ति तदाभवन्ति ॥ ६.१०.२ ॥
units.transliteration: evameva khalu somyemāḥ sarvāḥ prajāḥ sata āgamya na viduḥ sata  āgacchāmaha iti ta iha vyāghro vā siṃho vā vṛko vā varāho vā kīṭo vā  pataṅgo vā daṃśo vā maśako vā yadyadbhavanti tadābhavanti || 6.10.2 ||
units.translation: —In the same way, O Somya, all these beings, having come from Sat [Brahman], never know this. They never think, ‘We have come from Sat.’ Whatever they were before in this world—whether a tiger or lion or leopard or boar or bug or insect or flea or mosquito—they are born again [according to their karma. They never know that they came from Sat].

units.sanskrit: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि  श्वेतकेतो इति भूय एव मा भगवान्विज्ञापयत्विति तथा सोम्येति होवाच ॥ ६.१०.३ ॥
units.transliteration: sa ya eṣo'ṇimaitadātmyamidaṃ sarvaṃ tatsatyaṃ sa ātmā tattvamasi  śvetaketo iti bhūya eva mā bhagavānvijñāpayatviti tathā somyeti hovāca || 6.10.3 ||
units.translation: ‘That which is the subtlest of all is the Self of all this. It is the Truth. It is the Self. That thou art, O Śvetaketu.’ [Śvetaketu then said,] ‘Sir, please explain this to me again.’ ‘Yes, Somya, I will explain it again,’ replied his father.

units.sanskrit: अस्य सोम्य महतो वृक्षस्य यो मूलेऽभ्याहन्याज्जीवन्स्रवेद्यो मध्येऽभ्याहन्याज्जीवन्स्रवेद्योऽग्रेऽभ्याहन्याज्जीवन्स्रवेत्स एष  जीवेनात्मनानुप्रभूतः पेपीयमानो मोदमानस्तिष्ठति ॥ ६.११.१ ॥  अस्य यदेकां शाखां जीवो जहात्यथ सा शुष्यति द्वितीयां जहात्यथ सा  शुष्यति तृतीयां जहात्यथ सा शुष्यति सर्वं जहाति सर्वः शुष्यति ॥ ६.११.२ ॥
units.transliteration: asya somya mahato vṛkṣasya yo mūle'bhyāhanyājjīvansravedyo madhye'bhyāhanyājjīvansravedyo'gre'bhyāhanyājjīvansravetsa eṣa jīvenātmanānuprabhūtaḥ pepīyamāno modamānastiṣṭhati || 6.11.1 ||  asya yadekāṃ śākhāṃ jīvo jahātyatha sā śuṣyati dvitīyāṃ jahātyatha sā  śuṣyati tṛtīyāṃ jahātyatha sā śuṣyati sarvaṃ jahāti sarvaḥ śuṣyati || 6.11.2 ||
units.translation: O Somya, if someone strikes at the root of a big tree, it will continue to live, though it may exude some juice. If he strikes at the middle, it will still live, though it may exude some juice. If he strikes at the top of the tree, it will survive, though it may exude some juice. Pervaded by the Self, the tree will keep drinking juice and living happily. But if the Self leaves a branch of a tree, that branch withers away and dies.
