units.transliteration: samādhirvā'samādhirvā kāryaṁ cānyatkuto bhavet | māṁ hi dhyātvā ca buddhvā ca manyante kṛtakṛtyatām ||17||
units.translation: How can I have concentration, non-concentration or other actions in Me as all men feel that the acme of their lives is fulfilled when they meditate on Me and know Me?

units.sanskrit: अहं ब्रह्म ञ्स्म िवोऽञ्स्म शुद्धो बुद्धोऽस्वयतः िि । अजः िवयत एव हमजिश्च ियोऽमृतः॥१८॥
units.transliteration: evaṁahaṁ brahmāsmi sarvo'smi śuddho buddho'smyataḥ sadā | ajaḥ sarvata evāhamajaraścākṣayo'mṛtaḥ ||18||
units.translation: I am, therefore, Brahman, the all-comprehensive Principle, ever Pure, ever Awakened and ever Unborn, devoid of old age, imperishable and immortal.

units.sanskrit: मिन्यः िवयभूतेषु बोद्ध िञ्श्चन्न ववद्यते । िम य्यिश्च ि िी च चेत तनत्योऽगुणोऽद्वयः ॥१९॥
units.transliteration: madanyaḥ sarvabhūteṣu boddhā kaścinna vidyate | karmādhyakṣaśca sākṣī ca cetā nityo'guṇo'dvayaḥ ||19||
units.translation: There is no knower other than Myself among all the beings. I am the distributor of the results of their actions and the witness. It is I to whom all beings owe their consciousness. Without qualities and without a second I am eternal.

units.sanskrit: न िच्च हं न च िच्च नोभयं िे विः सशवः । न मे िं्य न ि बत्रव य न हव य िवयि दृशेः ॥२०॥
units.transliteration: na saccāhaṁ na cāsacca nobhayaṁ kevalaḥ śivaḥ | na me saṁdhyā na rātrirvā nāharvā sarvadā dṛśeḥ ||20||
units.translation: I am not the three visible elements nor the two invisible ones, neither am I both (i.e., their combination, the body). I am devoid of all attributes and the three Gunas. In Me there is neither night nor day nor their juncture as I am always of the nature of light.

units.sanskrit: िवयमूततयववयुक्त ं यद्यथ खं िूक्ष्ममद्वयम ् । तेन प्यञ्स्म ववन भूतं ब्रह्मैव हं तथ ऽद्वयम ् ॥२१॥
units.transliteration: sarvamūrtiviyuktaṁ yadyathā khaṁ sūkṣmamadvayam | tenāpyasmi vinābhūtaṁ brahmaivāhaṁ tathā'dvayam ||21||
units.translation: Just as the ether is subtle, without a second and devoid of all forms, so, I am the non-dual Brahman devoid even of the ether.

units.sanskrit: मम त्म स्वत आत्मेतत भेिो व्योवनो यथ भवेत ् 1। एिस्य िुवषभेिेन तथ मम वविञ्ल्पतः॥२२॥
units.transliteration: mamātmā svata ātmeti bhedo vyomno yathā bhavet | ekasya suṣibhedena tathā mama vikalpitaḥ ||22||
units.translation: The distinction between the Self in Itself and my Self is due to the superimposition (of different adjuncts on one and the same Self), just as difference (is wrongly conceived to) exist in one and the same ether owing to apertures (in various objects).

units.sanskrit: भेिोऽभेिस्तथ चैिो न न चेतत वविञ्ल्पतः । ज्ञेयं ज्ञ त गततगयन्त म्येिञ्स्मन्ि ु तो भवेत ् ॥२३॥
units.transliteration: bhedo'bhedastathā caiko nānā ceti vikalpitaḥ | jñeyaṁ jñāta gatirgantā mayyekasminkuto bhavet ||23||
units.translation: How can difference, absence of difference, oneness, manyness and the qualities of being known and being a knower, the results of actions and also agency and experiencing be attributed to Me who am one only?

units.sanskrit: न मे हेयं न च िेयमववि िी यतो ह्यहम ् । िि मुक्तस्तथ शुद्धः िि बुद्धोऽगुणोऽद्वयः ॥२४॥
units.transliteration: na me heyaṁ na cādeyamavikārī yato hyaham | sadā muktastathā śuddhaḥ sadā buddho'guṇo'dvayaḥ ||24||
units.translation: I have nothing to reject or accept inasmuch as I am changeless. Always free, pure, awakened and without qualities, I am without a second.

units.sanskrit: इत्येवं िवयि त्म नं ववद्य त्िवं िम दहतः । ववदित्व म ं स्विेहस्थमृवषमुयक्तो ध्रुवो भवेत ् ॥२५॥
units.transliteration: ityevaṁ sarvadātmānaṁ vidyātsarvaṁ samāhitaḥ | viditvā māṁ svadehasthamṛṣirmukto dhruvo bhavet ||25||
units.translation: One should, with great concentration of mind, always know the Self to be All. One certainly becomes all-knowing and free when one knows Me to be residing is one’s own body.

units.sanskrit: ि ृ ति ृ त्यश्च सिद्धश्च योगी ब्र ह्मण एव च । य एवं वेि तत्त्व थयमन्यथ ह्य त्मह भवेत ् ॥२६॥
units.transliteration: kṛtakṛtyaśca siddhaśca yogī brāhmaṇa eva ca | ya evaṁ veda tattvārthamanyathā hyātmahā bhavet ||26||
units.translation: He who thus knows the reality of the Self becomes successful in attaining the goal of his life and becomes perfect. He becomes a Knower of Brahman and one with It. One knowing the Self otherwise may be said to commit suicide.

units.sanskrit: वेि थो तनञ्श्चतो ह्येष िम िेन मयोदितः । िंन्य सिभ्यः प्रवक्तव्यः श न्तेभ्यः सशष्टबुवद्धन ॥२७॥
units.transliteration: vedārtho niścito hyeṣa samāsena mayoditaḥ | saṁnyāsibhyaḥ pravaktavyaḥ śāntebhyaḥ śiṣṭabuddhinā ||27||
units.translation: This ascertained meaning of the Vedas described briefly by me should be imparted to those who have given up worldly action and controlled their minds by one whose intellect has been trained (according to the scriptures under a teacher who has known Brahman).

units.sanskrit: नछत्त्र्ा त्यक्े न हस्तेन स्र्यं नात्मा वर्मशष्यते | तथा मशष्टेन सर्ेि येन येन वर्शेष्यते ॥१॥
units.transliteration: chittvā tyaktena hastena svayaṁ nātmā viśiṣyate | tathā śiṣṭena sarveṇa yena yena viśeṣyate ||1||
units.translation: The Self Itself is not qualified by an arm which has been cut off and thrown away. Similarly, It is not qualified by any of the remaining things by which It is (thought to be) qualified.

units.sanskrit: तस्मात्त्यक्े न हस्तेन तुल्यं सर्ं वर्शेषिम ् | अनात्मत्र्ेन तस्माज्ज्ञो मुक्ः सर्ैवर्वशेषिैः ॥२॥
units.transliteration: tasmāttyaktena hastena tulyaṁ sarvaṁ viśeṣaṇam | anātmatvena tasmājjño muktaḥ sarvairviśeṣaṇaiḥ ||2||
units.translation: Therefore all the qualifications are similar to the arm (cut off and) thrown away as they are all non-Self. So the Self is free from all qualifications.

units.sanskrit: वर्शेषिममदं सर्ं सा्र्लंकििं यथा | अवर्द्या्यासतः सर्ं ज्ञात आत्मन्यसद्भर्ेत ् ॥३॥
units.transliteration: viśeṣaṇamidaṁ sarvaṁ sādhvalaṁkaraṇaṁ yathā | avidyādhyāsataḥ sarvaṁ jñāta ātmanyasadbhavet ||3||
units.translation: It is reasonable that, like ornaments, all these are qualifications (of the Self) owing to superimposition through Ignorance. When the Self is known they prove to be unreal.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञातैर्ात्मा सदा राह्यो ज्ञेयमुत्सृज्य के र्लः | अहममत्यवप यद्-राहयं व्यपेताङ्गसमं दह तत ् ॥४॥
units.transliteration: jñātaivātmā sadā grāhyo jñeyamutsr̥jya kevalaḥ | ahamityapi yad-grāhayaṁ vyapetāṅgasamaṁ hi tat ||4||
units.translation: After rejecting the object portion one should accept the Self as the knower free from all qualifications. The ego, the object portion, is also like the part of the body cut off.

units.sanskrit: यार्ान्स्यादददमंशो यः स स्र्तोSन्यो वर्शेषिम ् | वर्शेषप्रक्षयो यत्र मसद्धो ज्ञग्श्चत्रगुयवथा ॥५॥
units.transliteration: yāvānsyādidamaṁśo yaḥ sa svato'nyo viśeṣaṇam | viśeṣaprakṣayo yatra siddho jñaścitraguryathā ||5||
units.translation: The Self of which the whole of the object portion is the qualification is different from it. Bereft of all qualifications It has an independent existence like that of a man possessing a variegated cow.

units.sanskrit: इदमंशोSहममत्यत्र त्याज्यो नात्मेनत पग्ण्ितैः | अहं ब्रह्मेनत मशष्टांशो भूतपूर्वगतेभवर्ेत ् ॥६॥
units.transliteration: edamaṁśo'hamityatra tyājyo nātmeti paṇḍitaiḥ | ahaṁ brahmeti śiṣṭāṁśo bhūtapūrvagaterbhavet ||6||
units.translation: As it is not the Self the object portion in the consciousness ‘I’ should be renounced by the wise. As It was mixed with egoism previously the remaining (non-object) portion is implied by the word ‘I’ in the sentence ‘I am Brahman.’

units.sanskrit: बुद््यारूढं सदा सर्ं दृश्यते यत्र तत्र र्ा | मया तस्मात्पिं ब्रह्म सर्वज्ञश्चाग्स्म सर्वगः ||१||
units.transliteration: buddhyārūḍhaṁ sadā sarvaṁ dr̥śyate yatra tatra vā | mayā tasmātparaṁ brahma sarvajñaścāsmi sarvagaḥ ||1||
units.translation: I am the supreme Brahman all-knowing and all-pervading, as pervaded by the intellect all things in all conditions are always illumined by me.

units.sanskrit: यथात्मबुद्चधचािािां साक्षी तद्र्त्पिेष्र्वप | नैर्ापोढ ुं न र्ादातुं शक्यस्तस्मात्पिो ह्यहम ् ||२||
units.transliteration: yathātmabuddhicārāṇāṁ sākṣī tadvatpareṣvapi | naivāpoḍhuṁ na vādātuṁ śakyastasmātparo hyaham ||2||
units.translation: Just as I am the witness of all the objects of my intellect, so, I am that of the objects of other intellects. I am not capable of being rejected or accepted. Therefore I am the supreme Brahman.

units.sanskrit: वर्करित्र्मशुद्धत्र्ं भौनतकत्र्ं न चात्मनः | अशेषबुद्चधसाक्षक्षत्र्ाद् बुद्चधर्च्चाल्पर्ेदना ||३||
units.transliteration: vikaritvamaśuddhatvaṁ bhautikatvaṁ na cātmanaḥ | aśeṣabuddhisākṣitvād buddhivaccālpavedanā ||3||
units.translation: As It is the witness of all intellects (and their modifications) the Self is not of limited knowledge like the intellects and has no change, impurity or material nature in It.

units.sanskrit: मिौ प्रकाश्यते यद्र्द्रक्ताद्याकाितातपे | मनय संदृश्यते सर्वमातपेनेर् तन्मया ||४||
units.transliteration: maṇau prakāśyate yadvadraktādyākāratātape | mayi saṁdr̥śyate sarvamātapeneva tanmayā ||4||
units.translation: Just as in the presence of sunlight colours such as red etc., (of flowers and other things) are manifested in a jewel, so, all objects are seen (in the intellect) in My Presence. All things are, therefore, illumined by Me like sunlight.

units.sanskrit: बुद्धौ दृश्यं भर्ेद् बुद्धौ सत्यां नाग्स्त वर्पयवये | द्रष्टा यस्मात्सदा द्रष्टा तस्माद् द्र्ैतं न वर्द्यते ||५||
units.transliteration: buddhau dr̥śyaṁ bhaved buddhau satyāṁ nāsti viparyaye | draṣṭā yasmātsadā draṣṭā tasmād dvaitaṁ na vidyate ||5||
units.translation: Objects of knowledge exist in the intellect as long as it is there (in waking and dream); but they do not exist in the opposite case (i.e., when the intellect is merged during deep sleep). The Knower is always the knower. Duality has, therefore, no existence.

units.sanskrit: अवर्र्ेकात्पिाभार्ं यथा बुद्चधिर्ैत्तथा | वर्र्ेकात्तु पिादन्यः स्र्यं चावप न वर्द्यते ||६||
units.transliteration: avivekātparābhāvaṁ yathā buddhiravaittathā | vivekāttu parādanyaḥ svayaṁ cāpi na vidyate ||6||
units.translation: The intellect knew the nonexistence of the supreme Brahman before the discrimination (between the Self and the non-Self). But after the discrimination there is no individual Self different from Brahman nor is there the intellect itself.

units.sanskrit: ईश्र्िश्चेदनात्मा स्यान्नासार्स्मीनत धाियेत ् । आत्मा चेदीश्र्िोऽस्मीनत वर्द्या साऽन्यननर्नतवका ॥१॥
units.transliteration: īśvaraścedanātmā syannāsāvasmīti dhārayet | ātmā cedīśvaro'smīti vidyā sā'nyanivartikā ||1||
units.translation: The aspirant cannot know that he is Brahman if It be different from the Self. (It then contradicts the Sruti.) But if he has the conviction that he, the Self, is Brahman (there is no contradiction to the Sruti). This is (right) Knowledge which destroys Ignorance.

units.sanskrit: आत्मनोऽन्यस्य चेद्धमाव अस्थूलत्र्ादयो मताः। अज्ञेयत्र्ेऽस्य क्रक ं तैः स्यादात्मत्र्े ह्यन्यधीह्नुनतः ॥२॥
units.transliteration: ātmano'nyasya ceddharmā asthūlatvādayo matāḥ | ajñeyatve'sya kiṁ taiḥ syādātmatve hyanyadhīhnutiḥ ||2||
units.translation: What would be the use (of the description by the Sruti) of the qualities, ‘not large’ etc. if they were the qualities of one other than the Self, it being not an object of search? But if Brahman (with these qualities) is the Self the ideas such as, largeness, smallness, etc. are negated from the latter.

units.sanskrit: ममथ्या्यासननषेधाथं ततोऽस्थूलादद गृह्यताम ् । पित्र चेग्न्नषेधाथं शून्यतार्िवनं दह तत ् ॥३॥
units.transliteration: mithyādhyāsaniṣedhārthaṁ tato'sthulādi gṛhyatām | paratra cenniṣedhārthaṁ śūnyatāvarṇanaṁ hi tat ||3||
units.translation: Know, therefore, that the Sruti, ‘ not large’ etc. is meant to negate the false superimposition (of largeness, smallness, etc. on the Self) as it would be a description of a void if it were meant to negate those qualities from one other than the Self.

units.sanskrit: बुभुत्सोयवदद चान्यत्र प्रत्यगात्मन इष्यते । अप्रािो ह्यमनाः शुभ्र इनत चानथवकं र्चः ॥४॥
units.transliteration: bubhutsoryadi cānyatra pratyagātmana iṣyate | aprāṇo hyamanāḥ śubhra iti cānarthakaṁ vacaḥ ||4||
units.translation: Moreover, the saying, ‘devoid of the vital force, devoid of the mind and pure’ would be unmeaning if these qualities were meant to be negated from one other than the individual Self, the aspirant.

units.sanskrit: प्रि शस्थं यथ िेहं ि िोिमसभमन्यते । द्रष्ट भ िं तथ चचत्तं द्रष्ट हसमतत मन्यते॥१॥
units.transliteration: prakāśasthaṁ yathā dehaṁ sālokamabhimanyate | draṣṭābhāsaṁ tathā cittaṁ draṣṭāhamiti manyate ||1||
units.translation: Just as a man (erroneously) looks upon his body placed in the sun as having the property of light in it, so, he looks upon the intellect pervaded by the reflection of Pure Consciousness as the Self, the witness.

units.sanskrit: यिेव दृश्यते िोिे तेन सभन्नत्वम त्मनः । प्रपद्यते ततो मूढस्तेन त्म नं न ववन्ितत ॥२॥
units.transliteration: yadeva dṛśyate loke tenābhinnatvamātmanaḥ | prapadyate tato mūḍhastenātmānaṁ na vindati ||2||
units.translation: The Self gets identified with whatever is seen in the world. It is for this reason that an ignorant man does not know himself (to be Brahman).Brahman). [The reason why people mistake the combination of the subtle and the gross bodies for the Self is this identification caused by Its reflection. On account of there being the reflection of Pure Consciousness in the body, the senses, the mind, the intellect and the vital force, they appear to be conscious and cannot, therefore, be discriminated from the Self.]

units.sanskrit: िशमस्य नव त्मत्वप्रततपवत्तवि त्मनः । दृश्येषु तद्विेव यं मूढो िोिो न च न्यथ ॥३॥
units.transliteration: daśamasya navātmatvapratipattivadātmanaḥ | dṛśyeṣu tadvadevāyaṁ mūḍho loko na cānyathā ||3||
units.translation: An ignorant man gets identified with objects of knowledge and does not know the Self (which is different from them) like the tenth boy who got identified as it were with the other nine.

units.sanskrit: त्वं ि ु रु त्वं तिेवेतत प्रत्यय वेिि सििौ । एिनीडौ िथं स्य त ं ववरुद्धौ न्य यतो वि ॥४॥
units.transliteration: tvaṁ kuru tvaṁ tadeveti pratyayāvekakālikau | ekanīḍau kathaṁ syātāṁ viruddhau nyāyato vāda ||4||
units.translation: Say how there can reasonably by the two contrary ideas, ‘You do this’ and ‘You are Brahman’ at the same time and in respect of the same person.

units.sanskrit: िेह सभम तननो िुःखं न िेहस्य स्वभ वतः । स्व पवत्तत्प्रह ण य तत्त्वसमत्युच््ते दृशेः ॥५॥
units.transliteration: dehābhimānino duḥkhaṁ nādehasya svabhāvataḥ | svāpavattatprahāṇāya tattvamityucyte dṛśeḥ ||5||
units.translation: Pain belongs to one identifying oneself with the body. One not identifying oneself with it, as in deep sleep, is, therefore, by nature free from pain. The teaching, ‘Thou art That’ is imparted in order that this identification might be removed from the Self.

units.sanskrit: दृशेश्छ य यि रूढ मुखच्छ येव िशयने । पश्यंस्तं प्रत्ययं योगी दृष्ट आत्मेतत मन्यते ॥६॥
units.transliteration: dṛśeśchāyā yadārūḍhā mukhacchāyeva darśane | paśyaṁstaṁ pratyayaṁ yogī dṛṣṭa ātmeti manyate ||6||
units.translation: An ignorant person looks upon the intellect as the Self, when there is the reflection of the Self in the intellect like that of a face in a mirror.

units.sanskrit: तं च मूढं च यद्यन्यं प्रत्ययं वेवत्त नो दृशेः । ि एव योचगन ं प्रेष्ठो नेतिः स्य न्न िंशयः ॥७॥
units.transliteration: taṁ ca mūḍhaṁ ca yadyanyaṁ pratyayaṁ vetti no dṛśeḥ | sa eva yogināṁ preṣṭho netaraḥ syanna saṁśayaḥ ||7||
units.translation: He who looks upon the ego, the indiscrimination that produces delusion and other mental modifications (or the reflection of the Self in them) as having no connection with the Self, is, without doubt, the dearest to the knowers of Brahman. No one else is so.

units.sanskrit: ववज्ञ तेययस्तु ववज्ञ त ि त्वसमत्युच्यते यतः । ि स्य िनुभवस्तस्य ततोऽन्योऽनुभवो मृष ॥८॥
units.transliteration: vijñāteryastu vijñātā sa tvamityucyate yataḥ | sa syādanubhavastasya tato'nyo'nubhavo mṛṣā ||8||
units.translation: As it is the Knower of knowledge that is referred to by the word ‘Thou’ (in the Sruti) the understanding of the term ‘Thou’ in this sense is correct. The other sense different from it is due to superimposition.

units.sanskrit: दृसशरूपे िि तनत्ये िशयन िशयने मतय । िथं स्य त ं ततो न न्य इष्यतेऽनुभवस्ततः ॥९॥
units.transliteration: dṛśirūpe sadānitye darśanādarśane mayi | kathaṁ syātāṁ tato nānya iṣyate'nubhavastataḥ ||9||
units.translation: How can there be knowledge or ignorance in Me who am eternal and always of the nature of Pure Consciousness? No knowledge, therefore, other than the Self can be accepted.

units.sanskrit: यत्स्थस्त पो िवेिेहे दृशेः ि ववषयो यथ । ित्त्वस्थस्तद्विेवेह दृशेः ि ववषयस्तथ ॥१०॥
units.transliteration: yatsthastāpo raverdehe dṛśeḥ sa viṣayo yathā | sattvasthastadvadeveha dṛśeḥ sa viṣayastathā ||10||
units.translation: Just as the heat of the sun (in a part of the body) together with that part of the body is the object of the knower, so, pain and pleasure together with the intellect in which they lie are the objects of the Self.

units.sanskrit: प्रततवषद्धे दृगंशोऽज्ञः खसमवैिििोऽद्वयः । तनत्यमुक्तः िि शुद्धः िोऽहं ब्रह्म ञ्स्म िे विः ॥११॥
units.transliteration: pratiṣiddhe dṛgaṁśo'jñaḥ khamivaikaraso'dvayaḥ | nityamuktaḥ sadā śuddhaḥ so'haṁ brahmāsmi kevalaḥ ||11||
units.translation: I am Brahman without attributes, ever pure, ever free, non-dual, homogeneous like the ether and of the nature of Consciousness from which the object portion has been negated.

units.sanskrit: ववज्ञ तुनैव ववज्ञ त पिोऽन्यः िंभवत्यतः । ववज्ञ त हं पिो मुक्तः िवयभूतेषु िवयि ॥१२॥
units.transliteration: vijñāturnaiva vijñātā paro'nyaḥ saṁbhavatyataḥ | vijñātāhaṁ paro muktaḥ sarvabhūteṣu sarvadā ||12||
units.translation: I am always the free supreme Knower in all beings inasmuch as there cannot be a more comprehensive knower different from Me.

units.sanskrit: यो वेि िुप्तदृवष्टत्वम त्म नोऽितृयत ं तथ । ब्रह्मववत्त्वं तथ मुक्त्व ि आत्म ज्ञो न चेतिः ॥१३॥
units.transliteration: yo vedāluptadṛṣṭitvamātmāno'kartṛtāṁ tathā | brahmavittvaṁ tathā muktvā sa ātmājño na cetaraḥ ||13||
units.translation: He who Knows that the Consciousness of the Self never ceases to exist, and that It is never an agent and also gives up the egoism that he is a Knower of Brahman is a (real) knower of the Self. Others are not so.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञ तैव हमववज्ञेयः शुद्धो मुक्तः ििेत्यवप । वववेिी प्रत्ययो बुद्धेदृयश्यत्व न्न शव न्यतः ॥१४॥
units.transliteration: jñataivāhamavijñeyaḥ śuddho muktaḥ sadetyapi | vivekī pratyayo buddherdṛśyatvānnāśavānyataḥ ||14||
units.translation: Capable by no means of being known I am the knower and always free and pure as the discriminating knowledge is in the intellect and liable to be destroyed on account of its being an object of Knowledge.

units.sanskrit: अिुप्त त्व त्मनो दृवष्टनोत्प द्य ि ििै ययतः । दृश्यय च न्यय दृष्ट्य जन्यत स्य ः प्रिञ्ल्पत ॥१५॥
units.transliteration: aluptā tvātmano dṛṣṭirnotpādyā kārakairyataḥ | dṛśyayā cānyayā dṛṣṭyā janyatāsyāḥ prakalpitā ||15||
units.translation: The Consciousness of the Self, on the other hand, never goes out of existence and is not capable of being produced by the action of agents etc. inasmuch as producibility is superimposed on It by another consciousness, Its object and different from It.

units.sanskrit: िेह त्मबुद्ध्यपेित्व ि त्मनः ितृयत मृष । नैव किञ्चचत्ििोमीतत ित्य बुवद्धः प्रम णज ॥१६॥
units.transliteration: dehātmabuddhyapekṣatvādātmanaḥ kartṛtā mṛṣā | naiva kiñcitkaromiti satyā buddhiḥ pramāṇajā ||16||
units.translation: The doership of the Self is false as it depends on the misconception of the body being the Self. That I do not do anything is the true knowledge which arises from the right evidence (the Vedas).

units.sanskrit: ितृयत्वं ि िि पेिमितृयत्वं स्वभ वतः । ित य भोक्ते तत ववज्ञ नं मृषैवेतत िुतनञ्श्चतम ् ॥१७॥
units.transliteration: kartṛtvaṁ kārakāpekṣamakartṛtvaṁ svabhāvataḥ | kartā bhokteti vijñānaṁ mṛṣaiveti suniścitam ||17||
units.translation: Agency depends on doership, instruments etc. but non-agency is natural. It has, therefore, been very well ascertained that the knowledge that one is a doer and experiencer is certainly false.

units.sanskrit: एवं श स्त्र नुम न भ्य ं स्वरूपेऽवगते ितत । तनयोज्योऽहसमतत ह्येष ित्य बुवद्धः िथं भवेत ॥१८॥
units.transliteration: evaṁ śāstrānumānābhyāṁ svarūpe'vagate sati | niyojyo'hamiti hyeṣā satyā buddhiḥ kathaṁ bhavet ||18||
units.translation: How can the idea that I am a person to be enjoined (by the Vedas to perform actions) be true when the real nature of the Self is thus known from the scriptures and inference?

units.sanskrit: यथ िव यन्तिं व्योम व्योवनोऽप्यभ्यन्तिो ह्यहम । तनववयि िोऽचिः शुद्धोऽजिो मुक्तः िि द्वयः॥१९॥
units.transliteration: yathā sarvāntaraṁ vyoma vyomno'pyabhantaro hyaham | nirvikāro'calaḥ śuddho'jaro muktaḥ sadādvayaḥ ||19||
units.translation: Just as the ether is in the interior of all, so, I am in the interior even of the ether. Therefore I am without change, without motion, pure, devoid of old age, ever free and without a second.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञातृज्ञानज्ञेयभेदः परे नात्मनि विद्यते। चिदानन्दैकरूपत्वाद्दीप्यते स्वयमेव हि ॥४१॥
units.transliteration: jñātṛjñānajñeyabhedaḥ pare nātmani vidyate. cidānandaikarūpatvāddīpyate svayameva hi || 41 ||
units.translation: There are no distinctions such as "Knower", the "Knowledge" and the "Object of Knowledge" in the Supreme Self. On account of Its being of the nature of endless Bliss, It does not admit of such distinctions within Itself. It alone shines by Itself.

units.sanskrit: एवमात्मारणौ ध्यानमथने सततं कृते। उदितावगतिज्वाला सर्वाज्ञानेन्धनं दहेत् ॥४२॥
units.transliteration: evamātmāraṇau dhyānamathane satataṃ kṛte. uditāvagatijvālā sarvājñānendhanaṃ dahet || 42 ||
units.translation: When this the lower and the higher aspects of the Self are well churned together, the fire of knowledge is born from it, which in its mighty conflagration shall burn down all the fuel of ignorance in us.

units.sanskrit: अरुणेनेव बोधेन पूर्वं संतमसे हृते। तत आविर्भवेदात्मा स्वयमेवांशुमानिव ॥४३॥
units.transliteration: aruṇeneva bodhena pūrvaṃ saṃtamase hṛte. tata āvirbhavedātmā svayamevāṃśumāniva || 43 ||
units.translation: The Lord of the early dawn (Aruna) himself has already looted away the thick darkness, when soon the sun rises. The Divine Consciousness of the Self rises when the right knowledge has already killed the darkness in the bosom.

units.sanskrit: आत्मा तु सततं प्राप्तोऽप्यप्राप्तवदविद्यया। तन्नाशे प्राप्तवद्भाति स्वकण्ठाभरणं यथा ॥४४॥
units.transliteration: ātmā tu satataṃ prāpto'pyaprāptavadavidyayā. tannāśe prāptavadbhāti svakaṇṭhābharaṇaṃ yathā || 44 ||
units.translation: Atman is an ever-present Reality. Yet, because of ignorance it is not realised. On the destruction of ignorance Atman is realised. It is like the missing ornament of one's neck.

units.sanskrit: स्थाणौ पुरुषवद्भ्रान्त्या कृता ब्रह्मणि जीवता। जीवस्य तात्त्विके रूपे तस्मिन्दृष्टे निवर्तते ॥४५॥
units.transliteration: sthāṇau puruṣavadbhrāntyā kṛtā brahmaṇi jīvatā. jīvasya tāttvike rūpe tasmindṛṣṭe nivartate || 45 ||
units.translation: Brahman appears to be a'Jiva' because of ignorance, just as a post appears to be a ghost. The ego-centric-individuality is destroyed when the real nature of the'Jiva' is realised as the Self.

units.sanskrit: तत्त्वस्वरूपानुभवादुत्पन्नं ज्ञानमञ्जसा। अहं ममेति चाज्ञानं बाधते दिग्भ्रमादिवत् ॥४६॥
units.transliteration: tattvasvarūpānubhavādutpannaṃ jñānamañjasā. ahaṃ mameti cājñānaṃ bādhate digbhramādivat || 46 ||
units.translation: The ignorance characterised by the notions 'I' and'Mine' is destroyed by the knowledge produced by the realisation of the true nature of the Self, just as right information removes the wrong notion about the directions.

units.sanskrit: सम्यग्विज्ञानवान्योगी स्वात्मन्येवाखिलं स्थितम्। एकं च सर्वमात्मानमीक्षते ज्ञानचक्षुषा ॥४७॥
units.transliteration: samyagvijñānavānyogī svātmanyevākhilaṃ sthitam. ekaṃ ca sarvamātmānamīkṣate jñānacakṣuṣā || 47 ||
units.translation: The Yogi of perfect realisation and enlightenment sees through his "eye of wisdom" (Gyana Chakshush) the entire universe in his own Self and regards everything else as his own Self and nothing else.

units.sanskrit: आत्मैवेदं जगत्सर्वमात्मनोऽन्यन्न किंचन। मृदो यद्वद्धटादीनि स्वात्मानं सर्वमीक्षते ॥४८॥
units.transliteration: ātmaivedaṃ jagatsarvamātmano'nyanna kiṃcana. mṛdo yadvaddhaṭādīni svātmānaṃ sarvamīkṣate || 48 ||
units.translation: Nothing whatever exists other than the Atman: the tangible universe is verily Atman. As pots and jars are verily made of clay and cannot be said to be anything but clay, so too, to the enlightened soul and that is perceived is the Self.

units.sanskrit: जीवन्मुक्तस्तु तद्विद्वान्पूर्वोपाधिगुणांस्त्यजेत्। स सच्चिदादिधर्मत्वं भेजे भ्रमरकीटवत् ॥४९॥
units.transliteration: jīvanmuktastu tadvidvānpūrvopādhiguṇāṃstyajet. sa saccidādidharmatvaṃ bheje bhramarakīṭavat || 49 ||
units.translation: A liberated one, endowed with Self-knowledge, gives up the traits of his previously explained equipments (Upadhis) and because of his nature of Sat-chit-ananda, he verily becomes Brahman like (the worm that grows to be) a wasp.

units.sanskrit: तीर्त्वा मोहार्णवं हत्वा रागद्वेषादिराक्षसान्। योगी शान्तिसमायुक्त आत्मा रामो विराजते ॥५०॥
units.transliteration: tīrtvā mohārṇavaṃ hatvā rāgadveṣādirākṣasān. yogī śāntisamāyukta ātmā rāmo virājate || 50 ||
units.translation: After crossing the ocean of delusion and killing the monsters of likes and dislikes, the Yogi who is united with peace dwells in the glory of his own realised Self - as an Atmaram.

units.sanskrit: देहेन्द्रियगुणान्कर्माण्यमले सच्चिदात्मनि। अध्यस्यन्त्यविवेकेन गगने नीलतादिवत् ॥२१॥
units.transliteration: dehendriyaguṇānkarmāṇyamale saccidātmani. adhyasyantyavivekena gagane nīlatādivat || 21 ||
units.translation: Fools, because they lack in their powers of discrimination superimpose on the Atman, the Absolute-Existence-Knowledge (Sat-Chit), all the varied functions of the body and the senses, just as they attribute blue colour and the like to the sky.

units.sanskrit: अज्ञानान्मानसोपाधेः कर्तृत्वादीनि चात्मनि। कल्प्यन्तेऽम्बुगते चन्द्रे चलनादि यथाम्भसः ॥२२॥
units.transliteration: ajñānānmānasopādheḥ kartṛtvādīni cātmani. kalpyante'mbugate candre calanādi yathāmbhasaḥ || 22 ||
units.translation: The tremblings that belong to the waters are attributed through ignorance to the reflected moon dancing on it: likewise agency of action, of enjoyment and of other limitations (which really belong to the mind) are delusively understood as the nature of the Self (Atman).

units.sanskrit: रागेच्छासुखदुःखादि बुद्धौ सत्यां प्रवर्तते। सुषुप्तौ नास्ति तन्नाशे तस्माद्बुद्धेस्तु नात्मनः ॥२३॥
units.transliteration: rāgecchāsukhaduḥkhādi buddhau satyāṃ pravartate. suṣuptau nāsti tannāśe tasmādbuddhestu nātmanaḥ || 23 ||
units.translation: Attachment, desire, pleasure, pain, etc., are perceived to exist so long as Buddhi or mind functions. They are not perceived in deep sleep when the mind ceases to exist. Therefore they belong to the mind alone and not to the Atman.

units.sanskrit: प्रकाशोऽर्कस्य तोयस्य शैत्यमग्नेर्यथोष्णता। स्वभावः सच्चिदानन्दनित्यनिर्मलतात्मनः ॥२४॥
units.transliteration: prakāśo'rkasya toyasya śaityamagneryathoṣṇatā. svabhāvaḥ saccidānandanityanirmalatātmanaḥ || 24 ||
units.translation: Just as luminosity is the nature of the Sun, coolness of water and heat of fire, so too the nature of the Atman is Eternity, Purity, Reality, Consciousness and Bliss.

units.sanskrit: आत्मनः सच्चिदंशश्च बुद्धेर्वृत्तिरिति द्वयम्। संयोज्य चाविवेकेन जानामीति प्रवर्तते ॥२५॥
units.transliteration: ātmanaḥ saccidaṃśaśca buddhervṛttiriti dvayam. saṃyojya cāvivekena jānāmīti pravartate || 25 ||
units.translation: By the indiscriminate blending of the two - the Existence-Knowledge-aspect of the Self and the thought-wave of the intellect - there arises the notion of "I know".

units.sanskrit: आत्मनो विक्रिया नास्ति बुद्धेर्बोधो न जात्विति। जीवः सर्वमलं ज्ञात्वा ज्ञाता द्रष्टेति मुह्यति ॥२६॥
units.transliteration: ātmano vikriyā nāsti buddherbodho na jātviti. jīvaḥ sarvamalaṃ jñātvā jñātā draṣṭeti muhyati || 26 ||
units.translation: Atman never does anything and the intellect of its own accord has no capacity to experience'I know'. But the individuality in us delusorily thinks he is himself the seer and the knower.

units.sanskrit: रज्जुसर्पवदात्मानं जीवं ज्ञात्वा भयं वहेत्। नाहं जीवः परात्मेति ज्ञातश्चेन्निर्भयो भवेत् ॥२७॥
units.transliteration: rajjusarpavadātmānaṃ jīvaṃ jñātvā bhayaṃ vahet. nāhaṃ jīvaḥ parātmeti jñātaścennirbhayo bhavet || 27 ||
units.translation: Just as the person who regards a rope as a snake is overcome by fear, so also one considering oneself as the ego (Jiva) is overcome by fear. The ego-centric individuality in us regains fearlessness by realising that It is not a Jiva but is Itself the Supreme Soul.

units.sanskrit: आत्मावभासयत्येको बुद्ध्यादीनीन्द्रियाणि हि। दीपो घटादिवत्स्वात्मा जडैस्तैर्नावभास्यते ॥२८॥
units.transliteration: ātmāvabhāsayatyeko buddhyādīnīndriyāṇi hi. dīpo ghaṭādivatsvātmā jaḍaistairnāvabhāsyate || 28 ||
units.translation: Just as a lamp illumines a jar or a pot, so also the Atman illumines the mind and the sense organs, etc. These material-objects by themselves cannot illumine themselves because they are inert.

units.sanskrit: स्वबोधे नान्यबोधेच्छा बोधरूपतयात्मनः। न दीपस्यान्यदीपेच्छा यथा स्वात्मप्रकाशने ॥२९॥
units.transliteration: svabodhe nānyabodhecchā bodharūpatayātmanaḥ. na dīpasyānyadīpecchā yathā svātmaprakāśane || 29 ||
units.translation: A lighted lamp does not need another lamp to illumine its light. So too, Atman which is Knowledge itself needs no other knowledge to know it.

units.sanskrit: निषिध्य निखिलोपाधीन्नेति नेतीति वाक्यतः। विद्यादैक्यं महावाक्यैर्जीवात्मपरमात्मनोः ॥३०॥
units.transliteration: niṣidhya nikhilopādhīnneti netīti vākyataḥ. vidyādaikyaṃ mahāvākyairjīvātmaparamātmanoḥ || 30 ||
units.translation: By a process of negation of the conditionings (Upadhis) through the help of the scriptural statement'It is not this, It is not this', the oneness of the individual soul and the Supreme Soul, as indicated by the great Mahavakyas, has to be realised.

units.sanskrit: बाह्यानित्यसुखासक्तिं हित्वात्मसुखनिर्वृतः। घटस्थदीपवच्छश्वदन्तरेव प्रकाशते ॥५१॥
units.transliteration: bāhyānityasukhāsaktiṃ hitvātmasukhanirvṛtaḥ. ghaṭasthadīpavacchaśvadantareva prakāśate || 51 ||
units.translation: The self-abiding Jivan Mukta, relinquishing all his attachments to the illusory external happiness and satisfied with the bliss derived from the Atman, shines inwardly like a lamp placed inside a jar.

units.sanskrit: उपाधिस्थोऽपि तद्धर्मैरलिप्तो व्योमवन्मुनिः। सर्वविन्मूढवत्तिष्ठेदसक्तो वायुवच्चरेत् ॥५२॥
units.transliteration: upādhistho'pi taddharmairalipto vyomavanmuniḥ. sarvavinmūḍhavattiṣṭhedasakto vāyuvaccaret || 52 ||
units.translation: Though he lives in the conditionings (Upadhis), he, the contemplative one, remains ever unconcerned with anything or he may move about like the wind, perfectly unattached.

units.sanskrit: उपाधिविलयाद्विष्णौ निर्विशेषं विशेन्मुनिः। जले जलं वियद्व्योम्नि तेजस्तेजसि वा यथा ॥५३॥
units.transliteration: upādhivilayādviṣṇau nirviśeṣaṃ viśenmuniḥ. jale jalaṃ viyadvyomni tejastejasi vā yathā || 53 ||
units.translation: On the destruction of the Upadhis, the contemplative one is totally absorbed in'Vishnu', the All-pervading Spirit, like water into water, space into space and light into light.

units.sanskrit: यल्लाभान्नापरो लाभो यत्सुखान्नापरं सुखम्। यज्ज्ञानान्नापरं ज्ञानं तद्ब्रह्मेत्यवधारयेत् ॥५४॥
units.transliteration: yallābhānnāparo lābho yatsukhānnāparaṃ sukham. yajjñānānnāparaṃ jñānaṃ tadbrahmetyavadhārayet || 54 ||
units.translation: Realise That to be Brahman, the attainment of which leaves nothing more to be attained, the blessedness of which leaves no other blessing to be desired and the knowledge of which leaves nothing more to be known.

units.sanskrit: यद्दृष्ट्वा नापरं दृश्यं यद्भूत्वा न पुनर्भवः। यज्ज्ञात्वा नापरं ज्ञेयं तद्ब्रह्मेत्यवधारयेत् ॥५५॥
units.transliteration: yaddṛṣṭvā nāparaṃ dṛśyaṃ yadbhūtvā na punarbhavaḥ. yajjñātvā nāparaṃ jñeyaṃ tadbrahmetyavadhārayet || 55 ||
units.translation: Realise that to be Brahman which, when seen, leaves nothing more to be seen, which having become one is not born again in this world and which, when knowing leaves nothing else to be known.

units.sanskrit: तिर्यगूर्ध्वमधः पूर्णं सच्चिदानन्दमद्वयम्। अनन्तं नित्यमेकं यत्तद्ब्रह्मेत्यवधारयेत् ॥५६॥
units.transliteration: tiryagūrdhvamadhaḥ pūrṇaṃ saccidānandamadvayam. anantaṃ nityamekaṃ yattadbrahmetyavadhārayet || 56 ||
units.translation: Realise that to be Brahman which is Existence-Knowledge-Bliss-Absolute, which is Non-dual, Infinite, Eternal and One and which fills all the quarters - above and below and all that exists between.

units.sanskrit: अतद्व्यावृत्तिरूपेण वेदान्तैर्लक्ष्यतेऽव्ययम्। अखण्डानन्दमेकं यत्तद्ब्रह्मेत्यवधारयेत् ॥५७॥
units.transliteration: atadvyāvṛttirūpeṇa vedāntairlakṣyate'vyayam. akhaṇḍānandamekaṃ yattadbrahmetyavadhārayet || 57 ||
units.translation: Realise that to be Brahman which is Non-dual, Indivisible, One and Blissful and which is indicated in Vedanta as the Immutable Substratum, realised after the negation of all tangible objects.

units.sanskrit: अखण्डानन्दरूपस्य तस्यानन्दलवाश्रिताः। ब्रह्माद्यास्तारतम्येन भवन्त्यानन्दिनोऽखिलाः ॥५८॥
units.transliteration: akhaṇḍānandarūpasya tasyānandalavāśritāḥ. brahmādyāstāratamyena bhavantyānandino'khilāḥ || 58 ||
units.translation: Deities like Brahma and others taste only a particle, of the unlimited Bliss of Brahman and enjoy in proportion their share of that particle.

units.sanskrit: तद्युक्तमखिलं वस्तु व्यवहारश्चिदन्वितः। तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म क्षीरे सर्पिरिवाखिले ॥५९॥
units.transliteration: tadyuktamakhilaṃ vastu vyavahāraścidanvitaḥ. tasmātsarvagataṃ brahma kṣīre sarpirivākhile || 59 ||
units.translation: All objects are pervaded by Brahman. All actions are possible because of Brahman: therefore Brahman permeates everything as butter permeates milk.

units.sanskrit: अनण्वस्थूलमह्रस्वमदीर्घमजमव्ययम्। अरूपगुणवर्णाख्यं तद्ब्रह्मेत्यवधारयेत् ॥६०॥
units.transliteration: anaṇvasthūlamahrasvamadīrghamajamavyayam. arūpaguṇavarṇākhyaṃ tadbrahmetyavadhārayet || 60 ||
units.translation: Realise that to be Brahman which is neither subtle nor gross: neither short nor long: without birth or change: without form, qualities, colour and name.

units.sanskrit: यद्भासा भास्यतेऽर्कादि भास्यैर्यत्तु न भास्यते। येन सर्वमिदं भाति तद्ब्रह्मेत्यवधारयेत् ॥६१॥
units.transliteration: yadbhāsā bhāsyate'rkādi bhāsyairyattu na bhāsyate. yena sarvamidaṃ bhāti tadbrahmetyavadhārayet || 61 ||
units.translation: That by the light of which the luminous, orbs like the Sun and the Moon are illuminated, but which is not illumined by their light, realise that to be Brahman.

units.sanskrit: स्वयमन्तर्बहिर्व्याप्य भासयन्नखिलं जगत्। ब्रह्म प्रकाशते वह्निप्रतप्तायसपिण्डवत् ॥६२॥
units.transliteration: svayamantarbahirvyāpya bhāsayannakhilaṃ jagat. brahma prakāśate vahniprataptāyasapiṇḍavat || 62 ||
units.translation: Pervading the entire universe outwardly and inwardly the Supreme Brahman shines of Itself like the fire that permeates a red-hot iron-ball and glows by itself.

units.sanskrit: जगद्विलक्षणं ब्रह्म ब्रह्मणोऽन्यन्न किंचन। ब्रह्मान्यद्भाति चेन्मिथ्या यथा मरुमरीचिका ॥६३॥
units.transliteration: jagadvilakṣaṇaṃ brahma brahmaṇo'nyanna kiṃcana. brahmānyadbhāti cenmithyā yathā marumarīcikā || 63 ||
units.translation: Brahman is other than this, the universe. There exists nothing that is not Brahman. If any object other than Brahman appears to exist, it is unreal like the mirage.

units.sanskrit: दृश्यते श्रूयते यद्यद्ब्रह्मणोऽन्यन्न तद्भवेत्। तत्त्वज्ञानाच्च तद्ब्रह्म सच्चिदानन्दमद्वयम् ॥६४॥
units.transliteration: dṛśyate śrūyate yadyadbrahmaṇo'nyanna tadbhavet. tattvajñānācca tadbrahma saccidānandamadvayam || 64 ||
units.translation: All that is perceived, or heard, is Brahman and nothing else. Attaining the knowledge of the Reality, one sees the Universe as the non-dual Brahman, Existence-Knowledge-Bliss-Absolute.

units.sanskrit: सर्वगं सच्चिदानन्दं ज्ञानचक्षुर्निरीक्षते। अज्ञानचक्षुर्नेक्षेत भास्वन्तं भानुमन्धवत् ॥६५॥
units.transliteration: sarvagaṃ saccidānandaṃ jñānacakṣurnirīkṣate. ajñānacakṣurnekṣeta bhāsvantaṃ bhānumandhavat || 65 ||
units.translation: Though Atman is Pure Consciousness and ever present everywhere, yet It is perceived by the eye-of-wisdom alone: but one whose vision is obscured by ignorance he does not see It; as the blind do not see the resplendent Sun.

units.sanskrit: श्रवणादिभिरुद्दीप्त ज्ञानाग्निपरितापितः। जीवः सर्वमलान्मुक्तः स्वर्णवद्द्योतते स्वयम् ॥६६॥
units.transliteration: śravaṇādibhiruddīpta jñānāgniparitāpitaḥ. jīvaḥ sarvamalānmuktaḥ svarṇavaddyotate svayam || 66 ||
units.translation: The'Jiva' free from impurities, being heated in the fire of knowledge kindled by hearing and so on, shines of itself like gold.

units.sanskrit: हृदाकाशोदितो ह्यात्मा बोधभानुस्तमोपहृत्। सर्वव्यापी सर्वधारी भाति भासयतेऽखिलम् ॥६७॥
units.transliteration: hṛdākāśodito hyātmā bodhabhānustamopahṛt. sarvavyāpī sarvadhārī bhāti bhāsayate'khilam || 67 ||
units.translation: The Atman, the Sun of Knowledge that rises in the sky of the heart, destroys the darkness of the ignorance, pervades and sustains all and shines and makes everything to shine.

units.sanskrit: तपोभिः क्षीणपापानां शान्तानां वीतरागिणाम्। मुमुक्षूणामपेक्ष्योऽयमात्मबोधो विधीयते ॥१॥
units.transliteration: tapobhiḥ kṣīṇapāpānāṃ śāntānāṃ vītarāgiṇām. mumukṣūṇāmapekṣyo'yamātmabodho vidhīyate || 1 ||
units.translation: I am composing Ātmabodha, this treatise of the Knowledge of the Self, for those who have purified themselves by austerities and are peaceful in heart and calm, who are free from cravings and are desirous of liberation.

units.sanskrit: बोधोऽन्यसाधनेभ्यो हि साक्षान्मोक्षैकसाधनम्। पाकस्य वह्निवज्ज्ञानं विना मोक्षो न सिध्यति ॥२॥
units.transliteration: bodho'nyasādhanebhyo hi sākṣānmokṣaikasādhanam. pākasya vahnivajjñānaṃ vinā mokṣo na sidhyati || 2 ||
units.translation: Just as the fire is the direct cause for cooking, so without Knowledge no emancipation can be had. Compared with all other forms of discipline Knowledge of the Self is the one direct means for liberation.

units.sanskrit: अविरोधितया कर्म नाविद्यां विनिवर्तयेत्। विद्याविद्यां निहन्त्येव तेजस्तिमिरसंघवत् ॥३॥
units.transliteration: avirodhitayā karma nāvidyāṃ vinivartayet. vidyāvidyāṃ nihantyeva tejastimirasaṃghavat || 3 ||
units.translation: Action cannot destroy ignorance, for it is not in conflict with or opposed to ignorance. Knowledge does verily destroy ignorance as light destroys deep darkness.

units.sanskrit: अवच्छिन्न इवाज्ञानात्तन्नाशे सति केवलः। स्वयं प्रकाशते ह्यात्मा मेघापायेंऽशुमानिव ॥४॥
units.transliteration: avacchinna ivājñānāttannāśe sati kevalaḥ. svayaṃ prakāśate hyātmā meghāpāyeṃ'śumāniva || 4 ||
units.translation: The Soul appears to be finite because of ignorance. When ignorance is destroyed the Self which does not admit of any multiplicity truly reveals itself by itself: like the Sun when the clouds pass away.

units.sanskrit: अज्ञानकलुषं जीवं ज्ञानाभ्यासाद्विनिर्मलम्। कृत्वा ज्ञानं स्वयं नश्येज्जलं कतकरेणुवत् ॥५॥
units.transliteration: ajñānakaluṣaṃ jīvaṃ jñānābhyāsādvinirmalam. kṛtvā jñānaṃ svayaṃ naśyejjalaṃ katakareṇuvat || 5 ||
units.translation: Constant practice of knowledge purifies the Self ('Jivatman'), stained by ignorance and then disappears itself - as the powder of the'Kataka-nut' settles down after it has cleansed the muddy water.

units.sanskrit: संसारः स्वप्नतुल्यो हि रागद्वेषादिसंकुलः। स्वकाले सत्यवद्भाति प्रबोधे सत्यसद्भवेत् ॥६॥
units.transliteration: saṃsāraḥ svapnatulyo hi rāgadveṣādisaṃkulaḥ. svakāle satyavadbhāti prabodhe satyasadbhavet || 6 ||
units.translation: The world which is full of attachments, aversions, etc., is like a dream. It appears to be real, as long as it continues but appears to be unreal when one is awake (i.e., when true wisdom dawns).

units.sanskrit: तावत्सत्यं जगद्भाति शुक्तिकारजतं यथा। यावन्न ज्ञायते ब्रह्म सर्वाधिष्ठानमद्वयम् ॥७॥
units.transliteration: tāvatsatyaṃ jagadbhāti śuktikārajataṃ yathā. yāvanna jñāyate brahma sarvādhiṣṭhānamadvayam || 7 ||
units.translation: The Jagat appears to be true (Satyam) so long as Brahman, the substratum, the basis of all this creation, is not realised. It is like the illusion of silver in the mother-of pearl.

units.sanskrit: उपादानेऽखिलाधारे जगन्ति परमेश्वरे। सर्गस्थितिलयान्यान्ति बुद्बुदानीव वारिणि ॥८॥
units.transliteration: upādāne'khilādhāre jaganti parameśvare. sargasthitilayānyānti budbudānīva vāriṇi || 8 ||
units.translation: Like bubbles in the water, the worlds rise, exist and dissolve in the Supreme Self, which is the material cause and the prop of everything.

units.sanskrit: सच्चिदात्मन्यनुस्यूते नित्ये विष्णौ प्रकल्पिताः। व्यक्तयो विविधाः सर्वा हाटके कटकादिवत् ॥९॥
units.transliteration: saccidātmanyanusyūte nitye viṣṇau prakalpitāḥ. vyaktayo vividhāḥ sarvā hāṭake kaṭakādivat || 9 ||
units.translation: All the manifested world of things and beings are projected by imagination upon the substratum which is the Eternal All-pervading Vishnu, whose nature is Existence-Intelligence; just as the different ornaments are all made out of the same gold.

units.sanskrit: यथाकाशो हृषीकेशो नानोपाधिगतो विभुः। तद्भेदाद्भिन्नवद्भाति तन्नाशे केवलो भवेत् ॥१०॥
units.transliteration: yathākāśo hṛṣīkeśo nānopādhigato vibhuḥ. tadbhedādbhinnavadbhāti tannāśe kevalo bhavet || 10 ||
units.translation: The All-pervading Akasa appears to be diverse on account of its association with various conditionings (Upadhis) which are different from each other. Space becomes one on the destruction of these limiting adjuncts: So also the Omnipresent Truth appears to be diverse on account of Its association with the various Upadhis and becomes one on the destruction of these Upadhis.

units.sanskrit: आविद्यकं शरीरादि दृश्यं बुद्बुदवत्क्षरम्। एतद्विलक्षणं विद्यादहं ब्रह्मेति निर्मलम् ॥३१॥
units.transliteration: āvidyakaṃ śarīrādi dṛśyaṃ budbudavatkṣaram. etadvilakṣaṇaṃ vidyādahaṃ brahmeti nirmalam || 31 ||
units.translation: The body, etc., up to the "Causal Body" - Ignorance - which are objects perceived, are as perishable as bubbles. Realise through discrimination that I am the'Pure Brahman' ever completely separate from all these.

units.sanskrit: देहान्यत्वान्न मे जन्मजराकार्श्यलयादयः। शब्दादिविषयैः सङ्गो निरिन्द्रियतया न च ॥३२॥
units.transliteration: dehānyatvānna me janmajarākārśyalayādayaḥ. śabdādiviṣayaiḥ saṅgo nirindriyatayā na ca || 32 ||
units.translation: I am other than the body and so I am free from changes such as birth, wrinkling, senility, death, etc. I have nothing to do with the sense objects such as sound and taste, for I am without the sense-organs.

units.sanskrit: अमनस्त्वान्न मे दुःखरागद्वेषभयादयः। अप्राणो ह्यमनाः शुभ्र इत्यादिश्रुतिशासनात् ॥३३॥
units.transliteration: amanastvānna me duḥkharāgadveṣabhayādayaḥ. aprāṇo hyamanāḥ śubhra ityādiśrutiśāsanāt || 33 ||
units.translation: I am other than the mind and hence, I am free from sorrow, attachment, malice and fear, for "HE is without breath and without mind, Pure, etc.", is the Commandment of the great scripture, the Upanishads.

units.sanskrit: निर्गुणो निष्क्रियो नित्यो निर्विकल्पो निरञ्जनः। निर्विकारो निराकारो नित्यमुक्तोऽस्मि निर्मलः ॥३४॥
units.transliteration: nirguṇo niṣkriyo nityo nirvikalpo nirañjanaḥ. nirvikāro nirākāro nityamukto'smi nirmalaḥ || 34 ||
units.translation: I am without attributes and actions; Eternal (Nitya) without any desire and thought (Nirvikalpa), without any dirt (Niranjana), without any change (Nirvikara), without form (Nirakara), ever-liberated (Nitya Mukta) ever-pure (Nirmala).

units.sanskrit: अहमाकाशवत्सर्वं बहिरन्तर्गतोऽच्युतः। सदा सर्वसमः सिद्धो निःसङ्गो निर्मलोऽचलः ॥३५॥
units.transliteration: ahamākāśavatsarvaṃ bahirantargato'cyutaḥ. sadā sarvasamaḥ siddho niḥsaṅgo nirmalo'calaḥ || 35 ||
units.translation: Like the space I fill all things within and without. Changeless and the same in all, at all times I am pure, unattached, stainless and motionless.

units.sanskrit: नित्यशुद्धविमुक्तैकमखण्डानन्दमद्वयम्। सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं यत्परं ब्रह्माहमेव तत् ॥३६॥
units.transliteration: nityaśuddhavimuktaikamakhaṇḍānandamadvayam. satyaṃ jñānamanantaṃ yatparaṃ brahmāhameva tat || 36 ||
units.translation: I am verily that Supreme Brahman alone which is Eternal, Pure and Free, One, indivisible and non-dual and of the nature of Changeless-Knowledge-Infinite.

units.sanskrit: एवं निरन्तरकृता ब्रह्मैवास्मीति वासना। हरत्यविद्याविक्षेपान्रोगानिव रसायनम् ॥३७॥
units.transliteration: evaṃ nirantarakṛtā brahmaivāsmīti vāsanā. haratyavidyāvikṣepānrogāniva rasāyanam || 37 ||
units.translation: The impression "I am Brahman" thus created by constant practice destroys ignorance and the agitation caused by it, just as medicine or Rasayana destroys disease.

units.sanskrit: विविक्तदेश आसीनो विरागो विजितेन्द्रियः। भावयेदेकमात्मानं तमनन्तमनन्यधीः ॥३८॥
units.transliteration: viviktadeśa āsīno virāgo vijitendriyaḥ. bhāvayedekamātmānaṃ tamanantamananyadhīḥ || 38 ||
units.translation: Sitting in a solitary place, freeing the mind from desires and controlling the senses, meditate with unswerving attention on the Atman which is One without-a-second.

units.sanskrit: आत्मन्येवाखिलं दृश्यं प्रविलाप्य धिया सुधीः। भावयेदेकमात्मानं निर्मलाकाशवत्सदा ॥३९॥
units.transliteration: ātmanyevākhilaṃ dṛśyaṃ pravilāpya dhiyā sudhīḥ. bhāvayedekamātmānaṃ nirmalākāśavatsadā || 39 ||
units.translation: The wise one should intelligently merge the entire world-of-objects in the Atman alone and constantly think of the Self ever as contaminated by anything as the sky.

units.sanskrit: रूपवर्णादिकं सर्वं विहाय परमार्थवित्। परिपूर्णचिदानन्दस्वरूपेणावतिष्ठते ॥४०॥
units.transliteration: rūpavarṇādikaṃ sarvaṃ vihāya paramārthavit. paripūrṇacidānandasvarūpeṇāvatiṣṭhate || 40 ||
units.translation: He who has realised the Supreme, discards all his identification with the objects of names and forms. (Thereafter) he dwells as an embodiment of the Infinite Consciousness and Bliss. He becomes the Self.

units.sanskrit: नानोपाधिवशादेव जातिनामाश्रमादयः। आत्मन्यारोपितास्तोये रसवर्णादिभेदवत् ॥११॥
units.transliteration: nānopādhivaśādeva jātināmāśramādayaḥ. ātmanyāropitāstoye rasavarṇādibhedavat || 11 ||
units.translation: Because of Its association with different conditionings (Upadhis) such ideas as caste, colour and position are super-imposed upon the Atman, as flavour, colour, etc., are super-imposed on water.

units.sanskrit: पञ्चीकृतमहाभूतसंभवं कर्मसंचितम्। शरीरं सुखदुःखानां भोगायतनमुच्यते ॥१२॥
units.transliteration: pañcīkṛtamahābhūtasaṃbhavaṃ karmasaṃcitam. śarīraṃ sukhaduḥkhānāṃ bhogāyatanamucyate || 12 ||
units.translation: Determined for each individual by his own past actions and made up of the Five elements - that have gone through the process of "five-fold self-division and mutual combination" (pañcīkaraṇa) - are born the gross-body, the medium through which pleasure and pain are experienced, the tent-of-experiences.

units.sanskrit: पञ्चप्राणमनोबुद्धिदशेन्द्रियसमन्वितम्। अपञ्चीकृतभूतोत्थं सूक्ष्माङ्गं भोगसाधनम् ॥१३॥
units.transliteration: pañcaprāṇamanobuddhidaśendriyasamanvitam. apañcīkṛtabhūtotthaṃ sūkṣmāṅgaṃ bhogasādhanam || 13 ||
units.translation: The five Pranas, the ten organs and the Manas and the Buddhi, formed from the rudimentary elements (Tanmatras) before their "five-fold division and mutual combination with one another" (pañcīkaraṇa) and this is the subtle body, the instruments-of-experience (of the individual).

units.sanskrit: अनाद्यविद्यानिर्वाच्या कारणोपाधिरुच्यते। उपाधित्रितयादन्यमात्मानमवधारयेत् ॥१४॥
units.transliteration: anādyavidyānirvācyā kāraṇopādhirucyate. upādhitritayādanyamātmānamavadhārayet || 14 ||
units.translation: Avidya which is indescribable and beginningless is the Causal Body. Know for certain that the Atman is other than these three conditioning bodies (Upadhis).

units.sanskrit: पञ्चकोशादियोगेन तत्तन्मय इव स्थितः। शुद्धात्मा नीलवस्त्रादियोगेन स्फटिको यथा ॥१५॥
units.transliteration: pañcakośādiyogena tattanmaya iva sthitaḥ. śuddhātmā nīlavastrādiyogena sphaṭiko yathā || 15 ||
units.translation: In its identification with the five-sheaths the Immaculate Atman appears to have borrowed their qualities upon Itself; as in the case of a crystal which appears to gather unto itself colour of its vicinity (blue cloth, etc.,).

units.sanskrit: वपुस्तुषादिभिः कोशैर्युक्तं युक्त्यवघाततः। आत्मानमान्तरं शुद्धं विविञ्च्यात्तण्डुलं यथा ॥१६॥
units.transliteration: vapustuṣādibhiḥ kośairyuktaṃ yuktyavaghātataḥ. ātmānamāntaraṃ śuddhaṃ viviñcyāttaṇḍulaṃ yathā || 16 ||
units.translation: Through discriminative self-analysis and logical thinking one should separate the Pure self within from the sheaths as one separates the rice from the husk, bran, etc., that are covering it.

units.sanskrit: सदा सर्वगतोऽप्यात्मा न सर्वत्रावभासते। बुद्धावेवावभासेत स्वच्छेषु प्रतिबिम्बवत् ॥१७॥
units.transliteration: sadā sarvagato'pyātmā na sarvatrāvabhāsate. buddhāvevāvabhāseta svaccheṣu pratibimbavat || 17 ||
units.translation: The Atman does not shine in everything although He is All-pervading. He is manifest only in the inner equipment, the intellect (Buddhi): just as the reflection in a clean mirror.

units.sanskrit: देहेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिप्रकृतिभ्यो विलक्षणम्। तद्वृत्तिसाक्षिणं विद्यादात्मानं राजवत्सदा ॥१८॥
units.transliteration: dehendriyamanobuddhiprakṛtibhyo vilakṣaṇam. tadvṛttisākṣiṇaṃ vidyādātmānaṃ rājavatsadā || 18 ||
units.translation: One should understand that the Atman is always like the King, distinct from the body, senses, mind and intellect, all of which constitute the matter (Prakriti); and is the witness of their functions.

units.sanskrit: व्यापृतेष्विन्द्रियेष्वात्मा व्यापारीवाविवेकिनाम्। दृश्यतेऽभ्रेषु धावत्सु धावन्निव यथा शशी ॥१९॥
units.transliteration: vyāpṛteṣvindriyeṣvātmā vyāpārīvāvivekinām. dṛśyate'bhreṣu dhāvatsu dhāvanniva yathā śaśī || 19 ||
units.translation: The moon appears to be running when the clouds move in the sky. Likewise to the non-discriminating person the Atman appears to be active when It is observed through the functions of the sense-organs.

units.sanskrit: आत्मचैतन्यमाश्रित्य देहेन्द्रियमनोधियः। स्वकीयार्थेषु वर्तन्ते सूर्यालोकं यथा जनाः ॥२०॥
units.transliteration: ātmacaitanyamāśritya dehendriyamanodhiyaḥ. svakīyārtheṣu vartante sūryālokaṃ yathā janāḥ || 20 ||
units.translation: Depending upon the energy of vitality of Consciousness (Atma Chaitanya) the body, senses, mind and intellect engage themselves in their respective activities, just as men work depending upon the light of the Sun.

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच ऊर्ध्वमूलमध:शाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् । छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | ūrdhva-mūlam adhaḥ-śākham aśvatthaṁ prāhur avyayam | chandāṁsi yasya parṇāni yas taṁ veda sa veda-vit || 1 ||
units.translation: They say that the Peepul Tree, which has its roots upward and the branches downward, and of which the Vedas are the leaves, is imperishable. He who realizes it is knower of the Vedas.

units.sanskrit: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवाला: । अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: adhaś cordhvaṁ prasṛtās tasya śākhā guṇa-pravṛddhā viṣaya-pravālāḥ | adhaś ca mūlāny anusantatāni karmānubandhīni manuṣya-loke || 2 ||
units.translation: The branches of that (Tree), extending downwards and upwards, are strengthened by the qualities and have sense-objects as their shoots. And the roots, which are followed by actions, spread downwards in the human world.

units.sanskrit: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते नान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा । अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलमसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: na rūpam asyeha tathopalabhyate nānto na cādir na ca sampratiṣṭhā | aśvattham enaṁ su-virūḍha-mūlam asaṅga-śastreṇa dṛḍhena chittvā || 3 ||
units.translation: Its form is not perceived here in that way; nor its end, nor beginning, nor continuance. After felling this Peepul whose roots are well developed, with the strong sword of detachment—

units.sanskrit: तत: पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यं यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूय: । तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये यत: प्रवृत्ति: प्रसृता पुराणी ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: tataḥ padaṁ tat parimārgitavyaṁ yasmin gatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥ | tam eva cādyaṁ puruṣaṁ prapadye yataḥ pravṛttiḥ prasṛtā purāṇī || 4 ||
units.translation: Thereafter, that State has to be sought for, going where they do not return again: I take refuge in that Primeval Person Himself, from whom has ensued the eternal Manifestation.

units.sanskrit: निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामा: । द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ता: सुखदु:खसंज्ञैर्गच्छन्त्यमूढा: पदमव्ययं तत् ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: nirmāna-mohā jita-saṅga-doṣā adhyātma-nityā vinivṛtta-kāmāḥ | dvandvair vimuktāḥ sukha-duḥkha-saṁjñair gacchanty amūḍhāḥ padam avyayaṁ tat || 5 ||
units.translation: The wise ones who are free from pride and non-discrimination, who have conquered the evil of association, who are ever devoted to spirituality, completely free from desires, free from the dualities called happiness and sorrow, reach that undecaying State.

units.sanskrit: न तद्भ‍ासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावक: । यद्ग‍त्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: na tad bhāsayate sūryo na śaśāṅko na pāvakaḥ | yad gatvā na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṁ mama || 6 ||
units.translation: Neither the sun nor the moon nor fire illumines That. That is My supreme Abode, reaching which they do not return.

units.sanskrit: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूत: सनातन: । मन:षष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: mamaivāṁśo jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ | manaḥ-ṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛti-sthāni karṣati || 7 ||
units.translation: An eternal portion of Myself becomes the living self in the world of beings, drawing to itself the senses, including the mind, which abide in nature, draws (to itself) the organs which have the mind as their sixth, and which abide in Nature.

units.sanskrit: शरीरं यदवाप्‍नोति यच्च‍ाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वर: । गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: śarīraṁ yad avāpnoti yac cāpy utkrāmatīśvaraḥ | gṛhītvaitāni saṁyāti vāyur gandhān ivāśayāt || 8 ||
units.translation: When the master leaves it and even when he assumes a body, he departs taking these, as wind (carries away) odours from their receptacles.

units.sanskrit: श्रोत्रं चक्षु: स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च । अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: śrotraṁ cakṣuḥ sparśanaṁ ca rasanaṁ ghrāṇam eva ca | adhiṣṭhāya manaś cāyaṁ viṣayān upasevate || 9 ||
units.translation: This one enjoys the objects by presiding over the ear, eyes, skin and tongue as also the nose and the mind.

units.sanskrit: उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वाऽपि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम् । विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुष: ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: utkrāmantaṁ sthitaṁ vāpi bhuñjānaṁ vā guṇānvitam | vimūḍhā nānupaśyanti paśyanti jñāna-cakṣuṣaḥ || 10 ||
units.translation: Persons who are diversely deluded do not see it even when it is leaving or residing (in this body), or experiencing, or in association with the qualities. Those with the eye of knowledge see.

units.sanskrit: यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् । यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतस: ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: yatanto yoginaś cainaṁ paśyanty ātmany avasthitam | yatanto ’py akṛtātmāno nainaṁ paśyanty acetasaḥ || 11 ||
units.translation: And the yogīs who are diligent see this one as existing in themselves. The non-discriminating ones who lack self-control—though (they be) diligent—do not see this one.

units.sanskrit: यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भ‍ासयतेऽखिलम् । यच्च‍न्द्रमसि यच्च‍ाग्न‍ौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: yad āditya-gataṁ tejo jagad bhāsayate ’khilam | yac candramasi yac cāgnau tat tejo viddhi māmakam || 12 ||
units.translation: That light in the sun which illumines the whole world, that which is in the moon, and that which is in fire—know that light to be Mine.

units.sanskrit: गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा । पुष्णामि चौषधी: सर्वा: सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मक: ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: gām āviśya ca bhūtāni dhārayāmy aham ojasā | puṣṇāmi cauṣadhīḥ sarvāḥ somo bhūtvā rasātmakaḥ || 13 ||
units.translation: And entering the earth I sustain the beings through (My) power; and nourish all the plants by becoming Soma, which is of the nature of sap.

units.sanskrit: अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रित: । प्राणापानसमायुक्त: पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: ahaṁ vaiśvānaro bhūtvā prāṇināṁ deham āśritaḥ | prāṇāpāna-samāyuktaḥ pacāmy annaṁ catur-vidham || 14 ||
units.translation: Taking the form of Vaiśvānara and residing in the bodies of creatures, I, in association with Prāṇa and Apāṇa, digest the four kinds of food.

units.sanskrit: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो मत्त: स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च । वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: sarvasya cāhaṁ hṛdi sanniviṣṭo mattaḥ smṛtir jñānam apohanaṁ ca | vedaiś ca sarvair aham eva vedyo vedānta-kṛd veda-vid eva cāham || 15 ||
units.translation: And I am seated in the hearts of all. From Me are memory, knowledge and their loss. I alone am the object to be known through all the Vedas; I am also the originator of the Vedānta, and I Myself am the knower of the Vedas.

units.sanskrit: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च । क्षर: सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: dvāv imau puruṣau loke kṣaraś cākṣara eva ca | kṣaraḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni kūṭa-stho ’kṣara ucyate || 16 ||
units.translation: There are these two persons in the world—the mutable and the immutable. The mutable consists of all things; the immutable is called the unchanging witness.

units.sanskrit: उत्तम: पुरुषस्त्वन्य: परमात्मेत्युदाहृत: । यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वर: ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: uttamaḥ puruṣas tv anyaḥ paramātmety udāhṛtaḥ | yo loka-trayam āviśya bibharty avyaya īśvaraḥ || 17 ||
units.translation: But different is the supreme Person who is spoken of as the transcendental Self, who, permeating the three worlds, upholds (them), and is the imperishable Bhagavān.

units.sanskrit: यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तम: । अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथित: पुरुषोत्तम: ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: yasmāt kṣaram atīto ’ham akṣarād api cottamaḥ | ato ’smi loke vede ca prathitaḥ puruṣottamaḥ || 18 ||
units.translation: Since I am transcendental to the mutable and above even the immutable, hence I am well known in the world and in the Vedas as the supreme Person.

units.sanskrit: यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम् । स सर्वविद्भ‍जति मां सर्वभावेन भारत ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: yo mām evam asammūḍho jānāti puruṣottamam | sa sarva-vid bhajati māṁ sarva-bhāvena bhārata || 19 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bhārata dynasty, he who, being free from delusion, knows Me the supreme Person thus, he is all-knowing and adores Me with his whole being.

units.sanskrit: इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयानघ । एतद्‍बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: iti guhya-tamaṁ śāstram idam uktaṁ mayānagha | etad buddhvā buddhimān syāt kṛta-kṛtyaś ca bhārata || 20 ||
units.translation: O sinless one, this most secret teaching has thus been uttered by Me. Understanding this, one becomes wise and has his duties fulfilled, O scion of the Bhārata dynasty

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच परं भूय: प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् । ‍‍ ॥ यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनय: सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गता: ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | paraṁ bhūyaḥ pravakṣyāmi jñānānāṁ jñānam uttamam | yaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṁ siddhim ito gatāḥ || 1 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: I shall speak again of the supreme Knowledge, the best of all knowledge, by realizing which all the contemplatives reached the highest Perfection from here.

units.sanskrit: इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागता: । सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: idaṁ jñānam upāśritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ | sarge ’pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti ca || 2 ||
units.translation: Those who attain identity with Me by resorting to this Knowledge are not born even during creation, nor do they suffer pain during dissolution.

units.sanskrit: मम योनिर्महद्‍ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् । सम्भव: सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy aham | sambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhārata || 3 ||
units.translation: My womb is the great-sustainer. In that I place the seed. From that, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, occurs the birth of all things.

units.sanskrit: सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तय: सम्भवन्ति या: । तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रद: पिता ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: sarva-yoniṣu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ | tāsāṁ brahma mahad yonir ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā || 4 ||
units.translation: O son of Kuntī, whatever forms are born from all the wombs, of them the great-sustainer is the womb; I am the father who deposits the seed.

units.sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणा: प्रकृतिसम्भवा: । निबध्‍नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: sattvaṁ rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛti-sambhavāḥ | nibadhnanti mahā-bāho dehe dehinam avyayam || 5 ||
units.translation: O mighty-armed one, the guṇas, viz. sattva, rajas and  tamas, born of Nature, being the immutable embodied being to the body.

units.sanskrit: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् । सुखसङ्गेन बध्‍नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: tatra sattvaṁ nirmalatvāt prakāśakam anāmayam | sukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena cānagha || 6 ||
units.translation: Among them, sattva, being pure, is an illuminator and is harmless. O sinless one, it binds through attachment to happiness and knowledge.

units.sanskrit: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भ‍वम् । तन्निबध्‍नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: rajo rāgātmakaṁ viddhi tṛṣṇā-saṅga-samudbhavam | tan nibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam || 7 ||
units.translation: Know rajas to be of the nature of passion, born of hankering and attachment. O son of Kuntī, that binds the embodied one through attachment to action.

units.sanskrit: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् । प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्‍नाति भारत ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: tamas tv ajñāna-jaṁ viddhi mohanaṁ sarva-dehinām | pramādālasya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata || 8 ||
units.translation: On the other hand, know tamas, which deludes all embodied beings, to be born of ignorance. O scion of the Bharata dynasty, that binds through inadvertence, laziness and sleep.

units.sanskrit: सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रज: कर्मणि भारत । ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तम: प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: sattvaṁ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata | jñānam āvṛtya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta || 9 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bhārata dynasty, sattva attaches one to happiness, rajas to action, while tamas, covering up knowledge, leads to inadvertence also.

units.sanskrit: रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत । रज: सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तम: सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: rajas tamaś cābhibhūya sattvaṁ bhavati bhārata | rajaḥ sattvaṁ tamaś caiva tamaḥ sattvaṁ rajas tathā || 10 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bhārata dynasty, sattva increases by subduing rajas and tamas, rajas by overpowering sattva and tamas, and tamas by dominating over sattva and rajas.

units.sanskrit: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते । ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: sarva-dvāreṣu dehe ’smin prakāśa upajāyate | jñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛddhaṁ sattvam ity uta || 11 ||
units.translation: When the illumination that is knowledge radiates in this body through all the doors (of the senses), then one should know that sattva has increased greatly.

units.sanskrit: लोभ: प्रवृत्तिरारम्भ: कर्मणामशम: स्पृहा । रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: lobhaḥ pravṛttir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśamaḥ spṛhā | rajasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe bharatarṣabha || 12 ||
units.translation: O best of the Bhārata dynasty, when rajas becomes predominant, these come into being: avarice, movement, undertaking of actions, unrest and hankering.

units.sanskrit: अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च । तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: aprakāśo ’pravṛttiś ca pramādo moha eva ca | tamasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe kuru-nandana || 13 ||
units.translation: O descendant of the Kuru dynasty, when tamas predominates these surely come into being: non-discrimination and inactivity, inadvertence and delusion.

units.sanskrit: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् । तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: yadā sattve pravṛddhe tu pralayaṁ yāti deha-bhṛt | tadottama-vidāṁ lokān amalān pratipadyate || 14 ||
units.translation: When an embodied one undergoes death while sattva is exclusively predominant, then he attains the taintless worlds of those who know the highest (truth).

units.sanskrit: रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते । तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: rajasi pralayaṁ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣu jāyate | tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣu jāyate || 15 ||
units.translation: When one dies while rajas predominates, he is born among people attached to activity. Similarly, when one dies while tamas predominates, he takes birth among the stupid species.

units.sanskrit: कर्मण: सुकृतस्याहु: सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् । रजसस्तु फलं दु:खमज्ञानं तमस: फलम् ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: karmaṇaḥ sukṛtasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṁ nirmalaṁ phalam | rajasas tu phalaṁ duḥkham ajñānaṁ tamasaḥ phalam || 16 ||
units.translation: They say that the result of good work is pure and is born of sattva. But the result of rajas is sorrow; the result of tamas is ignorance.

units.sanskrit: सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च । प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: sattvāt sañjāyate jñānaṁ rajaso lobha eva ca | pramāda-mohau tamaso bhavato ’jñānam eva ca || 17 ||
units.translation: From sattva is born knowledge, and from rajas, verily, avarice. From tamas are born inadvertence and delusion as also ignorance, to be sure.

units.sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसा: । जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसा: ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: ūrdhvaṁ gacchanti sattva-sthā madhye tiṣṭhanti rājasāḥ | jaghanya-guṇa-vṛtti-sthā adho gacchanti tāmasāḥ || 18 ||
units.translation: People who conform to sattva go higher up; those who conform to rajas stay in the middle; those who conform to tamas, who conform to the actions of the lowest quality, go down.

units.sanskrit: नान्यं गुणेभ्य: कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति । गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भ‍ावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: nānyaṁ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṁ yadā draṣṭānupaśyati | guṇebhyaś ca paraṁ vetti mad-bhāvaṁ so ’dhigacchati || 19 ||
units.translation: When the witness sees none other than the guṇas as the agent, and knows that which is superior to the guṇas, he attains My nature.

units.sanskrit: गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भ‍वान् । जन्ममृत्युजरादु:खैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्न‍ुते ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: guṇān etān atītya trīn dehī deha-samudbhavān | janma-mṛtyu-jarā-duḥkhair vimukto ’mṛtam aśnute || 20 ||
units.translation: Having transcended these three guṇas which are the origin of the body, the embodied one, becoming free from birth, death, old age and sorrows, experiences Immortality.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो । किमाचार: कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | kair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabho | kim-ācāraḥ kathaṁ caitāṁs trīn guṇān ativartate || 21 ||
units.translation: Arjuna said: O Bhagavān, by what signs is one (known) who has gone beyond these three guṇas? What is his behaviour, and how does he transcend these three guṇas?

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव । न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्‍क्षति ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | prakāśaṁ ca pravṛttiṁ ca moham eva ca pāṇḍava | na dveṣṭi sampravṛttāni na nivṛttāni kāṅkṣati || 22 ||
units.translation: O son of Pandu, he neither dislikes illumination (knowledge), activity and delusion when they appear, nor does he long for them when they disappear.

units.sanskrit: उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते । गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: udāsīna-vad āsīno guṇair yo na vicālyate | guṇā vartanta ity evaṁ yo ’vatiṣṭhati neṅgate || 23 ||
units.translation: He who, sitting like one indifferent, is not distracted by the three guṇas; he who, thinking that the guṇas alone act, remains firm and surely does not move;

units.sanskrit: समदु:खसुख: स्वस्थ: समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चन: । तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुति: ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣṭāśma-kāñcanaḥ | tulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-saṁstutiḥ || 24 ||
units.translation: He to whom sorrow and happiness are alike, who is established in his own Self, to whom a lump of earth, iron and gold are the same, to whom the agreeable and the disagreeable are the same, who is wise, to whom censure and his own praise are the same;

units.sanskrit: मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयो: । सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीत: स उच्यते ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: mānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāri-pakṣayoḥ | sarvārambha-parityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa ucyate || 25 ||
units.translation: He who is the same under honour and dishonour, who is equally disposed both towards the side of the friend and of the foe, who has renounced all enterprise—he is said to have gone beyond the guṇas.

units.sanskrit: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते । स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: māṁ ca yo ’vyabhicāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevate | sa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate || 26 ||
units.translation: And he who serves Me through the unswerving Yoga of Devotion, he, having gone beyond these guṇas, qualifies for becoming Brahman.

units.sanskrit: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च । शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: brahmaṇo hi pratiṣṭhāhamn amṛtasyāvyayasya ca | śāśvatasya ca dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya ca || 27 ||
units.translation: For I am the Abode of Brahman—the indestructible and immutable, the eternal, the Dharma and absolute Bliss.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच सन्न्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि । यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम् ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | sannyāsaṁ karmaṇāṁ kṛṣṇa punar yogaṁ ca śaṁsasi | yac chreya etayor ekaṁ tan me brūhi su-niścitam || 1 ||
units.translation: O Kṛṣṇa, You praise renunciation of actions, and again, (Karma-)Yoga. Tell me for certain which is better.

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच सन्न्यास: कर्मयोगश्च नि:श्रेयसकरावुभौ । तयोस्तु कर्मसन्न्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | sannyāsaḥ karma-yogaś ca niḥśreyasa-karāv ubhau | tayos tu karma-sannyāsāt karma-yogo viśiṣyate || 2 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: Both renunciation of actions and Karma-Yoga lead to Liberation. Of the two, karma-yoga is superior to renunciation of action.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञेय: स नित्यसन्न्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्‍क्षति । निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: jñeyaḥ sa nitya-sannyāsī yo na dveṣṭi na kāṅkṣati | nirdvandvo hi mahā-bāho sukhaṁ bandhāt pramucyate || 3 ||
units.translation: He who does not hate and does not crave should be known as a man of constant renunciation. For, O mighty-armed one, he who is free from duality becomes easily freed from bondage.

units.sanskrit: सां‍ख्ययोगौ पृथग्बाला: प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिता: । एकमप्यास्थित: सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: sāṅkhya-yogau pṛthag bālāḥ pravadanti na paṇḍitāḥ | ekam apy āsthitaḥ samyag ubhayor vindate phalam || 4 ||
units.translation: The fools, not the learned ones, speak of Sānkhya (the path of Knowledge) and (Karma-) Yoga as different. Anyone who properly resorts to even one (of them) gets the result of both.

units.sanskrit: यत्सां‍ख्यै: प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते । एकं सां‍ख्यं च योगं च य: पश्यति स पश्यति ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: yat sāṅkhyaiḥ prāpyate sthānaṁ tad yogair api gamyate | ekaṁ sāṅkhyaṁ ca yogaṁ ca yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati || 5 ||
units.translation: The State that is reached by the Sānkhyas is reached by the yogīs as well. He sees who sees Sānkhya and Yoga as one.

units.sanskrit: सन्न्यासस्तु महाबाहो दु:खमाप्‍तुमयोगत: । योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म न चिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: sannyāsas tu mahā-bāho duḥkham āptum ayogataḥ | yoga-yukto munir brahma na cireṇādhigacchati || 6 ||
units.translation: But, O mighty-armed one, renunciation is hard to attain without (Karma-)Yoga. The meditative man equipped with Yoga attains Brahman without delay.

units.sanskrit: योगयुक्तो विश‍ुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रिय: । सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: yoga-yukto viśuddhātmā vijitātmā jitendriyaḥ | sarva-bhūtātma-bhūtātmā kurvann api na lipyate || 7 ||
units.translation: Endowed with Yoga, pure in mind, controlled in body, a conqueror of the senses, the Self of the selves of all beings—he does not become tainted even while performing actions.

units.sanskrit: नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् । पश्यञ्श‍ृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्न‍न्गच्छन्स्वपन्श्वसन् ॥ ८ ॥ प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: naiva kiñcit karomīti yukto manyeta tattva-vit | paśyañ śṛṇvan spṛśañ jighrann aśnan gacchan svapañ śvasan || 8 || pralapan visṛjan gṛhṇann unmiṣan nimiṣann api | indriyāṇīndriyārtheṣu vartanta iti dhārayan || 9 ||
units.translation: Remaining absorbed in the Self, the knower of Reality should think, ‘I certainly do not do anything’, even while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, breathing, speaking, releasing, holding, opening and closing the eyes—remembering that the sense-organs function in relation to their objects.

units.sanskrit: ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति य: । लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: brahmaṇy ādhāya karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā karoti yaḥ | lipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā || 10 ||
units.translation: One who acts by dedicating actions to Brahman and by renouncing attachment does not become polluted by sin, just as a lotus leaf is not by water.

units.sanskrit: कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि । योगिन: कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्म‍श‍ुद्धये ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: kāyena manasā buddhyā kevalair indriyair api | yoginaḥ karma kurvanti saṅgaṁ tyaktvātma-śuddhaye || 11 ||
units.translation: By giving up attachment, the yogīs undertake work merely through the body, mind, intellect and even the organs, for self-purification.

units.sanskrit: युक्त: कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्‍नोति नैष्ठिकीम् । अयुक्त: कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: yuktaḥ karma-phalaṁ tyaktvā śāntim āpnoti naiṣṭhikīm | ayuktaḥ kāma-kāreṇa phale sakto nibadhyate || 12 ||
units.translation: Giving up the result of work by becoming resolute in faith, one attains Peace arising from steadfastness. One who is lacking in resolute faith, being attached to the result under the impulsion of desire, becomes bound.

units.sanskrit: सर्वकर्माणि मनसा सन्न्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी । नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: sarva-karmāṇi manasā sannyasyāste sukhaṁ vaśī | nava-dvāre pure dehī naiva kurvan na kārayan || 13 ||
units.translation: The embodied man of self-control, having given up all actions mentally, continues happily in the town of nine gates, without doing or causing (others) to do anything at all.

units.sanskrit: न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभु: । न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: na kartṛtvaṁ na karmāṇi lokasya sṛjati prabhuḥ | na karma-phala-saṁyogaṁ svabhāvas tu pravartate || 14 ||
units.translation: The Self does not create agentship or any objects (of desire) for anyone; nor association with the results of actions. But it is Nature that acts.

units.sanskrit: नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभु: । अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तव: ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: nādatte kasyacit pāpaṁ na caiva sukṛtaṁ vibhuḥ | ajñānenāvṛtaṁ jñānaṁ tena muhyanti jantavaḥ || 15 ||
units.translation: The Omnipresent neither accepts anybody’s sin nor even virtue. Knowledge remains covered by ignorance. Thereby the creatures become deluded.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मन: । तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: jñānena tu tad ajñānaṁ yeṣāṁ nāśitam ātmanaḥ | teṣām āditya-vaj jñānaṁ prakāśayati tat param || 16 ||
units.translation: But in the case of those of whom that ignorance of theirs becomes destroyed by the knowledge (of the Self), their Knowledge, like the sun, reveals that supreme Reality.

units.sanskrit: तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणा: । गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषा: ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: tad-buddhayas tad-ātmānas tan-niṣṭhās tat-parāyaṇāḥ | gacchanty apunar-āvṛttiṁ jñāna-nirdhūta-kalmaṣāḥ || 17 ||
units.translation: Those who have their intellect absorbed in That, whose Self is That, who are steadfast in That, who have That as their supreme Goal—they attain the state of non-returning, their dirt having been removed by Knowledge.

units.sanskrit: विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि । श‍ुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिता: समदर्शिन: ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini | śuni caiva śva-pāke ca paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśinaḥ || 18 ||
units.translation: The learned ones look with equanimity on a Brāhmana endowed with learning and humility, a cow, an elephant and even a dog as well as a dog-eater.

units.sanskrit: इहैव तैर्जित: सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मन: । निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद्‍ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिता: ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: ihaiva tair jitaḥ sargo yeṣāṁ sāmye sthitaṁ manaḥ | nirdoṣaṁ hi samaṁ brahma tasmād brahmaṇi te sthitāḥ || 19 ||
units.translation: Here itself is rebirth conquered by them whose minds are established on sameness. Since Brahman is the same (in all) and free from defects, therefore they are established in Brahman.

units.sanskrit: न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् । स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद्‍ब्रह्मणि स्थित: ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: na prahṛṣyet priyaṁ prāpya nodvijet prāpya cāpriyam | sthira-buddhir asammūḍho brahma-vid brahmaṇi sthitaḥ || 20 ||
units.translation: A knower of Brahman, who is established in Brahman, should have his intellect steady and should not be deluded. He should not get delighted by getting what is desirable, nor become dejected by getting what is undesirable.

units.sanskrit: बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् । स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्न‍ुते ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: bāhya-sparśeṣv asaktātmā vindaty ātmani yat sukham | sa brahma-yoga-yuktātmā sukham akṣayam aśnute || 21 ||
units.translation: With his heart unattached to external objects, he gets bliss in the Self. With his heart absorbed in meditation on Brahman, he acquires undecaying Bliss.

units.sanskrit: ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दु:खयोनय एव ते । आद्यन्तवन्त: कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुध: ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: ye hi saṁsparśa-jā bhogā duḥkha-yonaya eva te | ādy-antavantaḥ kaunteya na teṣu ramate budhaḥ || 22 ||
units.translation: Since enjoyments that result from contact (with objects) are verily the sources of sorrow and have a beginning and an end, (therefore) O son of Kuntī, the wise one does not delight in them.
