units.sanskrit: शक्न‍ोतीहैव य: सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् । कामक्रोधोद्भ‍वं वेगं स युक्त: स सुखी नर: ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: śaknotīhaiva yaḥ soḍhuṁ prāk śarīra-vimokṣaṇāt | kāma-krodhodbhavaṁ vegaṁ sa yuktaḥ sa sukhī naraḥ || 23 ||
units.translation: One who can withstand here itself—before departing from the body—the impulse arising from desire and anger, that man is a yogī and is happy.

units.sanskrit: योऽन्त:सुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव य: । स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: yo ’ntaḥ-sukho ’ntar-ārāmas tathāntar-jyotir eva yaḥ | sa yogī brahma-nirvāṇaṁ brahma-bhūto ’dhigacchati || 24 ||
units.translation: One who is happy within, whose pleasure is within, and who has his light only within, that yogī, having become Brahman, attains absorption in Brahman.

units.sanskrit: लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषय: क्षीणकल्मषा: । छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मान: सर्वभूतहिते रता: ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: labhante brahma-nirvāṇam ṛṣayaḥ kṣīṇa-kalmaṣāḥ | chinna-dvaidhā yatātmānaḥ sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ || 25 ||
units.translation: The seers whose sins have been attenuated, who are freed from doubt, whose senses are under control, who are engaged in doing good to all beings, attain absorption in Brahman.

units.sanskrit: कामक्रोधविमुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् । अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: kāma-krodha-vimuktānāṁ yatīnāṁ yata-cetasām | abhito brahma-nirvāṇaṁ vartate viditātmanām || 26 ||
units.translation: To the monks who have control over their internal organ, who are free from desire and anger, who have known the Self, brahma-nirvāṇa is present on all sides for them.

units.sanskrit: स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवो: । प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥ २७ ॥ यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायण: । विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो य: सदा मुक्त एव स: ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: sparśān kṛtvā bahir bāhyāṁś cakṣuś caivāntare bhruvoḥ | prāṇāpānau samau kṛtvā nāsābhyantara-cāriṇau || 27 || yatendriya-mano-buddhir munir mokṣa-parāyaṇaḥ | vigatecchā-bhaya-krodho yaḥ sadā mukta eva saḥ || 28 ||
units.translation: Keeping the external objects outside, the eyes at the juncture of the eye-brows, and making equal the outgoing and incoming breaths that move through the nostrils, the contemplative who has control over his organs, mind and intellect should be fully intent on Liberation and free from desire, fear and anger. He who is  ever thus is verily free.

units.sanskrit: भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् । सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: bhoktāraṁ yajña-tapasāṁ sarva-loka-maheśvaram | suhṛdaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ jñātvā māṁ śāntim ṛcchati || 29 ||
units.translation: One attains Peace by knowing Me who, as the great Bhagavān of all the worlds, am the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, (and) who am the friend of all creatures.

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच | इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम् । विवस्वान्मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत् ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | imaṁ vivasvate yogaṁ proktavān aham avyayam | vivasvān manave prāha manur ikṣvākave ’bravīt || 1 ||
units.translation: I imparted this imperishable Yoga to Vivasvān. Vivasvān taught this to Manu, and Manu transmitted this to Ikṣvāku.

units.sanskrit: एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः । स कालेनेह महता योगे नष्टः परन्तप ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: evaṁ paramparā-prāptam imaṁ rājarṣayo viduḥ | sa kāleneha mahatā yogo naṣṭaḥ paran-tapa || 2 ||
units.translation: The king-sages knew this (Yoga) which was received thus in regular succession. That Yoga, O destroyer of foes, in now lost owing to a long lapse of time.

units.sanskrit: स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः । भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: sa evāyaṁ mayā te ’dya yogaḥ proktaḥ purātanaḥ | bhakto ’si me sakhā ceti rahasyaṁ hy etad uttamam || 3 ||
units.translation: That ancient Yog itself, which is this, has been taught to you by Me today, considering that you are My devotee and friend. For, this (Yoga) is a profound secret.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः । कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | aparaṁ bhavato janma paraṁ janma vivasvataḥ | katham etad vijānīyāṁ tvam ādau proktavān iti || 4 ||
units.translation: Arjuna said: Your birth was later, (whereas) the birth of Vivasvān was earlier. How am I to understand this that You instructed (him) in the beginning?

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन । तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | bahūni me vyatītāni janmāni tava cārjuna | tāny ahaṁ veda sarvāṇi na tvaṁ vettha paran-tapa || 5 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, many lives of Mine have passed, and so have yours. I know them all, (but) you know not, O scorcher of enemies!

units.sanskrit: अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन् । प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय सम्भवाम्यात्ममायया ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: ajo ’pi sann avyayātmā bhūtānām īśvaro ’pi san | prakṛtiṁ svām adhiṣṭhāya sambhavāmy ātma-māyayā || 6 ||
units.translation: Though I am birthless, undecaying by nature, and the Bhagavān of beings, (still) by subjugating My Prakṛti, I take birth by means of My own Māyā.

units.sanskrit: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत । अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata | abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛjāmy aham || 7 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bharata dynasty, whenever there is a decline of virtue and increase of vice, then do I manifest Myself.

units.sanskrit: परित्राणाय साधुनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् । धर्मसंस्थानार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām | dharma-saṁsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge || 8 ||
units.translation: For the protection of the pious, the destruction of the evildoers, and establishing virtue, I manifest Myself in every age.

units.sanskrit: जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः । त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: janma karma ca me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ | tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna || 9 ||
units.translation: He who thus knows truly the divine birth and actions of Mine does not get rebirth after casting off the body. He attains Me, O Arjuna.

units.sanskrit: वीतरागभयक्रोधा मन्मया मामुपाश्रिताः । बहवो ज्ञानतपसा पूता मद्भ‍ावमागताः ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhā man-mayā mām upāśritāḥ | bahavo jñāna-tapasā pūtā mad-bhāvam āgatāḥ || 10 ||
units.translation: Many who were devoid of attachment, fear and anger, who were absorbed in Me, who had taken refuge in Me, and were purified by the austerity of Knowledge, have attained My state.

units.sanskrit: ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम् । मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: ye yathā māṁ prapadyante tāṁs tathaiva bhajāmy aham | mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ || 11 ||
units.translation: According to the manner in which they approach Me, I favour them in that very manner. O son of Prthā, human beings follow My path in every way.

units.sanskrit: काङ्क्षन्तः कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवता । क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: kāṅkṣantaḥ karmaṇāṁ siddhiṁ yajanta iha devatāḥ | kṣipraṁ hi mānuṣe loke siddhir bhavati karma-jā || 12 ||
units.translation: Longing for the fruition of actions (of their rites and duties), they worship the gods here. For, in the human world, success from action comes quickly.

units.sanskrit: चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः । तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: cātur-varṇyaṁ mayā sṛṣṭaṁ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśaḥ | tasya kartāram api māṁ viddhy akartāram avyayam || 13 ||
units.translation: The four castes have been created by Me through a classification of the guṇas and duties. Even though I am the agent of that (act of classification), still know Me to be a non-agent and changeless.

units.sanskrit: न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा । इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: na māṁ karmāṇi limpanti na me karma-phale spṛhā | iti māṁ yo ’bhijānāti karmabhir na sa badhyate || 14 ||
units.translation: Actions do not taint Me; for Me there is no hankering for the results of actions. One who knows Me thus, does not become bound by actions.

units.sanskrit: एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः । कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम् ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: evaṁ jñātvā kṛtaṁ karma pūrvair api mumukṣubhiḥ | kuru karmaiva tasmāt tvaṁ pūrvaiḥ pūrva-taraṁ kṛtam || 15 ||
units.translation: Having known thus, duties were performed even by the ancient seekers of Liberation. Therefore you undertake action itself as was performed earlier by the ancient ones.

units.sanskrit: किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः । तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽश‍ुभात् ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: kiṁ karma kim akarmeti kavayo ’py atra mohitāḥ | tat te karma pravakṣyāmi yaj jñātvā mokṣyase ’śubhāt || 16 ||
units.translation: Even the intelligent are confounded as to what is action and what is inaction. I shall tell you of that action by knowing which you will become free from evil.

units.sanskrit: कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मणः । अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गतिः ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: karmaṇo hy api boddhavyaṁ boddhavyaṁ ca vikarmaṇaḥ | akarmaṇaś ca boddhavyaṁ gahanā karmaṇo gatiḥ || 17 ||
units.translation: For there is something to be known even about action, and something to be known about prohibited action; and something has to be known about inaction. The true nature of action is inscrutable.

units.sanskrit: कर्मण्यकर्म यः पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म यः । स बुद्धिमान्मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्मकृत् ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: karmaṇy akarma yaḥ paśyed akarmaṇi ca karma yaḥ | sa buddhimān manuṣyeṣu sa yuktaḥ kṛtsna-karma-kṛt || 18 ||
units.translation: He who finds inaction in action, and action in inaction, he is the wise one among men; he is engaged in Yoga and is a performer of all actions!

units.sanskrit: यस्य सर्वे समारम्भाः कामसंकल्पवर्जिताः । ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: yasya sarve samārambhāḥ kāma-saṅkalpa-varjitāḥ | jñānāgni-dagdha-karmāṇaṁ tam āhuḥ paṇḍitaṁ budhāḥ || 19 ||
units.translation: The wise call him learned whose actions are all devoid of desires and their thoughts, and whose actions have been burnt away by the fire of wisdom.

units.sanskrit: त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः । कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: tyaktvā karma-phalāsaṅgaṁ nitya-tṛpto nirāśrayaḥ | karmaṇy abhipravṛtto ’pi naiva kiñcit karoti saḥ || 20 ||
units.translation: Having given up attachment to the results of action, he who is ever-contented, dependent on nothing, he really does not do anything even though engaged in action.

units.sanskrit: निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः । शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्‍नोति किल्बिषम् ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: nirāśīr yata-cittātmā tyakta-sarva-parigrahaḥ | śārīraṁ kevalaṁ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣam || 21 ||
units.translation: One who is without solicitation, who has the mind and sense-organs under control, (and) is totally without possessions, he incurs no sin by performing actions merely for the (maintenance of the) body.

units.sanskrit: यदृच्छालाभसंतुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सरः । समः सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: yadṛcchā-lābha-santuṣṭo dvandvātīto vimatsaraḥ | samaḥ siddhāv asiddhau ca kṛtvāpi na nibadhyate || 22 ||
units.translation: Remaining satisfied with what comes unasked for, having transcended the dualities, being free from spite, and equipoised in success and failure, he is not bound even by performing actions.

units.sanskrit: गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः । यज्ञायाचरतः कर्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: gata-saṅgasya muktasya jñānāvasthita-cetasaḥ | yajñāyācarataḥ karma samagraṁ pravilīyate || 23 ||
units.translation: Of the liberated person who has got rid of attachment, whose mind is fixed in Knowledge, actions undertaken for sacrifice get totally destroyed.

units.sanskrit: ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्न‍ौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम् । ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: brahmārpaṇaṁ brahma havir brahmāgnau brahmaṇā hutam | brahmaiva tena gantavyaṁ brahma-karma-samādhinā || 24 ||
units.translation: The ladle is Brahman, the oblation is Brahman, the offering is poured by Brahman in the fire of Brahman. Brahman alone is to be reached by him who has concentration on Brahman as the objective.

units.sanskrit: दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्युपासते । ब्रह्माग्न‍ावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: daivam evāpare yajñaṁ yoginaḥ paryupāsate | brahmāgnāv apare yajñaṁ yajñenaivopajuhvati || 25 ||
units.translation: Other yogīs undertake sacrifice to gods alone. Others offer the Self, as a sacrifice by the Self itself, in the fire of Brahman.

units.sanskrit: श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्न‍िषु जुह्वति । शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्न‍िषु जुह्वति ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: śrotrādīnīndriyāṇy anye saṁyamāgniṣu juhvati | śabdādīn viṣayān anya indriyāgniṣu juhvati || 26 ||
units.translation: Others offer the sense-organs, namely ear etc., in the fires of self-control. Others offer the objects, namely sound etc., in the fires of the sense-organs.

units.sanskrit: सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे । आत्मसंयमयोगाग्न‍ौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: sarvāṇīndriya-karmāṇi prāṇa-karmāṇi cāpare | ātma-saṁyama-yogāgnau juhvati jñāna-dīpite || 27 ||
units.translation: Others offer all the activities of the sense-organs and the vital force into the fire of the Yoga of self-control, which has been lighted by Knowledge.

units.sanskrit: द्रव्ययज्ञास्तपोयज्ञा योगयज्ञास्तथापरे । स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाश्च यतयः संशितव्रताः ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: dravya-yajñās tapo-yajñā yoga-yajñās tathāpare | svādhyāya-jñāna-yajñāś ca yatayaḥ saṁśita-vratāḥ || 28 ||
units.translation: Similarly, others are performers of sacrifices through wealth, through austerity, through Yoga, and through study and knowledge; others are ascetics with severe vows.

units.sanskrit: अपाने जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणेऽपानं तथापरे । प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: apāne juhvati prāṇaṁ prāṇe ’pānaṁ tathāpare | prāṇāpāna-gatī ruddhvā prāṇāyāma-parāyaṇāḥ || 29 ||
units.translation: Constantly practising control of the vital forces by stopping the movements of the outgoing and the incoming breaths, some offer as a sacrifice the outgoing breath in the incoming breath; while still others, the incoming breath in the outgoing breath.

units.sanskrit: अपरे नियताहाराः प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति | सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः ॥ ३० ॥
units.transliteration: apare niyatāhārāḥ prāṇān prāṇeṣu juhvati | sarve ’py ete yajña-vido yajña-kṣapita-kalmaṣāḥ || 30 ||
units.translation: Others, having their food regulated, offer the vital forces in the vital forces. All of them are knowers of the sacrifice and have their sins destroyed by sacrifice.

units.sanskrit: यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम् | नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतोऽन्यः कुरुसत्तम ॥ ३१ ॥
units.transliteration: yajña-śiṣṭāmṛta-bhujo yānti brahma sanātanam | nāyaṁ loko ’sty ayajñasya kuto ’nyaḥ kuru-sattama || 31 ||
units.translation: Those who partake of the nectar left over after a sacrifice, reach the eternal Brahman. This world ceases to exist for one who does not perform sacrifices. What to speak of the other (world), O best among the Kurus (Arjuna)!

units.sanskrit: एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे । कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे ॥ ३२ ॥
units.transliteration: evaṁ bahu-vidhā yajñā vitatā brahmaṇo mukhe | karma-jān viddhi tān sarvān evaṁ jñātvā vimokṣyase || 32 ||
units.translation: Thus, various kinds of sacrifices lie spread at the mouth of the Vedas. Know them all to be born of action. Knowing thus, you will become liberated.

units.sanskrit: श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप । सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते ॥ ३३ ॥
units.transliteration: śreyān dravya-mayād yajñāj jñāna-yajñaḥ paran-tapa | sarvaṁ karmākhilaṁ pārtha jñāne parisamāpyate || 33 ||
units.translation: O destroyer of enemies, the sacrifice of Knowledge is considered greater than sacrifices requiring materials. O son of Prthā, all actions in their totality culminate in Knowledge.

units.sanskrit: तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्न‍ेन सेवया । उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ॥ ३४ ॥
units.transliteration: tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśnena sevayā | upadekṣyanti te jñānaṁ jñāninas tattva-darśinaḥ || 34 ||
units.translation: Know That through prostration, inquiry and service. The wise ones who have realized the Truth will impart the Knowledge to you.

units.sanskrit: यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव । येन भूतान्यशेषाणि द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि ॥ ३५ ॥
units.transliteration: yaj jñātvā na punar moham evaṁ yāsyasi pāṇḍava | yena bhūtāny aśeṣāṇi drakṣyasy ātmany atho mayi || 35 ||
units.translation: Knowing which, O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), you will not come under delusion again in this way, and through which you will see all beings without exception in the Self and also in Me.

units.sanskrit: अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः । सर्वं ज्ञानप्ल‍वेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि ॥ ३६ ॥
units.transliteration: api ced asi pāpebhyaḥ sarvebhyaḥ pāpa-kṛt-tamaḥ | sarvaṁ jñāna-plavenaiva vṛjinaṁ santariṣyasi || 36 ||
units.translation: Even if you be the worst sinner among all sinners, still you will cross over all wickedness with the raft of Knowledge alone.

units.sanskrit: यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्न‍िर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन । ज्ञानाग्न‍िः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा ॥ ३७ ॥
units.transliteration: yathaidhāṁsi samiddho ’gnir bhasma-sāt kurute ’rjuna | jñānāgniḥ sarva-karmāṇi bhasma-sāt kurute tathā || 37 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, as a blazing fire reduces pieces of wood to ashes, similarly the fire of Knowledge reduces all actions to ashes.

units.sanskrit: न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते । तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति ॥ ३८ ॥
units.transliteration: na hi jñānena sadṛśaṁ pavitram iha vidyate | tat svayaṁ yoga-saṁsiddhaḥ kālenātmani vindati || 38 ||
units.translation: Indeed, there is nothing purifying here comparable to Knowledge. One who has become perfected after a (long) time through Yoga, realizes That by himself in his own heart.

units.sanskrit: श्रद्धावाँल्ल‍भते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः । ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥ ३९ ॥
units.transliteration: śraddhāvāḻ labhate jñānaṁ tat-paraḥ saṁyatendriyaḥ | jñānaṁ labdhvā parāṁ śāntim acireṇādhigacchati || 39 ||
units.translation: The man who has faith, is diligent and has control over the sense-organs, attains Knowledge. Achieving Knowledge, one soon attains supreme Peace.

units.sanskrit: अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति । नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः ॥ ४० ॥
units.transliteration: ajñaś cāśraddadhānaś ca saṁśayātmā vinaśyati | nāyaṁ loko ’sti na paro na sukhaṁ saṁśayātmanaḥ || 40 ||
units.translation: One who is ignorant and faithless, and has a doubting mind perishes. Neither this world nor the next nor happiness exists for one who has a doubting mind.

units.sanskrit: योगसन्न्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसञ्छिन्नसंशयम् । आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्न‍‍न्ति धनञ्जय ॥ ४१ ॥
units.transliteration: yoga-sannyasta-karmāṇaṁ jñāna-sañchinna-saṁśayam | ātmavantaṁ na karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañ-jaya || 41 ||
units.translation: O Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), actions do not bind one who has renounced actions through Yoga, whose doubt has been fully dispelled by Knowledge, and who is not careless.

units.sanskrit: तस्मादज्ञानसम्भूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनात्मनः । छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत ॥ ४२ ॥
units.transliteration: tasmād ajñāna-sambhūtaṁ hṛt-sthaṁ jñānāsinātmanaḥ | chittvainaṁ saṁśayaṁ yogam ātiṣṭhottiṣṭha bhārata || 42 ||
units.translation: Therefore, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, take recourse to Yoga and rise up, cutting asunder with the sword of Knowledge this doubt of your own in the heart, arising from ignorance.

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच मय्यासक्तमना: पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रय: । असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | mayy āsakta-manāḥ pārtha yogaṁ yuñjan mad-āśrayaḥ | asaṁśayaṁ samagraṁ māṁ yathā jñāsyasi tac chṛṇu || 1 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: O Pārtha, hear how you, having the mind fixed on Me, practising the Yoga of Meditation and taking refuge in Me, will know Me with certainty and in fullness.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषत: । यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: jñānaṁ te ’haṁ sa-vijñānam idaṁ vakṣyāmy aśeṣataḥ | yaj jñātvā neha bhūyo ’nyaj jñātavyam avaśiṣyate || 2 ||
units.translation: I shall tell you in detail of this Knowledge which is combined with realization, after experiencing which there remains nothing else here to be known again.

units.sanskrit: मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये । यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वत: ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: manuṣyāṇāṁ sahasreṣu kaścid yatati siddhaye | yatatām api siddhānāṁ kaścin māṁ vetti tattvataḥ || 3 ||
units.translation: Among thousands of men, a rare one endeavours for perfection. Even of the perfected ones who are diligent, one perchance knows Me in truth

units.sanskrit: भूमिरापोऽनलो वायु: खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च । अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: bhūmir āpo ’nalo vāyuḥ khaṁ mano buddhir eva ca | ahaṅkāra itīyaṁ me bhinnā prakṛtir aṣṭadhā || 4 ||
units.translation: This Prakṛti of Mine is divided eight-fold thus: earth, water, fire, air, space, mind, intellect and also egoism.

units.sanskrit: अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् । जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: apareyam itas tv anyāṁ prakṛtiṁ viddhi me parām | jīva-bhūtāṁ mahā-bāho yayedaṁ dhāryate jagat || 5 ||
units.translation: O mighty-armed one, this is the inferior (Prakṛti). Know the other Prakṛti of Mine which, however, is higher than this, which has taken the form of individual Ātman(s), and by which this world is upheld.

units.sanskrit: एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय । अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगत: प्रभव: प्रलयस्तथा ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇīty upadhāraya | ahaṁ kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā || 6 ||
units.translation: Understand thus that all things (sentient and insentient) have these as their source. I am the origin as also the end of the whole Universe.

units.sanskrit: मत्त: परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय । मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñcid asti dhanañ-jaya | mayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva || 7 ||
units.translation: O Dhanañjaya, there is nothing else whatsoever higher than Myself. All this is strung on Me like pearls on a string.

units.sanskrit: रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययो: । प्रणव: सर्ववेदेषु शब्द: खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śaśi-sūryayoḥ | praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣu śabdaḥ khe pauruṣaṁ nṛṣu || 8 ||
units.translation: O son of Kuntī, I am the taste of water, I am the effulgence of the moon and the sun; (the letter) ĀŪṀ in all the Vedas, sound in space, and manhood in men.

units.sanskrit: पुण्यो गन्ध: पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ । जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛthivyāṁ ca tejaś cāsmi vibhāvasau | jīvanaṁ sarva-bhūteṣu tapaś cāsmi tapasviṣu || 9 ||
units.translation: I am also the sweet fragrance in the earth; I am the brilliance in fire, and the life in all beings; and I am the austerity of the ascetics.

units.sanskrit: बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् । बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam | buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham || 10 ||
units.translation: O Pārtha, know Me to be the eternal Seed of all beings. I am the intellect of the intelligent, I am the courage of the courageous.

units.sanskrit: बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम् । धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: balaṁ balavatāṁ cāhaṁ kāma-rāga-vivarjitam | dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu kāmo ’smi bharatarṣabha || 11 ||
units.translation: And of the strong I am the strength which is devoid of passion and attachment. Among creatures I am desire which is not contrary to righteousness, O scion of the Bhārata dynasty.

units.sanskrit: ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये । मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: ye caiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasās tāmasāś ca ye | matta eveti tān viddhi na tv ahaṁ teṣu te mayi || 12 ||
units.translation: Those things that indeed are made of (the quality of) sattva, and those things that are made of (the quality of) rājas and tamas, know them to have sprung from Me alone. However, I am not in  them; they are in Me!

units.sanskrit: त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभि: सर्वमिदं जगत् । मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्य: परमव्ययम् ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: tribhir guṇa-mayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idaṁ jagat | mohitaṁ nābhijānāti mām ebhyaḥ param avyayam || 13 ||
units.translation: All this world, deluded as it is by these three things made of the guṇas (qualities), does not know Me who am transcendental to these and undecaying.

units.sanskrit: दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया । मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayā | mām eva ye prapadyante māyām etāṁ taranti te || 14 ||
units.translation: Since this divine Māyā of Mine which is constituted by the guṇas is difficult to cross over, (therefore) those who take refuge in Me alone cross over this Māyā.

units.sanskrit: न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढा: प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमा: । माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिता: ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: na māṁ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ | māyayāpahṛta-jñānā āsuraṁ bhāvam āśritāḥ || 15 ||
units.translation: The foolish evildoers, who are the most depraved among men, who are deprived of (their) wisdom by Māyā, and who resort to demoniacal ways, do not take refuge in Me.

units.sanskrit: चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जना: सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन । आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: catur-vidhā bhajante māṁ janāḥ su-kṛtino ’rjuna | ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī ca bharatarṣabha || 16 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, foremost of the Bhārata dynasty, four classes of people of virtuous deeds adore Me: the afflicted, the seeker of Knowledge, the seeker of wealth and the man of Knowledge.

units.sanskrit: तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते । प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रिय: ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: teṣāṁ jñānī nitya-yukta eka-bhaktir viśiṣyate | priyo hi jñānino ’tyartham ahaṁ sa ca mama priyaḥ || 17 ||
units.translation: Of them, the man of Knowledge, endowed with constant steadfastness and one-pointed devotion, excels. For I am very much dear to the man of Knowledge, and he too is dear to Me.

units.sanskrit: उदारा: सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् । आस्थित: स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tv ātmaiva me matam | āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamāṁ gatim || 18 ||
units.translation: All of these, indeed, are noble, but the man of Knowledge is the very Self. (This is) My opinion. For, with a steadfast mind, he is set on the path leading to Me alone who am the super-excellent Goal.

units.sanskrit: बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते । वासुदेव: सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभ: ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: bahūnāṁ janmanām ante jñānavān māṁ prapadyate | vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā su-durlabhaḥ || 19 ||
units.translation: At the end of many births the man of Knowledge attains Me, (realizing) that Vāsudeva is all. Such a high Ātman is very rare.

units.sanskrit: कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञाना: प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवता: । तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियता: स्वया ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: kāmais tais tair hṛta-jñānāḥ prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥ | taṁ taṁ niyamam āsthāya prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā || 20 ||
units.translation: People, deprived of their wisdom by desires for various objects and guided by their own nature, resort to other deities following the relevant methods.

units.sanskrit: यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्त: श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति । तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: yo yo yāṁ yāṁ tanuṁ bhaktaḥ śraddhayārcitum icchati | tasya tasyācalāṁ śraddhāṁ tām eva vidadhāmy aham || 21 ||
units.translation: Whichever form (of a deity) any devotee wants to worship with faith, that very firm faith of his I strengthen.

units.sanskrit: स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते । लभते च तत: कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: sa tayā śraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhate | labhate ca tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān || 22 ||
units.translation: Being imbued with that faith, that person engages in worshipping that form, and he gets those very desired results therefrom as they are dispensed by Me alone.

units.sanskrit: अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भ‍वत्यल्पमेधसाम् । देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भ‍क्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: antavat tu phalaṁ teṣāṁ tad bhavaty alpa-medhasām | devān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api || 23 ||
units.translation: That result of theirs who are of poor intellect is indeed limited. The worshippers of gods go to the gods. My devotees go to Me alone.

units.sanskrit: अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धय: । परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: avyaktaṁ vyaktim āpannaṁ manyante mām abuddhayaḥ| paraṁ bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam || 24 ||
units.translation: The unintelligent, unaware of My supreme state which is immutable and unsurpassable, think of Me as the unmanifest that has become manifest.

units.sanskrit: नाहं प्रकाश: सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृत: । मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: nāhaṁ prakāśaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥ | mūḍho ’yaṁ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam || 25 ||
units.translation: Being enveloped by Yoga-māyā, I do not become manifest to all. This deluded world does not know Me who am birthless and undecaying.

units.sanskrit: वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन । भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: vedāhaṁ samatītāni vartamānāni cārjuna | bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni māṁ tu veda na kaścana || 26 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, I know the past, the present, as also the future beings; but no one knows Me!

units.sanskrit: इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत । सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: icchā-dveṣa-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhārata | sarva-bhūtāni sammohaṁ sarge yānti paran-tapa || 27 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bhārata dynasty, O destroyer of foes, due to the delusion of duality arising from likes and dislikes, all creatures become bewildered at the time of their birth.

units.sanskrit: येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् । ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रता: ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: yeṣāṁ tv anta-gataṁ pāpaṁ janānāṁ puṇya-karmaṇām | te dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante māṁ dṛḍha-vratāḥ || 28 ||
units.translation: On the other hand, those persons who are of virtuous deeds, whose sin has come to an end, they, being free from the delusion of duality and firm in their convictions, adore Me.

units.sanskrit: जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये । ते ब्रह्म तद्विदु: कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम् ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: jarā-maraṇa-mokṣāya mām āśritya yatanti ye | te brahma tad viduḥ kṛtsnam adhyātmaṁ karma cākhilam || 29 ||
units.translation: Those who strive by resorting to Me for becoming free from old age and death, they know that Brahman, everything about the individual Self, and all about actions.

units.sanskrit: साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदु: । प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतस: ॥ ३० ॥
units.transliteration: sādhibhūtādhidaivaṁ māṁ sādhiyajñaṁ ca ye viduḥ | prayāṇa-kāle ’pi ca māṁ te vidur yukta-cetasaḥ || 30 ||
units.translation: Those who know Me as existing in the physical and the divine planes, and also in the context of the sacrifice, they of concentrated minds know Me even at the time of death.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच । ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन । तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | jyāyasī cet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdana | tat kiṁ karmaṇi ghore māṁ niyojayasi keśava || 1 ||
units.translation: Arjuna said: O Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), if it be Your opinion that wisdom is superior to action, why then do you urge me to horrible action, O Keśava?

units.sanskrit: व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे । तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्‍नुयाम् ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: vyāmiśreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva me | tad ekaṁ vada niścitya yena śreyo ’ham āpnuyām || 2 ||
units.translation: You bewilder my understanding, as it were, by a seemingly conflicting statement! Tell me for certain one of these by which I may attain the highest Good.

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ । ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | loke ’smin dvi-vidhā niṣṭhā purā proktā mayānagha | jñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṁ karma-yogena yoginām || 3 ||
units.translation: Kṛṣṇa said: O unblemished one, two kinds of steadfastness in this world were spoken of by Me in the days of yore—through the Yoga of Knowledge for the men of realization; through the Yoga of Action for  the yogīs.

units.sanskrit: न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्य पुरुषोऽश्न‍ुते । न च सन्न्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣkarmyaṁ puruṣo ’śnute | na ca sannyasanād eva siddhiṁ samadhigacchati || 4 ||
units.translation: A person does not attain freedom from action by abstaining from action; nor does he attain fulfilment merely through renunciation.

units.sanskrit: न हि कश्चित्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत् । कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: na hi kaścit kṣaṇam api jātu tiṣṭhaty akarma-kṛt | kāryate hy avaśaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛti-jair guṇaiḥ || 5 ||
units.translation: Because no one ever remains even for a moment without doing work. For all are made to work under compulsion by the guṇas born of Nature.

units.sanskrit: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् । इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: karmendriyāṇi saṁyamya ya āste manasā smaran | indriyārthān vimūḍhātmā mithyācāraḥ sa ucyate || 6 ||
units.translation: One who, after withdrawing the organs of action, sits mentally recollecting the objects of the senses, that one of deluded mind is called a hypocrite.

units.sanskrit: यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन । कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: yas tv indriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate ’rjuna | karmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśiṣyate || 7 ||
units.translation: But, O Arjuna, one who engages in Karma-Yoga with the organs of action, controlling the sense organs with the mind and becoming unattached—that one excels.

units.sanskrit: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः । शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hy akarmaṇaḥ | śarīra-yātrāpi ca te na prasidhyed akarmaṇaḥ || 8 ||
units.translation: Perform the obligatory duties, for action is superior to inaction. And, through inaction, even the maintenance of your body will not be possible.

units.sanskrit: यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः । तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्गः समाचर ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: yajñārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ | tad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya mukta-saṅgaḥ samācara || 9 ||
units.translation: Actions other than those performed for the sake of sacrifice cause bondage in this world.

units.sanskrit: सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः । अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक् ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: saha-yajñāḥ prajāḥ sṛṣṭvā purovāca prajāpatiḥ | anena prasaviṣyadhvam eṣa vo ’stv iṣṭa-kāma-dhuk || 10 ||
units.translation: In the days of yore, having created the beings together with the sacrifices, Prajāpati said: ‘By this you multiply. Let this be your yielder of coveted objects of desire.’

units.sanskrit: देवान्भावयतातेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः । परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: devān bhāvayatānena te devā bhāvayantu vaḥ | parasparaṁ bhāvayantaḥ śreyaḥ param avāpsyatha || 11 ||
units.translation: ‘You nourish the gods with this. Let those gods nourish you. Nourishing one another, you shall attain the supreme Good.’

units.sanskrit: इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः । तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: iṣṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajña-bhāvitāḥ | tair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ || 12 ||
units.translation: ‘Being nourished by sacrifices, the gods will indeed give you the coveted enjoyments. He is certainly a thief who enjoys what have been given by them without offering (these) to them.’

units.sanskrit: यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषै । भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: yajña-śiṣṭāśinaḥ santo mucyante sarva-kilbiṣaiḥ | bhuñjate te tv aghaṁ pāpā ye pacanty ātma-kāraṇāt || 13 ||
units.translation: By becoming partakers of the remnants of sacrifices, they become freed from all sins. But the unholy persons who cook for themselves, they incur sin.

units.sanskrit: अन्नाद्भ‍वन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः । यज्ञा‍द्भ‍‍वति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भ‍वः ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥ | yajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ || 14 ||
units.translation: From food are born the creatures; the origin of food is from rainfall; rainfall originates from sacrifice; sacrifice has action as its origin.

units.sanskrit: कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भ‍वं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भ‍वम् । तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: karma brahmodbhavaṁ viddhi brahmākṣara-samudbhavam | tasmāt sarva-gataṁ brahma nityaṁ yajñe pratiṣṭhitam || 15 ||
units.translation: Know that action has the Vedas as its origin; the Vedas have the Immutable as its source. Hence, the all-pervading Veda is forever based on sacrifice.

units.sanskrit: एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः । अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: evaṁ pravartitaṁ cakraṁ nānuvartayatīha yaḥ | aghāyur indriyārāmo moghaṁ pārtha sa jīvati || 16 ||
units.translation: O Pārtha, he lives in vain who does not follow here the wheel thus set in motion, whose life is sinful, and who indulges in the senses.

units.sanskrit: यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्त‍श्च मानवः । आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: yas tv ātma-ratir eva syād ātma-tṛptaś ca mānavaḥ | ātmany eva ca santuṣṭas tasya kāryaṁ na vidyate || 17 ||
units.translation: But that man who rejoices only in the Self and is satisfied with the Self, and is contented only in the Self—for him there is no duty to perform.

units.sanskrit: नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन । न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्चिदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: naiva tasya kṛtenārtho nākṛteneha kaścana | na cāsya sarva-bhūteṣu kaścid artha-vyapāśrayaḥ || 18 ||
units.translation: For him there is no concern here at all with performing action; nor any (concern) with non-performance. Moreover, for him there is no dependence on any object to serve any purpose.

units.sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर । असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्‍नोति पूरूषः ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samācara | asakto hy ācaran karma param āpnoti pūruṣaḥ || 19 ||
units.translation: Therefore, remaining unattached, always perform the obligatory duty, for by performing (one’s) duty without attachment, a person attains the Highest.

units.sanskrit: कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः । लोकसङ्ग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: karmaṇaiva hi saṁsiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ | loka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśyan kartum arhasi || 20 ||
units.translation: For Janaka and others strove to attain Liberation through action itself. You ought to perform (your duties) keeping also in view the prevention of mankind from going astray.

units.sanskrit: यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः । स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: yad yad ācarati śreṣṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ | sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate || 21 ||
units.translation: Whatever a superior person does, another person does that very thing! Whatever he upholds as authority, an ordinary person follows that.

units.sanskrit: न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन । नानवाप्त‍मवाप्त‍व्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: na me pārthāsti kartavyaṁ triṣu lokeṣu kiñcana | nānavāptam avāptavyaṁ varta eva ca karmaṇi || 22 ||
units.translation: In all the three worlds, O Pārtha, there is no duty whatsoever for Me (to fulfil); nothing remains unachieved or to be achieved. (Still) do I continue in action.

units.sanskrit: यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः । मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: yadi hy ahaṁ na varteyaṁ jātu karmaṇy atandritaḥ | mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ || 23 ||
units.translation: For, O Pārtha, if at any time I do not continue vigilantly in action, men will follow My path in every way.

units.sanskrit: उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम् । सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryāṁ karma ced aham | saṅkarasya ca kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ || 24 ||
units.translation: These worlds will be ruined if I do not perform action. And I shall become the agent of intermingling (of castes), and shall be destroying these beings.

units.sanskrit: सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत । कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्चिकीर्षुर्लोकसङ्‍‍ग्रहम् ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: saktāḥ karmaṇy avidvāṁso yathā kurvanti bhārata | kuryād vidvāṁs tathāsaktaś cikīrṣur loka-saṅgraham || 25 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bhārata dynasty, as the unenlightened people act with attachment to work, so should the enlightened person act, but without attachment, desiring to prevent people from going astray.

units.sanskrit: न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम् । जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान्युक्तः समाचरन् ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: na buddhi-bhedaṁ janayed ajñānāṁ karma-saṅginām | joṣayet sarva-karmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācaran || 26 ||
units.translation: The enlightened man should not create disturbance in the beliefs of the ignorant, who are attached to work. Working, while himself remaining diligent, he should make them do all the  duties.

units.sanskrit: प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः । अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ | ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate || 27 ||
units.translation: While actions are being done in every way by the guṇas (qualities) of Nature, one who is deluded by egoism thinks thus: ‘I am the doer.’

units.sanskrit: तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः । गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्ज‍ते ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: tattva-vit tu mahā-bāho guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ | guṇā guṇeṣu vartanta iti matvā na sajjate || 28 ||
units.translation: But, O mighty-armed one, the one who is a knower of the facts about the varieties of the guṇas (qualities) and actions does not become attached, thinking thus: ‘The sense organs rest (act) on their objects.’

units.sanskrit: प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढाः सज्ज‍न्ते गुणकर्मसु । तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत् ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: prakṛter guṇa-sammūḍhāḥ sajjante guṇa-karmasu | tān akṛtsna-vido mandān kṛtsna-vin na vicālayet || 29 ||
units.translation: Those who are wholly deluded by the guṇas of Nature become attached to the activities of the guṇas. The knower of the All should not disturb those of dull intellect, who do not know the All.

units.sanskrit: मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि सन्न्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा । निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥ ३० ॥
units.transliteration: mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-cetasā | nirāśīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ || 30 ||
units.translation: Surrendering all actions to Me, with your mind centered on the Self, free from expectation and egoism, fight without agitation.

units.sanskrit: ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः । श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः ॥ ३१ ॥
units.transliteration: ye me matam idaṁ nityam anutiṣṭhanti mānavāḥ | śraddhāvanto ’nasūyanto mucyante te ’pi karmabhiḥ || 31 ||
units.translation: Those men who ever follow this teaching of Mine with faith and without cavil, they also become freed from actions.

units.sanskrit: ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम् । सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः ॥ ३२ ॥
units.transliteration: ye tv etad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣṭhanti me matam | sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāṁs tān viddhi naṣṭān acetasaḥ || 32 ||
units.translation: But those who, decrying this, do not follow My teaching, know them—who are deluded about all knowledge and who are devoid of discrimination—to have gone to ruin.

units.sanskrit: सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि । प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति ॥ ३३ ॥
units.transliteration: sadṛśaṁ ceṣṭate svasyāḥ prakṛter jñānavān api | prakṛtiṁ yānti bhūtāni nigrahaḥ kiṁ kariṣyati || 33 ||
units.translation: Even a man of wisdom behaves according to his own nature. Beings follow (their) nature. What can restraint do?

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ । तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ ॥ ३४ ॥
units.transliteration: indriyasyendriyasyārthe rāga-dveṣau vyavasthitau | tayor na vaśam āgacchet tau hy asya paripanthinau || 34 ||
units.translation: Attraction and repulsion are ordained with regard to the objects of all the sense organs. One should not come under the sway of these two, because they are his adversaries.

units.sanskrit: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् । स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः ॥ ३५ ॥
units.transliteration: śreyān sva-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣṭhitāt | sva-dharme nidhanaṁ śreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ || 35 ||
units.translation: One’s own duty, though defective, is superior to another’s duty well-performed. Death is better while engaged in one’s own duty; (performing) another’s duty is fraught with fear.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः । अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः ॥ ३६ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | atha kena prayukto ’yaṁ pāpaṁ carati pūruṣaḥ | anicchann api vārṣṇeya balād iva niyojitaḥ || 36 ||
units.translation: Arjuna said: Now then, O scion of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty (Kṛṣṇa), impelled by what does this man commit sin even against his wish, as if constrained by force?

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भ‍वः । महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥ ३७ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | kāma eṣa krodha eṣa rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ | mahāśano mahā-pāpmā viddhy enam iha vairiṇam || 37 ||
units.translation: This desire, this anger, born of the quality of rājas, is a great devourer, a great sinner. Know this to be the enemy here.

units.sanskrit: धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथादर्शो मलेन च । यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृत्तम् ॥ ३८ ॥
units.transliteration: dhūmenāvriyate vahnir yathādarśo malena ca | yatholbenāvṛto garbhas tathā tenedam āvṛtam || 38 ||
units.translation: As fire is enveloped by smoke, as a mirror by dirt, and as a foetus remains enclosed in the womb, so is this shrouded by that.

units.sanskrit: आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा । कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च ॥ ३९ ॥
units.transliteration: āvṛtaṁ jñānam etena jñānino nitya-vairiṇā | kāma-rūpeṇa kaunteya duṣpūreṇānalena ca || 39 ||
units.translation: O son of Kuntī, Knowledge is covered by this constant enemy of the wise in the form of desire, which is an insatiable fire.

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते । एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम् ॥ ४० ॥
units.transliteration: indriyāṇi mano buddhir asyādhiṣṭhānam ucyate | etair vimohayaty eṣa jñānam āvṛtya dehinam || 40 ||
units.translation: The sense organs, mind, and the intellect are said to be its abode. This one diversely deludes the embodied being by veiling Knowledge with the help of these.

units.sanskrit: तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ । पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम् ॥ ४१ ॥
units.transliteration: tasmāt tvam indriyāṇy ādau niyamya bharatarṣabha | pāpmānaṁ prajahi hy enaṁ jñāna-vijñāna-nāśanam || 41 ||
units.translation: Therefore, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, after first controlling the sense organs, renounce this one which is sinful and a destroyer of learning and wisdom.

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः । मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः ॥ ४२ ॥
units.transliteration: indriyāṇi parāṇy āhur indriyebhyaḥ paraṁ manaḥ | manasas tu parā buddhir yo buddheḥ paratas tu saḥ || 42 ||
units.translation: They say that the sense organs are superior (to the gross body); the mind is superior to the sense organs; but the intellect is superior to the mind. However, the one who is superior to the intellect is He.

units.sanskrit: एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना । जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम् ॥ ४३ ॥
units.transliteration: evaṁ buddheḥ paraṁ buddhvā saṁstabhyātmānam ātmanā | jahi śatruṁ mahā-bāho kāma-rūpaṁ durāsadam || 43 ||
units.translation: Thus knowing the Self to be superior to the intellect, steady the mind by the Self, and slay the enemy in the form of desire, O mighty-armed one, which is difficult to conquer.

units.sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच । तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम् । विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: sañjaya uvāca | taṁ tathā kṛpayāviṣṭam aśru-pūrṇākulekṣaṇam | viṣīdantam idaṁ vākyam uvāca madhusūdanaḥ || 1 ||
units.translation: Sañjaya said: To him who had been thus filled with pity, whose eyes were filled with tears and showed distress, and who was sorrowing, Madhusudan uttered these words:

units.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच । कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् । अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यकीर्तिकरमर्जुन ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | kutas tvā kaśmalam idaṁ viṣame samupasthitam | anārya-juṣṭam asvargyam akīrti-karam arjuna || 2 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, in this perilous place, whence has come to you this impurity entertained by unenlightened persons, which does not lead to heaven and which brings infamy?

units.sanskrit: क्ल‍ैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते । क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: klaibyaṁ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayy upapadyate | kṣudraṁ hṛdaya-daurbalyaṁ tyaktvottiṣṭha paran-tapa || 3 ||
units.translation: O Pārtha, yield not to unmanliness. This does not befit you. O scorcher of foes, arise, giving up the petty weakness of the heart.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच । कथं भीष्ममहं संख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन । इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | kathaṁ bhīṣmam ahaṁ saṅkhye droṇaṁ ca madhusūdana | iṣubhiḥ pratiyotsyāmi pūjārhāv ari-sūdana || 4 ||
units.translation: Arjuna said: O Madhusudan, O destroyer of enemies, how can I fight with arrows in battle against Bhishma and Drona who are worthy of adoration?

units.sanskrit: गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान् श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके । हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव भुज्ज‍ीय भोगान्‍रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: gurūn ahatvā hi mahānubhāvān śreyo bhoktuṁ bhaikṣyam apīha loke | hatvārtha-kāmāṁs tu gurūn ihaiva bhuñjīya bhogān rudhira-pradigdhān || 5 ||
units.translation: Rather than killing the noble-minded elders, it is better in this world to live even on alms. But by killing the elders, we shall only be enjoying here the pleasures of wealth and desirable things drenched in blood.

units.sanskrit: न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः । यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषामस्तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: na caitad vidmaḥ kataran no garīyo yad vā jayema yadi vā no jayeyuḥ | yān eva hatvā na jijīviṣāmaste ’vasthitāḥ pramukhe dhārtarāṣṭrāḥ || 6 ||
units.translation: We do not know this as well as to which is the better for us, (and) whether we shall win, or whether they shall conquer us. Those very sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, by killing whom we do not wish to live, stand in confrontation.

units.sanskrit: कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसम्मूढचेताः । यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्‍चितं ब्रूहि तन्मे शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम् ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: kārpaṇya-doṣopahata-svabhāvaḥ pṛcchāmi tvāṁ dharma-sammūḍha-cetāḥ | yac chreyaḥ syān niścitaṁ brūhi tan me śiṣyas te ’haṁ śādhi māṁ tvāṁ prapannam || 7 ||
units.translation: With my nature overpowered by weak commiseration, with a mind bewildered about duty, I supplicate You. Tell me for certain which is better; I am Your disciple. Instruct me who have taken refuge in You.

units.sanskrit: न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद् यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम् । अवाप्य भूभावसपत्‍नमृद्धं राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: na hi prapaśyāmi mamāpanudyād yac chokam ucchoṣaṇam indriyāṇām | avāpya bhūmāv asapatnam ṛddhaṁ rājyaṁ surāṇām api cādhipatyam || 8 ||
units.translation: Because, I do not see that which can, even after acquiring on this earth a prosperous kingdom free from enemies and even sovereignty over the gods, remove my sorrow (which is) blasting the senses.

units.sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच । एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तपः । न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दामुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: sañjaya uvāca | evam uktvā hṛṣīkeśaṁ guḍākeśaḥ paran-tapaḥ | na yotsya iti govindam uktvā tūṣṇīṁ babhūva ha || 9 ||
units.translation: Having spoken thus to Hrsīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), Guḍākeśa, (Arjuna), the afflictor of foes, verily became silent, telling Govinda, ‘I shall not fight.’

units.sanskrit: तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत । सेनयोरूभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: tam uvāca hṛṣīkeśaḥ prahasann iva bhārata | senayor ubhayor madhye viṣīdantam idaṁ vacaḥ || 10 ||
units.translation: O descendant of Bharata, to him who was sorrowing between the two armies, Hrsīkeśa, mocking as it were, said these words:

units.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच । अशोच्यनन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे । गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | aśocyān anvaśocas tvaṁ prajñā-vādāṁś ca bhāṣase | gatāsūn agatāsūṁś ca nānuśocanti paṇḍitāḥ || 11 ||
units.translation: You grieve for whose who are not to be grieved for; and you speak words of wisdom! The learned do not grieve for the departed or for those who have not departed.

units.sanskrit: न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः । न चैव नभविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम् ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: na tv evāhaṁ jātu nāsaṁ na tvaṁ neme janādhipāḥ | na caiva na bhaviṣyāmaḥ sarve vayam ataḥ param || 12 ||
units.translation: Never was there a time when I did not exist, nor you, nor all these kings; nor in the future shall any of us cease to be.

units.sanskrit: देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा । तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: dehino ’smin yathā dehe kaumāraṁ yauvanaṁ jarā | tathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati || 13 ||
units.translation: As are boyhood, youth, and decrepitude to an embodied being in this (present) body, similar is the acquisition of another body. This being so, an intelligent person does not get deluded.

units.sanskrit: मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः । आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: mātrā-sparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ | āgamāpāyino ’nityās tāṁs titikṣasva bhārata || 14 ||
units.translation: But the contacts of the organs with the objects are the producers of cold and heat, happiness, and sorrow. They have a  beginning and an end, (and) are transient. Bear them, O descendant  of Bhārata.

units.sanskrit: यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ । समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: yaṁ hi na vyathayanty ete puruṣaṁ puruṣarṣabha | sama-duḥkha-sukhaṁ dhīraṁ so ’mṛtatvāya kalpate || 15 ||
units.translation: O (Arjuna), foremost among men, verily, the person whom these do not torment, the wise man to whom sorrow and happiness are the same—he is fit for Immortality.

units.sanskrit: नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः । उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: nāsato vidyate bhāvo nābhāvo vidyate sataḥ | ubhayor api dṛṣṭo ’ntas tv anayos tattva-darśibhiḥ || 16 ||
units.translation: Of the unreal there is no being; the real has no nonexistence. But the nature of both these, indeed, has been realized by the seers of Truth.

units.sanskrit: अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् । विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: avināśi tu tad viddhi yena sarvam idaṁ tatam | vināśam avyayasyāsya na kaścit kartum arhati || 17 ||
units.translation: But know That by which all this is pervaded to be indestructible. None can bring about the destruction of the Immutable.

units.sanskrit: अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः । अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: antavanta ime dehā nityasyoktāḥ śarīriṇaḥ | anāśino ’prameyasya tasmād yudhyasva bhārata || 18 ||
units.translation: These destructible bodies are said to belong to the everlasting, indestructible, indeterminable, embodied One. Therefore, O descendant of Bhārata, join the battle.

units.sanskrit: य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् । उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: ya enaṁ vetti hantāraṁ yaś cainaṁ manyate hatam | ubhau tau na vijānīto nāyaṁ hanti na hanyate || 19 ||
units.translation: He who thinks of this One as the killer, and he who thinks of this One as the killed—both of them do not know. This One does not kill, nor is It killed.

units.sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः । अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: na jāyate mriyate vā kadācin nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ | ajo nityaḥ śāśvato ’yaṁ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre || 20 ||
units.translation: Never is this One born, and never does It die; nor, having come to exist, will It again cease to be. This One is birthless, eternal, undecaying, ancient; It is not killed when the body is killed.

units.sanskrit: वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् । कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: vedāvināśinaṁ nityaṁ ya enam ajam avyayam | kathaṁ sa puruṣaḥ pārtha kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam || 21 ||
units.translation: O Pārtha, for he who knows this One as indestructible, eternal, birthless, and undecaying, how and whom does that person kill, or whom does he cause to be killed?

units.sanskrit: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि । तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णान्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: vāsāṁsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya navāni gṛhṇāti naro ’parāṇi | tathā śarīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇāny anyāni saṁyāti navāni dehī || 22 ||
units.translation: As after rejecting worn-out clothes a man takes up other new ones, likewise after rejecting worn-out bodies the embodied one unites with other new ones.

units.sanskrit: नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः । न चैनं क्ल‍ेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: nainaṁ chindanti śastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ | na cainaṁ kledayanty āpo na śoṣayati mārutaḥ || 23 ||
units.translation: Weapons do not cut It, fire does not burn It, water does not moisten It, and air does not dry It.

units.sanskrit: अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्ल‍ेद्योऽशोष्य एव च । नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: acchedyo ’yam adāhyo ’yam akledyo ’śoṣya eva ca | nityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur acalo ’yaṁ sanātanaḥ || 24 ||
units.translation: It cannot be cut, It cannot be burnt, cannot be moistened, and surely cannot be dried up. It is eternal, omnipresent, stationary, unmoving, and changeless.

units.sanskrit: अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते । तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: avyakto ’yam acintyo ’yam avikāryo ’yam ucyate | tasmād evaṁ viditvainaṁ nānuśocitum arhasi || 25 ||
units.translation: It is said that This is unmanifest; This is inconceivable; This is unchangeable. Therefore, having known This thus, you ought not grieve.

units.sanskrit: अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् । तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैनं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: atha cainaṁ nitya-jātaṁ nityaṁ vā manyase mṛtam | tathāpi tvaṁ mahā-bāho nainaṁ śocitum arhasi || 26 ||
units.translation: On the other hand, if you think this One is born continually or dies constantly, even then, O mighty-armed one, you ought not to grieve thus.

units.sanskrit: जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च । तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: jātasya hi dhruvo mṛtyur dhruvaṁ janma mṛtasya ca | tasmād aparihārye ’rthe na tvaṁ śocitum arhasi || 27 ||
units.translation: For death of anyone born is certain, and of the dead (re-)birth is a certainty. Therefore you ought not to grieve over an inevitable fact.

units.sanskrit: अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत । अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: avyaktādīni bhūtāni vyakta-madhyāni bhārata | avyakta-nidhanāny eva tatra kā paridevanā || 28 ||
units.translation: O descendant of Bhārata, all beings remain unmanifest in the beginning; they become manifest in the middle. After death they certainly become unmanifest. What lamentation can there be with regard to them?

units.sanskrit: आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेनमाश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः । आश्चर्यवच्च‍ैनमन्यः श‍ृणोति श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: āścarya-vat paśyati kaścid enam āścarya-vad vadati tathaiva cānyaḥ | āścarya-vac cainam anyaḥ śṛṇoti śrutvāpy enaṁ veda na caiva kaścit || 29 ||
units.translation: Someone visualizes It as a wonder; and similarly indeed, someone else talks of It as a wonder; and someone else hears of It as a wonder. And someone else, indeed, does not realize It even after hearing about It.

units.sanskrit: देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत । तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥ ३० ॥
units.transliteration: dehī nityam avadhyo ’yaṁ dehe sarvasya bhārata | tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṁ śocitum arhasi || 30 ||
units.translation: O descendant of Bharata, this embodied Self existing in everyone’s body can never be killed. Therefore you ought not to grieve for all (these) beings.

units.sanskrit: स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि । धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥ ३१ ॥
units.transliteration: sva-dharmam api cāvekṣya na vikampitum arhasi | dharmyād dhi yuddhāc chreyo ’nyat kṣatriyasya na vidyate || 31 ||
units.translation: Even considering your own duty you should not waver, since there is nothing else better for a Kṣatriya than a righteous battle.

units.sanskrit: यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् । सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥ ३२ ॥
units.transliteration: yadṛcchayā copapannaṁ svarga-dvāram apāvṛtam | sukhinaḥ kṣatriyāḥ pārtha labhante yuddham īdṛśam || 32 ||
units.translation: O son of Prthā, happy are the Kṣatriyas who come across this kind of a battle, which presents itself unsought for and which is an open gate to heaven.

units.sanskrit: अथ चेत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं सङ्ग्रामं न करिष्यसि । ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥ ३३ ॥
units.transliteration: atha cet tvam imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṅgrāmaṁ na kariṣyasi | tataḥ sva-dharmaṁ kīrtiṁ ca hitvā pāpam avāpsyasi || 33 ||
units.translation: On the other hand, if you will not fight this righteous battle,  then, forsaking your own duty and fame, you will incur sin.

units.sanskrit: अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् । सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥ ३४ ॥
units.transliteration: akīrtiṁ cāpi bhūtāni kathayiṣyanti te ’vyayām | sambhāvitasya cākīrtir maraṇād atiricyate || 34 ||
units.translation: People also will speak of your unending infamy. And to an honoured person infamy is worse than death.

units.sanskrit: भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः । येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ॥ ३५ ॥
units.transliteration: bhayād raṇād uparataṁ maṁsyante tvāṁ mahā-rathāḥ | yeṣāṁ ca tvaṁ bahu-mato bhūtvā yāsyasi lāghavam || 35 ||
units.translation: The great chariot-fighters will think of you as having desisted from the fight out of fear; and you will fall into disgrace before them to whom you had been estimable.

units.sanskrit: अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून्वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः । निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्य ततो दुःखतरं नु किम् ॥ ३६ ॥
units.transliteration: avācya-vādāṁś ca bahūn vadiṣyanti tavāhitāḥ | nindantas tava sāmarthyaṁ tato duḥkha-taraṁ nu kim || 36 ||
units.translation: And your enemies will speak many indecent words while denigrating your might. What can be more painful than that?

units.sanskrit: हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्ग जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् । तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥ ३७ ॥
units.transliteration: hato vā prāpsyasi svargaṁ jitvā vā bhokṣyase mahīm | tasmād uttiṣṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛta-niścayaḥ || 37 ||
units.translation: Either by being killed you will attain heaven, or by winning you will enjoy the earth. Therefore, O Arjuna, rise up with determination for fighting.

units.sanskrit: सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ । ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥ ३८ ॥
units.transliteration: sukha-duḥkhe same kṛtvā lābhālābhau jayājayau | tato yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṁ pāpam avāpsyasi || 38 ||
units.translation: Treating happiness and sorrow, gain and loss, and conquest and defeat with equanimity, then engage in battle. Thus you will not incur sin.

units.sanskrit: एषा तेऽभिहिता सांख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां श‍ृणु । बुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि ॥ ३९ ॥
units.transliteration: eṣā te ’bhihitā sāṅkhye buddhir yoge tv imāṁ śṛṇu | buddhyā yukto yayā pārtha karma-bandhaṁ prahāsyasi || 39 ||
units.translation: O Pārtha, this wisdom has been imparted to you from the standpoint of Self-realization. But listen to this (wisdom) from the standpoint of Yoga, endowed with which wisdom you will get rid of the bondage of action.

units.sanskrit: नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते । स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥ ४० ॥
units.transliteration: nehābhikrama-nāśo ’sti pratyavāyo na vidyate | sv-alpam apy asya dharmasya trāyate mahato bhayāt || 40 ||
units.translation: In this endeavor there is no loss or diminution, and a little advancement on this path can protect one from the most dangerous type of fear.

units.sanskrit: व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरूनन्दन । बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् ॥ ४१ ॥
units.transliteration: vyavasāyātmikā buddhir ekeha kuru-nandana | bahu-śākhā hy anantāś ca buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām || 41 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Kuru dynasty, in this there is a single, one-pointed conviction. The thoughts of the irresolute ones have many branches indeed, and are innumerable.

units.sanskrit: यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः । वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः ॥ ४२ ॥ कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् । क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति ॥ ४३ ॥
units.transliteration: yām imāṁ puṣpitāṁ vācaṁ pravadanty avipaścitaḥ | veda-vāda-ratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥ || 42 || kāmātmānaḥ svarga-parā janma-karma-phala-pradām | kriyā-viśeṣa-bahulāṁ bhogaiśvarya-gatiṁ prati || 43 ||
units.translation: O son of Pṛthā, those undiscerning people who utter flowery talk—which promises birth as a result of rites and duties, and is full of various special rites meant for the attainment of enjoyment  and affluence—they remain engrossed in the utterances of the Vedas and declare that nothing else exists; their minds are full of desires and they have heaven as the goal.

units.sanskrit: भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् । व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥ ४४ ॥
units.transliteration: bhogaiśvarya-prasaktānāṁ tayāpahṛta-cetasām | vyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ samādhau na vidhīyate || 44 ||
units.translation: One-pointed conviction does not become established in the minds of those who delight in enjoyment and affluence, and whose intellects are carried away by that (speech).

units.sanskrit: त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन । निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥ ४५ ॥
units.transliteration: trai-guṇya-viṣayā vedā nistrai-guṇyo bhavārjuna | nirdvandvo nitya-sattva-stho niryoga-kṣema ātmavān || 45 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, the Vedas have the three qualities as their object. You become free from worldliness, free from the pairs of duality, ever-poised in the quality of sattva, without (desire for) acquisition and protection, and self-collected.

units.sanskrit: यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः सम्प्लुतोदके । तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः ॥ ४६ ॥
units.transliteration: yāvān artha uda-pāne sarvataḥ samplutodake | tāvān sarveṣu vedeṣu brāhmaṇasya vijānataḥ || 46 ||
units.translation: A Brāhmana with realization has that much utility in all the Vedas as a man has in a well when there is a flood all around.

units.sanskrit: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन । मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥ ४७ ॥
units.transliteration: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana | mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stv akarmaṇi ||
units.translation: Your right is for action alone, never for the results. Do not become the agent of the results of action. May you not have any inclination for inaction.

units.sanskrit: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय । सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥ ४८ ॥
units.transliteration: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañ-jaya | siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga ucyate || 48 ||
units.translation: By being established in Yoga, O Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), undertake actions, casting off attachment and remaining equipoised in success and failure. Equanimity is called Yoga.

units.sanskrit: दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय । बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥ ४९ ॥
units.transliteration: dūreṇa hy avaraṁ karma buddhi-yogād dhanañ-jaya | buddhau śaranam anviccha kṛpaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ || 49 ||
units.translation: O Dhanañjaya, indeed, action is quite inferior to the Yoga of wisdom. Take resort to wisdom. Those who thirst for rewards are pitiable.

units.sanskrit: बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते । तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥ ५० ॥
units.transliteration: buddhi-yukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛta-duṣkṛte | tasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśalam || 50 ||
units.translation: Possessed of wisdom, one rejects here both virtue and vice. Therefore devote yourself to (Karma-)Yoga. Yoga is skilfulness in action.

units.sanskrit: कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः । जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥ ५१ ॥
units.transliteration: karma-jaṁ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṁ tyaktvā manīṣiṇaḥ | janma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padaṁ gacchanty anāmayam || 51 ||
units.translation: Because those who are devoted to wisdom become men of Enlightenment by giving up the fruits produced by actions, and reach the state beyond evils by having become freed from the bondage of birth.

units.sanskrit: यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति । तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च ॥ ५२ ॥
units.transliteration: yadā te moha-kalilaṁ buddhir vyatitariṣyati | tadā gantāsi nirvedaṁ śrotavyasya śrutasya ca || 52 ||
units.translation: When your mind will go beyond the turbidity of delusion, then you will acquire dispassion for what will be heard and what has been heard.

units.sanskrit: श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला । समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि ॥ ५३ ॥
units.transliteration: śruti-vipratipannā te yadā sthāsyati niścalā | samādhāv acalā buddhis tadā yogam avāpsyasi || 53 ||
units.translation: When your mind that has become bewildered by hearing will become unshakable and steadfast in the Self, then you will attain Yoga that arises from discrimination.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव । स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥ ५४ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | sthita-prajñasya kā bhāṣā samādhi-sthasya keśava | sthita-dhīḥ kiṁ prabhāṣeta kim āsīta vrajeta kim || 54 ||
units.translation: Arjuna said: O Keśava, what is the description of a man of steady wisdom who is Self-absorbed? How does the man of steady wisdom  speak? How does he sit? How does he move about?

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान् । आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥ ५५ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha manogatān | ātmany evātmanā tuṣṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadocyate || 55 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: O Pārtha, when one fully renounces all the desires that have entered the mind, and remains satisfied in the Self alone by the Self, then he is called a man of steady wisdom.

units.sanskrit: दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्न‍मनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः । वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थिधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥ ५६ ॥
units.transliteration: duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ | vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir ucyate || 56 ||
units.translation: That monk is called a man of steady wisdom when his mind is unperturbed in sorrow, he is free from longing for delights, and has gone beyond attachment, fear and anger.

units.sanskrit: यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य श‍ुभाश‍ुभम् । नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥ ५७ ॥
units.transliteration: yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śubhāśubham | nābhinandati na dveṣṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā || 57 ||
units.translation: The wisdom of that person remains established who has not attachment for anything anywhere, who neither welcomes nor rejects anything whatever good or bad when he comes across it.

units.sanskrit: यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥ ५८ ॥
units.transliteration: yadā saṁharate cāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśaḥ | indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā || 58 ||
units.translation: And when this one fully withdraws the senses from the objects of the senses, as a tortoise wholly (withdraws) the limbs, then his wisdom remains established.

units.sanskrit: विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः । रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥ ५९ ॥
units.transliteration: viṣayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ | rasa-varjaṁ raso ’py asya paraṁ dṛṣṭvā nivartate || 59 ||
units.translation: The objects recede from an abstinent man, with the exception of the taste (for them). Even the taste of this person falls away after realizing the Absolute.

units.sanskrit: यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः । इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥ ६० ॥
units.transliteration: yatato hy api kaunteya puruṣasya vipaścitaḥ | indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ || 60 ||
units.translation: For, O son of Kuntī, the turbulent organs violently snatch away the mind of an intelligent person, even while he is striving diligently.

units.sanskrit: तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः । वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥ ६१ ॥
units.transliteration: tāni sarvāṇi saṁyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ | vaśe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā || 61 ||
units.translation: Controlling all of them, one should remain concentrated on Me as the supreme. For, the wisdom of one whose organs are under control becomes steadfast.

units.sanskrit: ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते । सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥ ६२ ॥
units.transliteration: dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate | saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate || 62 ||
units.translation: In the case of a person who dwells on objects, there arises attachment for them. From attachment grows hankering, from hankering springs anger.

units.sanskrit: क्रोधाद्भ‍वति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः । स्मृतिभ्रंशाद्बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥ ६३ ॥
units.transliteration: krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛti-vibhramaḥ | smṛti-bhraṁśād buddhi-nāśo buddhi-nāśāt praṇaśyati || 63 ||
units.translation: From anger follows delusion; from delusion, failure of memory; from failure of memory, the loss of understanding; from the loss of understanding, he perishes.

units.sanskrit: रागद्वेषविमुक्तैस्तु विषयनिन्द्रियैश्चरन् । आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ॥ ६४ ॥
units.transliteration: rāga-dveṣa-vimuktais tu viṣayān indriyaiś caran | ātma-vaśyair vidheyātmā prasādam adhigacchati || 64 ||
units.translation: But by perceiving objects with the organs that are free from attraction and repulsion, and are under his own control, the self-controlled man attains serenity.

units.sanskrit: प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते । प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याश‍ु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ॥ ६५ ॥
units.transliteration: prasāde sarva-duḥkhānāṁ hānir asyopajāyate | prasanna-cetaso hy āśu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣṭhate || 65 ||
units.translation: When there is serenity, there follows eradication of all his sorrows, because the wisdom of one who has a serene mind soon becomes firmly established.

units.sanskrit: नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना । न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥ ६६ ॥
units.transliteration: nāsti buddhir ayuktasya na cāyuktasya bhāvanā | na cābhāvayataḥ śāntir aśāntasya kutaḥ sukham || 66 ||
units.translation: For the unsteady there is no wisdom, and there is no meditation for the unsteady man. And for an unmeditative man there is no peace. How can there be happiness for one without peace?

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते । तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥ ६७ ॥
units.transliteration: indriyāṇāṁ hi caratāṁ yan mano ’nuvidhīyate | tad asya harati prajñāṁ vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi || 67 ||
units.translation: For, the mind which follows in the wake of the wandering senses, that (mind) carries away his wisdom like the wind (diverting) a boat on the waters.

units.sanskrit: तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥ ६८ ॥
units.transliteration: tasmād yasya mahā-bāho nigṛhītāni sarvaśaḥ | indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā || 68 ||
units.translation: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, his wisdom becomes established whose organs in all their varieties are withdrawn from their objects.

units.sanskrit: या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी । यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥ ६९ ॥
units.transliteration: yā niśā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti saṁyamī | yasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśā paśyato muneḥ || 69 ||
units.translation: The self-restrained man keeps awake during that which is night for all creatures. That during which creatures keep awake, it is night to the seeing sage.

units.sanskrit: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् । तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे स शान्तिमाप्‍नोति न कामकामी ॥ ७० ॥
units.transliteration: āpūryamāṇam acala-pratiṣṭhaṁ samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat | tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśanti sarve sa śāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī || 70 ||
units.translation: That man attains peace into whom all desires enter in the same way as the waters flow into a sea that remains unchanged (even) when being filled up from all sides. Not so one who is desirous of objects.
