units.sanskrit: विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः । निर्ममो निरहङ्कार स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥ ७१ ॥
units.transliteration: vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumāṁś carati niḥspṛhaḥ | nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sa śāntim adhigacchati || 71 ||
units.translation: That man attains peace who, after rejecting all desires, moves about free from hankering, without the idea of (‘I’ and) ‘mine’, and devoid of pride.

units.sanskrit: एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिःपार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति । स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥ ७२ ॥
units.transliteration: eṣā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṁ prāpya vimuhyati | sthitvāsyām anta-kāle ’pi brahma-nirvāṇam ṛcchati || 72 ||
units.translation: O Pārtha, this is the state of being established in Brahman. One does not become deluded after attaining this. One attains identification with Brahman by being established in this state even in the closing years of one’s life.

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच अनाश्रित: कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति य: । स सन्न्यासी च योगी च न निरग्न‍िर्न चाक्रिय: ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | anāśritaḥ karma-phalaṁ kāryaṁ karma karoti yaḥ | sa sannyāsī ca yogī ca na niragnir na cākriyaḥ || 1 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: He who performs an action which is his duty, without depending on the result of action, he is a monk and a yogī; (but) not he who does not keep a fire and is actionless.

units.sanskrit: यं सन्न्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव । न ह्यसन्न्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: yaṁ sannyāsam iti prāhur yogaṁ taṁ viddhi pāṇḍava | na hy asannyasta-saṅkalpo yogī bhavati kaścana || 2 ||
units.translation: That which they call monasticism, know that to be Yoga, O  Pāṇḍava. For, nobody who has not given up expectations can be a yogī.

units.sanskrit: आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते । योगारूढस्यतस्यैव शम: कारणमुच्यते ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: ārurukṣor muner yogaṁ karma kāraṇam ucyate | yogārūḍhasya tasyaiva śamaḥ kāraṇam ucyate || 3 ||
units.translation: For the sage who wishes to ascend to (Dhyāna-)Yoga, action is said to be the means. For that person, when he has ascended to (Dhyāna-)Yoga, inaction alone is said to be the means.

units.sanskrit: यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्ज‍ते । सर्वसङ्कल्पसन्न्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: yadā hi nendriyārtheṣu na karmasv anuṣajjate | sarva-saṅkalpa-sannyāsī yogārūḍhas tadocyate || 4 ||
units.translation: Verily, when a man who has given up thought about everything does not get attached to sense-objects or actions, he is then said to be established in Yoga.

units.sanskrit: उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् । आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव आत्मनः ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet | ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ || 5 ||
units.translation: One should save oneself by oneself; one should not lower oneself. For oneself is verily one’s own friend; oneself is verily one’s own enemy.

units.sanskrit: बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जित: । अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ | anātmanas tu śatrutve vartetātmaiva śatru-vat || 6 ||
units.translation: Of him, by whom has been conquered his very self by the self, his self is the friend of his self. But, for one who has not conquered his self, his self itself acts inimically like an enemy.

units.sanskrit: जितात्मन: प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहित: । शीतोष्णसुखदु:खेषु तथा मानापमानयो: ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: jitātmanaḥ praśāntasya paramātmā samāhitaḥ | śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ || 7 ||
units.translation: The supreme Self of one who has control over the aggregate of his body and sense-organs, and who is tranquil, becomes manifest. (He  should be equipoised) in the midst of cold and heat, happiness and sorrow, as also honour and dishonour.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्‍तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रिय: । युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्ट्राश्मकाञ्चन: ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: jñāna-vijñāna-tṛptātmā kūṭa-stho vijitendriyaḥ | yukta ity ucyate yogī sama-loṣṭrāśma-kāñcanaḥ || 8 ||
units.translation: One whose mind is satisfied with knowledge and realization, sense-who is unmoved, who has his organs under control, is said to be Self-absorbed. The yogī treats a lump of earth, a stone and gold equally.

units.sanskrit: सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु । साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: suhṛn-mitrāry-udāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣya-bandhuṣu | sādhuṣv api ca pāpeṣu sama-buddhir viśiṣyate || 9 ||
units.translation: He excels who has sameness of view with regard to a benefactor, a friend, a foe, a neutral, an arbiter, the hateful, a relative, good people and even sinners.

units.sanskrit: योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थित: । एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रह: ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṁ rahasi sthitaḥ | ekākī yata-cittātmā nirāśīr aparigrahaḥ || 10 ||
units.translation: A yogī should constantly concentrate his mind by staying in a solitary place, alone, with mind and body controlled, free from expectations, (and) free from acquisition.

units.sanskrit: श‍ुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मन: । नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥ ११ ॥ तत्रैकाग्रं मन: कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रिय । उपविश्यासने युञ्‍ज्याद्योगमात्मविश‍ुद्धये ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: śucau deśe pratiṣṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ | nāty-ucchritaṁ nāti-nīcaṁ cailājina-kuśottaram || 11 || tatraikāgraṁ manaḥ kṛtvā yata-cittendriya-kriyaḥ | upaviśyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśuddhaye || 12 ||
units.translation: Having firmly established in a clean place his seat, neither too high nor too low, and made of cloth, skin and kuśa-grass, placed successively one below the other; sitting on that seat, he should concentrate his mind for  the purification of the internal organ, making the mind one-pointed and keeping the actions of the mind and senses under control.

units.sanskrit: समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिर: । सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥ १३ ॥ प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थित: । मन: संयम्य मच्च‍ित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्पर: ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: samaṁ kāya-śiro-grīvaṁ dhārayann acalaṁ sthiraḥ | samprekṣya nāsikāgraṁ svaṁ diśaś cānavalokayan || 13 || praśāntātmā vigata-bhīr brahmacāri-vrate sthitaḥ | manaḥ saṁyamya mac-citto yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ || 14 ||
units.translation: Holding the body, head and neck erect and still, being steady, looking at the tip of his own nose—and not looking around; He should remain seated with a placid mind, free from fear, firm in the vow of celibacy, and with the mind fixed on Me by controlling it through concentration, having Me as the supreme Goal.

units.sanskrit: युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानस: । शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī niyata-mānasaḥ | śāntiṁ nirvāṇa-paramāṁ mat-saṁsthām adhigacchati || 15 ||
units.translation: Concentrating the mind thus forever, the yogī of controlled mind achieves the Peace that culminates in Liberation and abides in Me.

units.sanskrit: नात्यश्न‍तस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्न‍त: । न चातिस्वप्‍नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: nāty-aśnatas tu yogo ’sti na caikāntam anaśnataḥ | na cāti-svapna-śīlasya jāgrato naiva cārjuna || 16 ||
units.translation: But, O Arjuna, Yoga is not for one who eats too much, nor for  one who does not eat at all; neither for one who habitually sleeps too long, nor surely for one who keeps awake.

units.sanskrit: युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु । युक्तस्वप्‍नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दु:खहा ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: yuktāhāra-vihārasya yukta-ceṣṭasya karmasu | yukta-svapnāvabodhasya yogo bhavati duḥkha-hā || 17 ||
units.translation: Yoga becomes a destroyer of sorrow of one whose eating and movements are regulated, whose effort in works is moderate, andwhose sleep and wakefulness are temperate.

units.sanskrit: यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते । निस्पृह: सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: yadā viniyataṁ cittam ātmany evāvatiṣṭhate | nispṛhaḥ sarva-kāmebhyo yukta ity ucyate tadā || 18 ||
units.translation: A man who has become free from hankering for all desirable objects is then said to be Self-absorbed when the controlled mind rests in the Self alone.

units.sanskrit: यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता । योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मन: ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā | yogino yata-cittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ || 19 ||
units.translation: As a lamp kept in a windless place does not flicker, such is the simile thought of for the yogī whose mind is under control, and who is engaged in concentration on the Self.

units.sanskrit: यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया । यत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: yatroparamate cittaṁ niruddhaṁ yoga-sevayā | yatra caivātmanātmānaṁ paśyann ātmani tuṣyati || 20 ||
units.translation: When the mind restrained through the practice of Yoga gets withdrawn, and just when by seeing the Self by the self one remains contented in the Self alone;

units.sanskrit: सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद्‍बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम् । वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वत: ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: sukham ātyantikaṁ yat tad buddhi-grāhyam atīndriyam | vetti yatra na caivāyaṁ sthitaś calati tattvataḥ || 21 ||
units.translation: When one experiences that absolute Bliss that can be intuited by the intellect and is beyond the senses, and being established (thus) this person surely does not swerve from Reality;

units.sanskrit: यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं तत: । यस्मिन्स्थितो न दु:खेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: yaṁ labdhvā cāparaṁ lābhaṁ manyate nādhikaṁ tataḥ | yasmin sthito na duḥkhena guruṇāpi vicālyate || 22 ||
units.translation: Obtaining which one does not think of any other acquisition to be superior to that, and being established in which one is not perturbed even by great sorrow;

units.sanskrit: तं विद्याद्दु:खसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् | स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: taṁ vidyād duḥkha-saṁyoga-viyogaṁ yoga-saṁjñitam | sa niścayena yoktavyo yogo ’nirviṇṇa-cetasā || 23 ||
units.translation: One should know that severance of contact with sorrow to be what is called Yoga. That Yoga has to be practised with perseverance and with an undepressed heart.

units.sanskrit: सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषत: । मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्तत: ॥ २४ ॥ शनै: शनैरुपरमेद्‍बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया । आत्मसंस्थं मन: कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāṁs tyaktvā sarvān aśeṣataḥ | manasaivendriya-grāmaṁ viniyamya samantataḥ || 24 || śanaiḥ śanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛti-gṛhītayā | ātma-saṁsthaṁ manaḥ kṛtvā na kiñcid api cintayet || 25 ||
units.translation: By totally eschewing all desires which arise from thoughts, and restraining with the mind itself all the organs from every side, one should gradually withdraw with the intellect endowed with steadiness. Making the mind fixed in the Self, one should not think of anything whatsoever.

units.sanskrit: यतो यतो निश्चलति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् । ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: yato yato niścalati manaś cañcalam asthiram | tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmany eva vaśaṁ nayet || 26 ||
units.translation: (The yogī) should bring (his mind) under the subjugation of the Self Itself, by restraining it from all those causes whatever due to which the restless, unsteady mind wanders away.

units.sanskrit: प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् । उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: praśānta-manasaṁ hy enaṁ yoginaṁ sukham uttamam | upaiti śānta-rajasaṁ brahma-bhūtam akalmaṣam || 27 ||
units.translation: Supreme Bliss comes to this yogī alone whose mind has become perfectly tranquil, whose (quality of) rājas has been eliminated, who has become identified with Brahman, and is taintless.

units.sanskrit: युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मष: । सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्न‍ुते ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī vigata-kalmaṣaḥ | sukhena brahma-saṁsparśam atyantaṁ sukham aśnute || 28 ||
units.translation: By concentrating his mind constantly thus, the taintless yogī easily attains the absolute Bliss of contact with Brahman.

units.sanskrit: सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि । ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शन: ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: sarva-bhūta-stham-ātmānaṁ sarva-bhūtāni cātmani | īkṣate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśanaḥ || 29 ||
units.translation: One who has his mind Self-absorbed through Yoga has the vision of sameness everywhere, sees his Self existing in everything, and everything in his Self.

units.sanskrit: यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति । तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥ ३० ॥
units.transliteration: yo māṁ paśyati sarvatra sarvaṁ ca mayi paśyati | tasyāhaṁ na praṇaśyāmi sa ca me na praṇaśyati || 30 ||
units.translation: One who sees Me in everything, and sees all things in Me—I  do not go out of his vision, and he also is not lost to My vision.

units.sanskrit: सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थित: । सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते ॥ ३१ ॥
units.transliteration: sarva-bhūta-sthitaṁ yo māṁ bhajaty ekatvam āsthitaḥ | sarvathā vartamāno ’pi sa yogī mayi vartate || 31 ||
units.translation: That yogī who, being established in unity, adores Me as existing in all things, he exists in Me in whatever condition he may be.

units.sanskrit: आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन । सुखं वा यदि वा दु:खं स योगी परमो मत: ॥ ३२ ॥
units.transliteration: ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṁ paśyati yo ’rjuna | sukhaṁ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṁ sa yogī paramo mataḥ || 32 ||
units.translation: O Arjuna, that yogī is considered the best who judges what is happiness and sorrow in all beings by the same standard as he would apply to himself.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्त: साम्येन मधुसूदन । एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥ ३३ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | yo ’yaṁ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana | etasyāhaṁ na paśyāmi cañcalatvāt sthitiṁ sthirām || 33 ||
units.translation: O Madhusudan (Kṛṣṇa), this Yoga that has been spoken of by You as sameness, I do not see its steady continuance, owing to the restlessness (of the mind).

units.sanskrit: चञ्चलं हि मन: कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम् । तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥ ३४ ॥
units.transliteration: cañcalaṁ hi manaḥ kṛṣṇa pramāthi balavad dṛḍham | tasyāhaṁ nigrahaṁ manye vāyor iva su-duṣkaram || 34 ||
units.translation: For, O Kṛṣṇa, the mind is unsteady, turbulent, strong and obstinate. I consider its control to be as greatly difficult as of the wind.

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम् । अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥ ३५ ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | asaṁśayaṁ mahā-bāho mano durnigrahaṁ calam | abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa ca gṛhyate || 35 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: O mighty-armed one, undoubtedly the mind is intractable and restless. But, O son of Kuntī, it is brought under control through practice and detachment.

units.sanskrit: असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मति: । वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्‍तुमुपायत: ॥ ३६ ॥
units.transliteration: asaṁyatātmanā yogo duṣprāpa iti me matiḥ | vaśyātmanā tu yatatā śakyo ’vāptum upāyataḥ || 36 ||
units.translation: My conviction is that Yoga is difficult to be attained by one of uncontrolled mind. But it is possible to be attained through the (above) means by one who strives and has a controlled mind.

units.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच | अयति: श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्च‍‍लितमानस: । अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति ॥ ३७ ॥
units.transliteration: arjuna uvāca | ayatiḥ śraddhayopeto yogāc calita-mānasaḥ | aprāpya yoga-saṁsiddhiṁ kāṁ gatiṁ kṛṣṇa gacchati || 37 ||
units.translation: Arjuna said: O Kṛṣṇa, failing to achieve perfection in Yoga, what goal does one attain who, though possessed of faith, is not diligent and whose mind becomes deflected from Yoga?

units.sanskrit: कच्च‍िन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति । अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मण: पथि ॥ ३८ ॥
units.transliteration: kaccin nobhaya-vibhraṣṭaś chinnābhram iva naśyati | apratiṣṭho mahā-bāho vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ pathi || 38 ||
units.translation: O Mighty-armed one, fallen from both, without support, deluded on the path to Brahman, does he not get ruined like a scattered cloud?

units.sanskrit: एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषत: । त्वदन्य: संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते ॥ ३९ ॥
units.transliteration: etan me saṁśayaṁ kṛṣṇa chettum arhasy aśeṣataḥ | tvad-anyaḥ saṁśayasyāsya chettā na hy upapadyate || 39 ||
units.translation: O Kṛṣṇa, You should totally eradicate this doubt of mine. For none other than Yourself can be the dispeller of this doubt!

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते । न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद्दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति ॥ ४० ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | pārtha naiveha nāmutra vināśas tasya vidyate | na hi kalyāṇa-kṛt kaścid durgatiṁ tāta gacchati || 40 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: O Pārtha, there is certainly no ruin for him here or hereafter. For, no one engaged in good meets with a deplorable end, My son!

units.sanskrit: प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वती: समा: । श‍ुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते ॥ ४१ ॥
units.transliteration: prāpya puṇya-kṛtāṁ lokān uṣitvā śāśvatīḥ samāḥ | śucīnāṁ śrīmatāṁ gehe yoga-bhraṣṭo ’bhijāyate || 41 ||
units.translation: Attaining the worlds of the righteous, and residing there for eternal years, the man fallen from Yoga is born in the house of the pious and the prosperous.

units.sanskrit: अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् । एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् ॥ ४२ ॥
units.transliteration: atha vā yoginām evan kule bhavati dhīmatām | etad dhi durlabha-taraṁ loke janma yad īdṛśam || 42 ||
units.translation: Or he is born in the family of wise yogīs only. Such a birth is surely more difficult to get in this world.

units.sanskrit: तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम् । यतते च ततो भूय: संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन ॥ ४३ ॥
units.transliteration: tatra taṁ buddhi-saṁyogaṁ labhate paurva-dehikam | yatate ca tato bhūyaḥ saṁsiddhau kuru-nandana || 43 ||
units.translation: There he becomes endowed with that wisdom acquired in the previous body. And he strives more than before for perfection, O scion of the Kuru dynasty.

units.sanskrit: पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि स: । जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते ॥ ४४ ॥
units.transliteration: pūrvābhyāsena tenaiva hriyate hy avaśo ’pi saḥ | jijñāsur api yogasya śabda-brahmātivartate || 44 ||
units.translation: For, by that very past practice, he is carried forward even in spite of himself! Even a seeker of Yoga transcends the result of the performance of Vedic rituals!

units.sanskrit: प्रयत्‍नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संश‍ुद्धकिल्बिष: । अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥ ४५ ॥
units.transliteration: prayatnād yatamānas tu yogī saṁśuddha-kilbiṣaḥ | aneka-janma-saṁsiddhas tato yāti parāṁ gatim || 45 ||
units.translation: However, the yogī, applying himself assiduously, becoming purified from sin and attaining perfection through many births, thereby achieves the highest Goal.

units.sanskrit: तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिक: । कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन ॥ ४६ ॥
units.transliteration: tapasvibhyo ’dhiko yogī jñānibhyo ’pi mato ’dhikaḥ | karmibhyaś cādhiko yogī tasmād yogī bhavārjuna || 46 ||
units.translation: A yogī is higher than men of austerity; he is considered higher even than men of knowledge. The yogī is also higher than men of action. Therefore, O Arjuna, become a yogī.

units.sanskrit: योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्ग‍तेनान्तरात्मना । श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मत: ॥ ४७ ॥
units.transliteration: yoginām api sarveṣāṁ mad-gatenāntar-ātmanā | śraddhāvān bhajate yo māṁ sa me yukta-tamo mataḥ || 47 ||
units.translation: Even among all the yogīs, he who adores Me with his mind fixed on Me, with faith, he is considered by Me to be the best of the yogīs.

units.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते । एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहु: क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विद: ॥ 1 ॥
units.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | idaṁ śarīraṁ kaunteya kṣetram ity abhidhīyate | etad yo vetti taṁ prāhuḥ kṣetra-jña iti tad-vidaḥ || 1 ||
units.translation: Śrī Bhagavān said: O son of Kuntī, this body is referred to as the ‘field’. Those who are versed in this call him who is conscious of it as the ‘knower of the field’.

units.sanskrit: क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत । क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: kṣetra-jñaṁ cāpi māṁ viddhi sarva-kṣetreṣu bhārata | kṣetra-kṣetrajñayor jñānaṁ yat taj jñānaṁ mataṁ mama || 2 ||
units.translation: And, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, understand Me to be the ‘Knower of the field’ in all the fields. In My opinion, that is Knowledge which is the knowledge of the field and the knower of the field.

units.sanskrit: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च‍ यादृक्‍च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् । स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श‍ृणु ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: tat kṣetraṁ yac ca yādṛk ca yad-vikāri yataś ca yat | sa ca yo yat-prabhāvaś ca tat samāsena me śṛṇu || 3 ||
units.translation: Hear from Me in brief about (all) that as to what that field is and how it is; what its changes are, and from what cause arises what effect; and who He is, and what His powers are.

units.sanskrit: ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधै: पृथक् । ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भ‍िर्विनिश्चितै: ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: ṛṣibhir bahudhā gītaṁ chandobhir vividhaiḥ pṛthak | brahma-sūtra-padaiś caiva hetumadbhir viniścitaiḥ || 4 ||
units.translation: It has been sung of in various ways by the ṛṣis, separately by the different kinds of Vedic texts, and also by the rational and convincing sentences themselves which are indicative of, and lead to, Brahman.

units.sanskrit: महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च । इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचरा: ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: mahā-bhūtāny ahaṅkāro buddhir avyaktam eva ca | indriyāṇi daśaikaṁ ca pañca cendriya-gocarāḥ || 5 ||
units.translation: The great elements, egoism, intellect and the Unmanifest itself; the ten organs and the one, and the five objects of the senses;

units.sanskrit: इच्छा द्वेष: सुखं दु:खं सङ्घातश्चेतना धृति: । एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: icchā dveṣaḥ sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ saṅghātaś cetanā dhṛtiḥ | etat kṣetraṁ samāsena sa-vikāram udāhṛtam || 6 ||
units.translation: Desire, repulsion, happiness, sorrow, the aggregate (of body and sense-organs), sentience, fortitude—this field, together with its modifications, has been spoken of briefly.

units.sanskrit: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् । आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रह: ॥ ७ ॥
units.transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvam ahiṁsā kṣāntir ārjavam | ācāryopāsanaṁ śaucaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥ || 7 ||
units.translation: Humility, unpretentiousness, non-injury, forbearance, sincerity, service of the teacher, cleanliness, steadiness, control of body and organs;

units.sanskrit: इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च । जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदु:खदोषानुदर्शनम् ॥ ८ ॥
units.transliteration: indriyārtheṣu vairāgyam anahaṅkāra eva ca | janma-mṛtyu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣānudarśanam || 8 ||
units.translation: Non-attachment with regard to objects of the senses, and also absence of egotism; seeing the evil in birth, death, old age, diseases and miseries;

units.sanskrit: असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्ग: पुत्रदारगृहादिषु । नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु ॥ ९ ॥
units.transliteration: asaktir anabhiṣvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛhādiṣu | nityaṁ ca sama-cittatvam iṣṭāniṣṭopapattiṣu || 9 ||
units.translation: Non-attachment and absence of fondness with regard to sons, wives, homes, etc., and constant equanimity of the mind with regard to the attainment of the desirable and the undesirable;

units.sanskrit: मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी । विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ॥ १० ॥
units.transliteration: mayi cānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhicāriṇī | vivikta-deśa-sevitvam aratir jana-saṁsadi || 10 ||
units.translation: And unwavering devotion to Me with single-minded concentration; inclination to withdraw to a pure place; lack of delight in a crowd of people;

units.sanskrit: अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् । एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा ॥ ११ ॥
units.transliteration: adhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśanam | etaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā || 11 ||
units.translation: Steadfastness in the knowledge of the Self, contemplation on the Goal of the knowledge of Reality—this is spoken of as Knowledge. Ignorance is that which is other than this.

units.sanskrit: ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्न‍ुते । अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ॥ १२ ॥
units.transliteration: jñeyaṁ yat tat pravakṣyāmi yaj jñātvāmṛtam aśnute | anādi mat-paraṁ brahma na sat tan nāsad ucyate || 12 ||
units.translation: I shall speak of that which is to be known, by realizing which one attains Immortality. The supreme Brahman is without any beginning. That is called neither being nor non-being.

units.sanskrit: सर्वत: पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् । सर्वत:श्रुतिमल्ल‍ोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ॥ १३ ॥
units.transliteration: sarvataḥ pāṇi-pādaṁ tat sarvato ’kṣi-śiro-mukham | sarvataḥ śrutimal loke sarvam āvṛtya tiṣṭhati || 13 ||
units.translation: That (Knowable), which has hands and feet everywhere, which has eyes, heads and mouths everywhere, which has ears everywhere, exists in creatures by pervading them all.

units.sanskrit: सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् । असक्तं सर्वभृच्च‍ैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ॥ १४ ॥
units.transliteration: sarvendriya-guṇābhāsaṁ sarvendriya-vivarjitam | asaktaṁ sarva-bhṛc caiva nirguṇaṁ guṇa-bhoktṛ ca || 14 ||
units.translation: Shining through the functions of all the organs, (yet) devoid of all the organs; unattached, and verily the supporter of all; without quality, and the perceiver of qualities;

units.sanskrit: बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च । सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ॥ १५ ॥
units.transliteration: bahir antaś ca bhūtānām acaraṁ caram eva ca | sūkṣmatvāt tad avijñeyaṁ dūra-sthaṁ cāntike ca tat || 15 ||
units.translation: Existing outside and inside all beings; moving as well as non-moving, It is incomprehensible due to subtleness. So also, It is far away, and yet near.

units.sanskrit: अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् । भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ॥ १६ ॥
units.transliteration: avibhaktaṁ ca bhūteṣu vibhaktam iva ca sthitam | bhūta-bhartṛ ca taj jñeyaṁ grasiṣṇu prabhaviṣṇu ca || 16 ||
units.translation: And the Knowable, though undivided, appears to be existing as divided in all beings, and It is the sustainer of all beings as also the devourer and originator.

units.sanskrit: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमस: परमुच्यते । ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ॥ १७ ॥
units.transliteration: jyotiṣām api taj jyotis tamasaḥ param ucyate | jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ jñāna-gamyaṁ hṛdi sarvasya viṣṭhitam || 17 ||
units.translation: That is the Light even of the lights; It is spoken of as beyond darkness. It is Knowledge, the Knowable, and the Known. It exists specially in the hearts of all.

units.sanskrit: इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासत: । मद्भ‍क्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भ‍ावायोपपद्यते ॥ १८ ॥
units.transliteration: iti kṣetraṁ tathā jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ coktaṁ samāsataḥ | mad-bhakta etad vijñāya mad-bhāvāyopapadyate || 18 ||
units.translation: Thus has been spoken of in brief the field as also Knowledge and the Knowable. By understanding this My devotee becomes qualified for My state.

units.sanskrit: प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि । विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान् ॥ १९ ॥
units.transliteration: prakṛtiṁ puruṣaṁ caiva viddhy anādī ubhāv api | vikārāṁś ca guṇāṁś caiva viddhi prakṛti-sambhavān || 19 ||
units.translation: Know both prakṛti and puruṣa to be without beginning; know the modifications as also the qualities as born of Nature.

units.sanskrit: कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतु: प्रकृतिरुच्यते । पुरुष: सुखदु:खानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ॥ २० ॥
units.transliteration: kārya-kāraṇa-kartṛtve hetuḥ prakṛtir ucyate | puruṣaḥ sukha-duḥkhānāṁ bhoktṛtve hetur ucyate || 20 ||
units.translation: With regard to the source of body and organs, Nature is said to be the cause. The Ātman is the cause so far as enjoyership of happiness and sorrow is concerned.

units.sanskrit: पुरुष: प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् । कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥ २१ ॥
units.transliteration: puruṣaḥ prakṛti-stho hi bhuṅkte prakṛti-jān guṇān | kāraṇaṁ guṇa-saṅgo ’sya sad-asad-yoni-janmasu || 21 ||
units.translation: Since the Ātman is seated in Nature, therefore it experiences the qualities born of Nature. Contact with the qualities is the cause of its births in good and evil wombs.

units.sanskrit: उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वर: । परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुष: पर: ॥ २२ ॥
units.transliteration: upadraṣṭānumantā ca bhartā bhoktā maheśvaraḥ | paramātmeti cāpy ukto dehe ’smin puruṣaḥ paraḥ || 22 ||
units.translation: He who is the Witness, the Permitter, the Sustainer, the  Experiencer, the great Bhagavān, and who is also spoken of as the transcendental Self, is the supreme Person in this body.

units.sanskrit: य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणै: सह । सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥ २३ ॥
units.transliteration: ya evaṁ vetti puruṣaṁ prakṛtiṁ ca guṇaiḥ saha | sarvathā vartamāno ’pi na sa bhūyo ’bhijāyate || 23 ||
units.translation: He who knows thus the Person and Nature along with the qualities will not be born again, in whatever way he may live.

units.sanskrit: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना । अन्ये सांख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ॥ २४ ॥
units.transliteration: dhyānenātmani paśyanti kecid ātmānam ātmanā | anye sāṅkhyena yogena karma-yogena cāpare || 24 ||
units.translation: Through meditation some realize the Self in (their) intellect with the help of the internal organ; others through Sāṅkhya-Yoga, and others through Karma-Yoga.

units.sanskrit: अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्त: श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते । तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणा: ॥ २५ ॥
units.transliteration: anye tv evam ajānantaḥ śrutvānyebhya upāsate | te ’pi cātitaranty eva mṛtyuṁ śruti-parāyaṇāḥ || 25 ||
units.translation: Others, again, who do not know thus, take to thinking after hearing from others; they, too, who are devoted to hearing, certainly overcome death.

units.sanskrit: यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् । क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥ २६ ॥
units.transliteration: yāvat sañjāyate kiñcit sattvaṁ sthāvara-jaṅgamam | kṣetra-kṣetrajña-saṁyogāt tad viddhi bharatarṣabha || 26 ||
units.translation: O scion of the Bharata dynasty, whatever object, moving or non-moving, comes into being, know that to be from the association of the field and the Knower of the field!

units.sanskrit: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् । विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं य: पश्यति स पश्यति ॥ २७ ॥
units.transliteration: samaṁ sarveṣu bhūteṣu tiṣṭhantaṁ parameśvaram | vinaśyatsv avinaśyantaṁ yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati || 27 ||
units.translation: He sees who sees the supreme Bhagavān as existing equally in all beings, and as the Imperishable among the perishable.

units.sanskrit: समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् । न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥ २८ ॥
units.transliteration: samaṁ paśyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśvaram | na hinasty ātmanātmānaṁ tato yāti parāṁ gatim || 28 ||
units.translation: Since by seeing equally Bhagavān who is present alike everywhere he does not injure the Self by the Self, therefore he attains the supreme Goal.

units.sanskrit: प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वश: । य: पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति ॥ २९ ॥
units.transliteration: prakṛtyaiva ca karmāṇi kriyamāṇāni sarvaśaḥ | yaḥ paśyati tathātmānam akartāraṁ sa paśyati || 29 ||
units.translation: And he who sees actions as being done in various ways by Nature itself, and also the Self as the non-agent—he sees.

units.sanskrit: यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति । तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ॥ ३० ॥
units.transliteration: yadā bhūta-pṛthag-bhāvam eka-stham anupaśyati | tata eva ca vistāraṁ brahma sampadyate tadā || 30 ||
units.translation: When one realizes that the state of diversity of living things is rooted in the One, and that their manifestation is also from That, then one becomes identified with Brahman.

units.sanskrit: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्यय: । शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥ ३१ ॥
units.transliteration: anāditvān nirguṇatvāt paramātmāyam avyayaḥ | śarīra-stho ’pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate || 31 ||
units.translation: Being without beginning and without qualities, O son of Kuntī, this immutable, supreme Self does not act; nor is It affected, although existing in the body.

units.sanskrit: यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते । सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते ॥ ३२ ॥
units.transliteration: yathā sarva-gataṁ saukṣmyād ākāśaṁ nopalipyate | sarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate || 32 ||
units.translation: As the all-pervading space is not defiled because of its subtlety, similarly the Self, present everywhere in the body, is not defiled.

units.sanskrit: यथा प्रकाशयत्येक: कृत्स्‍नं लोकमिमं रवि: । क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्‍नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥ ३३ ॥
units.transliteration: yathā prakāśayaty ekaḥ kṛtsnaṁ lokam imaṁ raviḥ | kṣetraṁ kṣetrī tathā kṛtsnaṁ prakāśayati bhārata || 33 ||
units.translation: As the single sun illumines this whole world, similarly, O descendant of the Bhārata dynasty, the Knower of the field illumines the whole field.

units.sanskrit: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा । भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ॥ ३४ ॥
units.transliteration: kṣetra-kṣetrajñayor evam antaraṁ jñāna-cakṣuṣā | bhūta-prakṛti-mokṣaṁ ca ye vidur yānti te param || 34 ||
units.translation: Those who know thus through the eye of wisdom the  distinction between the field and the Knower of the field, and the  annihilation of the Matrix of beings,—they reach the Supreme.
